PMC10999549.diagnosis: Gallbladder torsion (volvulus) causing ischemic/necrotic acalculous cholecystitis
rationale_summary: Previously healthy child with acute epigastric/RLQ pain, vomiting, positive Murphy sign, no jaundice and normal liver/biliary labs

Ultrasound: enlarged gallbladder with wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid/fat inflammation, no gallstones; repeat shows absent wall vascular flow and a cone-shaped structure at the neck with gallbladder displacement/mobility on repositioning

CT: fundus deviated left of gallbladder bed with poorly enhancing irregular wall (ischemia), pericholecystic fluid/fat stranding, and focal hepatic perfusion changes, supporting torsion rather than calculous disease

PMC11535749.diagnosis: SBP-101-associated toxic maculopathy (drug-induced retinal pigment epithelium/outer retinal atrophy)
rationale_summary: Normal baseline eye exam/OCT before SBP-101, then vision loss began after the second monthly cycle
Bilateral progressive central vision loss with photopsias and central scotomas
Macular pigmentary changes with punctate hypo/hyperautofluorescence and later central hypoautofluorescence with surrounding ring
OCT shows ellipsoid zone disruption, sub-RPE hyperreflective deposits, and progression to incomplete RPE and outer retinal atrophy with choroidal thinning
ERG largely preserves rod function with relatively greater cone/central dysfunction (reduced 30 Hz flicker)
Paraneoplastic retinopathy workup negative (anti-recoverin/anti-enolase normal) and no diabetes/hypertension to explain findings

PMC11821356.diagnosis: Adult-onset PFAPA syndrome (Periodic Fever, Aphthous stomatitis, Pharyngitis, Adenitis)
rationale_summary: Recurrent stereotyped febrile episodes every 6-8 weeks for 5 years with complete wellness between attacks and predictable onset
Each episode includes sore throat/pharyngitis with white exudate and tender cervical/submandibular lymphadenopathy
Inflammatory markers elevated during attacks (CRP, serum amyloid A) with negative infectious workup and no response to antibiotics
Lymph node biopsy shows reactive hyperplasia and bone marrow/cytogenetics are unremarkable, arguing against malignancy

PMC10622404.diagnosis: Severe rhabdomyolysis causing hyperkalemia, metabolic acidosis, and acute kidney injury (pigment nephropathy)
rationale_summary: Several days of diffuse myalgia with inability to walk
Marked muscle injury markers: creatine phosphokinase >22,000 U/L and myoglobin >30,000 μg/L with very high AST/LDH
Life-threatening hyperkalemia (K 7.5 mmol/L) with peaked T waves and bradyarrhythmia
Metabolic acidosis (pH 7.26)
Severe acute kidney injury on CKD: creatinine 6.58 mg/dL and BUN 304 mg/dL

PMC5676292.diagnosis: Cerebral salt wasting syndrome (CSW) secondary to Mycoplasma pneumoniae-associated encephalitis
rationale_summary: Acute CNS illness with encephalopathy and seizures (drowsiness, hallucinations, EEG abnormalities) consistent with encephalitis; Mycoplasma pneumoniae IgM positive and treated with azithromycin
Developed severe hyponatremia (Na 112) with low serum osmolality (227)
Inappropriately high urine sodium (129) and high urine osmolality (437) with elevated FeNa
Marked polyuria with clinical hypovolemia/dehydration (tachycardia, decreased skin turgor, weight loss), favoring CSW over SIADH

Viral hepatitis (A, B, C) and CMV testing negative; ultrasound without biliary obstruction

PMC10216786.diagnosis: Hyperventilation-induced severe hypophosphatemia causing acute tetraparesis (respiratory alkalosis-related intracellular phosphate shift)
rationale_summary: Both episodes occurred with acute hyperventilation and hypocapnia/respiratory alkalosis (pH 7.57, PaCO2 19.2).
Marked hypophosphatemia was present (0.2 mmol/L; also severe in first episode) with no evidence of renal phosphate wasting.
Neuromuscular workup was unrevealing (normal MRI, normal nerve conduction/myography, negative myasthenia testing).
Periodic paralysis was investigated and ruled out (normal exercise tests; negative SCN4A, KCNJ18, KCNJ2, CACNA1S).
Clinical signs of alkalosis-related neuromuscular irritability (tetany, positive Trousseau) accompanied the weakness; rhabdomyolysis occurred in the second episode.

5-year progressive, length-dependent distal lower-limb weakness with bilateral foot drop and no bulbar/upper-limb involvement
Exam shows distal motor weakness with absent knee/ankle reflexes and minimal sensory findings (pin/light-touch reduced; vibration/proprioception intact)
NCS: mildly reduced peroneal/tibial CMAP amplitudes with normal velocities/latencies and normal sensory studies, supporting a predominantly motor axonal neuropathy

EMG: distal denervation and chronic reinnervation (fibrillations/PSWs, long-duration polyphasic MUAPs, reduced recruitment) with normal proximal/upper-limb muscles, consistent with length-dependent motor neuropathy

Secondary causes and mimics (lumbar MRI, metabolic labs, SPEP, paraneoplastic antibodies, CMT panel, DM1/DM2 testing) were unrevealing; myotonic discharges can be seen in motor neuron/axonal disorders

Large solid enhancing renal-region mass causing hydronephrosis and mass effect on collecting system
Gross whorled, firm gray-white lobulated tumor with necrosis/myxoid change, typical of smooth muscle neoplasm
Microscopy: interlacing fascicles/whorls of spindle cells with cigar-shaped nuclei, marked mitotic activity (20-25/10 HPF) and tumor necrosis consistent with leiomyosarcoma

Immunoprofile supports smooth muscle differentiation (diffuse SMA, desmin, vimentin) with absence of epithelial markers (pancytokeratin/HMWCK negative) arguing against sarcomatoid RCC or epithelial tumor

Acute bilateral horizontal gaze palsy localizes to pontine PPRF lesions seen on MRI
One spinal lesion enhances (active) while others do not (older), supporting lesions of different ages
Anti-AQP4 and anti-MOG antibodies negative and VEP normal, making NMOSD/MOGAD less likely in this presentation

PMC3884960.diagnosis: Clear cell adenocarcinoma of the renal pelvis (Müllerian-type)
rationale_summary: Renal pelvis tumor with papillary architecture lined by clear and hobnail cells, hyaline basement membrane-like cores, and focal psammoma bodies
Entire pelvicalyceal lining replaced by these cells with invasion into renal parenchyma and peripelvic fat; ureter uninvolved
Immunoprofile supports Müllerian/renal epithelial differentiation: CK7+, HNF-1β+, PAX8+, focal CA-125+

PMC8957291.diagnosis: Acute disseminated encephalomyelitis (ADEM) involving the brainstem, post-inactivated SARS‑CoV‑2 vaccination
rationale_summary: Subacute onset of multifocal brainstem symptoms (quadriparesis, dysphagia/drooling, bilateral facial paralysis) after recent vaccination with no evidence of infection and negative SARS‑CoV‑2 PCR
MRI shows multifocal poorly marginated T2/FLAIR hyperintense lesions in cerebral peduncle, pons, and medulla with faint enhancement, consistent with inflammatory demyelination

PMC9176094.diagnosis: Pseudohypoaldosteronism type 1 (primary, likely autosomal recessive systemic ENaC defect)
rationale_summary: Neonatal recurrent salt-wasting crises with severe hyponatremia, hyperkalemia, and metabolic acidosis causing dehydration and shock
Normal female genitalia and repeat hormonal studies inconsistent with congenital adrenal hyperplasia
Poor/inadequate response to hydrocortisone and need for ongoing mineralocorticoid therapy indicates aldosterone resistance rather than deficiency

Consanguineous parents support an autosomal recessive form

PMC10225816.diagnosis: Neurolymphomatosis (lymphomatous infiltration of the right brachial plexus/cervical roots and peripheral nerves)
rationale_summary: Progressive, painful, multifocal neuropathy with severe right upper-limb weakness/atrophy and recurrent cranial neuropathy (facial palsy)
NCS shows axonal degeneration in postganglionic segments (absent median/ulnar CMAP/SNAP; reduced axillary) with normal lower limbs, localizing to brachial plexus/peripheral nerves
Ultrasound/MRI show massive focal nerve, root, and brachial plexus enlargement with loss of fascicular pattern and increased vascularity
FDG-PET/CT shows abnormal uptake in the same nerves/plexus/roots, supporting lymphomatous nerve infiltration
Elevated soluble IL-2 receptor and lack of response to steroids/IVIG support a malignant/infiltrative process over immune neuropathy

PMC10843258.diagnosis: Nonclassic congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency
rationale_summary: Young woman with new oligomenorrhea/spotting and virilization signs (deeper voice, mild clitoromegaly, acne)
Markedly elevated total testosterone above female reference range

PMC11447760.diagnosis: Non-islet cell tumor hypoglycemia (IGF-2-mediated hypoglycemia) from metastatic myoepithelial carcinoma
rationale_summary: Recurrent fasting hypoglycemia with Whipple's triad and documented glucose ~30 mg/dL
During hypoglycemia: suppressed insulin and C-peptide, arguing against endogenous/exogenous insulin or sulfonylurea effect
Low β-hydroxybutyrate indicates suppressed ketogenesis consistent with insulin-like growth factor effect
Known metastatic malignancy plus very low IGF-1 and elevated IGF-2:IGF-1 molar ratio (10) supports IGF-2-mediated non-islet cell tumor hypoglycemia
Cortisol level during episode not low, making adrenal insufficiency unlikely

PMC4689918.diagnosis: Embryonal rhabdomyosarcoma with chemotherapy-induced lipoblast-like (adipocytic) cytoplasmic vacuolation
rationale_summary: Initial biopsy showed rhabdomyosarcoma phenotype (spindle/ovoid rhabdomyoblasts; desmin and myogenin positive; FOXO1 rearrangement negative supporting embryonal type).
Post-chemotherapy excision retained diffuse rhabdomyoblastic round-cell tumor with desmin positivity.

Subset of tumor cells developed prominent cytoplasmic lipid vacuoles (Oil Red O+, adipophilin+) with S-100 positivity, mimicking adipocytes/lipoblasts, while PAS stains were negative, supporting lipid rather than glycogen.
Vacuolated cells lacked nuclear myogenin despite desmin positivity, consistent with altered differentiation after treatment rather than a primary lipogenic sarcoma.

PMC4446922.diagnosis: IgA-dominant membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis (IgA-dominant infection-related GN pattern)
rationale_summary: Nephrotic-range proteinuria (4.2 g/day) with hematuria and acute kidney injury (creatinine 7.0 mg/dL)
Biopsy shows MPGN pattern: lobular accentuation, endocapillary proliferation with neutrophils, hyaline thrombi, and diffuse GBM splitting/duplication with mesangial interposition
Immunofluorescence: dominant 4+ IgA and C3 granular capillary-wall/mesangial staining with lesser IgG; C1q negative; polyclonal light chains
Electron microscopy: extensive subendothelial deposits with GBM duplication; no subepithelial deposits
Autoimmune/vasculitic serologies (ANA, ANCA, anti-GBM) and cryoglobulins negative, supporting an IgA-dominant MPGN/infection-related pattern rather than lupus/cryoglobulinemia

Lack of sustained response to steroids, IVIG, plasma exchange, and rituximab argues against autoimmune/drug-related leukoencephalopathy and supports occult CNS lymphoma (biopsy potentially nondiagnostic due to sampling/steroid effect)

PMC8123563.diagnosis: Metastatic medullary thyroid carcinoma with miliary pulmonary metastases
rationale_summary: Progressive dyspnea, cough, intermittent fever/chills, and marked weight loss with diffuse bilateral 1-3 mm miliary-pattern nodules on CXR and innumerable randomly distributed nodules on CT suggest hematogenous metastases rather than infection
Tuberculosis workup negative (AFB smears/cultures and MTB PCR) and no response to empiric anti-TB therapy

PMC3652784.diagnosis: Duchenne muscular dystrophy (dystrophinopathy) presenting with pseudohypertrophy and markedly elevated CK
rationale_summary: 10-year-old boy with progressive lower-limb symptoms and difficulty standing, with bilateral calf and thigh hypertrophy and tight hamstrings/gastrocnemius
Marked muscle enzyme elevation (CK 13,126 U/L, LDH 2245 U/L) with elevated ALT, suggesting muscle breakdown rather than inflammatory arthritis
Persistently normal ESR/CRP, almost acellular synovial fluid, and MRI knee showing only small effusion argue against inflammatory synovitis
Muscle MRI shows selective muscle edema and reduced posterior-compartment bulk consistent with a myopathic process

PMC7284055.diagnosis: Nodular pulmonary amyloidosis (localized AL amyloidosis associated with IgM kappa monoclonal gammopathy)
rationale_summary: CT showed multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules, some calcified, with no extrapulmonary lesions on PET-CT
Lung biopsy demonstrated amorphous eosinophilic material with surrounding plasma cells and no neoplastic cells, consistent with amyloid deposition

Immunofixation revealed an isolated monoclonal kappa IgM with otherwise normal SPEP/UPEP and minimal marrow involvement, supporting localized AL-type amyloidosis rather than disseminated malignancy or infection
Extensive infectious and autoimmune workup was negative (negative BAL cultures/AFB; negative ANA/ANCA; normal ACE)

PMC5412606.diagnosis: Typical slow-fast AV nodal reentrant tachycardia (AVNRT) unmasked after ablation of left posterior fascicular ILVT
rationale_summary: After RF, tachycardia became narrow-complex (QRS 85 ms) with 1:1 VA conduction and a relatively short VA interval with concentric atrial activation-features most consistent with AVNRT rather than atrial tachycardia or AVRT.
Earliest atrial activation near the His region during tachycardia supports a septal/AV nodal reentry mechanism.

The initial wide-complex tachycardia showed a negative HV interval and was verapamil-sensitive, consistent with fascicular VT; its ablation likely terminated ILVT and allowed induction/manifestation of AVNRT.

rationale_summary: Day-6 presentation with acute heart failure/respiratory distress, metabolic acidosis and very high lactate (14.8 mmol/L)
Marked myocardial injury biomarkers (hs-troponin T 1001 ng/L, CK-MB 37.4 mcg/L)
ECG with inferior Q waves and lateral repolarization abnormalities consistent with ischemic injury
Echo with regional wall motion abnormalities (inferior hypokinesis, septal dyskinesis) and depressed systolic function

Coronary angiography showed normal origins/anatomy, and clinical/ECG/echo findings rapidly normalized with supportive care, consistent with a transient ischemic event rather than fixed coronary anomaly or myocarditis (viral studies negative)

PMC11387283.diagnosis: Parasitic (iatrogenic) leiomyoma of the pelvis after laparoscopic myomectomy

PMC6151222.diagnosis: Systemic sarcoidosis with osseous and lymph node involvement (necrotizing/nonnecrotizing granulomatous sarcoidosis)

Serum ACE within reference range but does not exclude sarcoidosis; alternative malignancy workup (SPEP/UPEP, β-hCG, testicular US) negative

TEE/CTA demonstrate an echo-dense mass/thrombus (≤1 cm) attached to the mitral prosthesis

PMC10586915.diagnosis: Bioprosthetic aortic valve thrombosis with systemic embolization (occipital ischemic stroke)
rationale_summary: Bioprosthetic aortic valve with new mobile 10×7.5 mm heterogeneous echogenic mass on TEE plus moderate stenosis, severe paravalvular regurgitation, and leaflet degeneration
Acute left occipital infarct causing right homonymous hemianopia consistent with embolic stroke from a valvular source

PMC3253064.diagnosis: Subclinical hyperthyroidism due to an autonomously functioning thyroid nodule (toxic adenoma)
rationale_summary: Postoperative labs show suppressed TSH (0.001 mIU/mL) with normal free T4 and free T3, persisting for 2 months (subclinical hyperthyroidism).
Thyroid scintigraphy demonstrates focal uptake in the left lobe with suppression of the remaining thyroid tissue, a pattern consistent with an autonomous hyperfunctioning nodule.
Thyroid ultrasound shows a normal-sized gland without diffuse hyperfunction, supporting a focal rather than diffuse cause.

PMC10602372.diagnosis: Iatrogenic aortic valve cusp injury (non-coronary cusp laceration/perforation) causing acute severe aortic regurgitation after PCI
rationale_summary: Acute dyspnoea and new early diastolic aortic murmur developed shortly after PCI, with previously normal TTE pre-procedure
Repeat TTE/TEE showed new moderate-to-severe/severe aortic regurgitation with a mobile filamentous structure on the non-coronary cusp causing a coaptation defect, without aortic root dilation
CT excluded aortic dissection and there was no clinical/laboratory evidence of infective endocarditis, supporting a procedural mechanical valve injury as the cause

PMC4637487.diagnosis: AL (light-chain) amyloidosis causing bilateral phrenic neuropathy with diaphragmatic weakness leading to restrictive ventilatory failure
rationale_summary: IgG-kappa myeloma with markedly elevated kappa free light chains (K/L ratio 2121) and Bence Jones proteinuria, predisposing to AL amyloidosis
Progressive dyspnea with hypercapnic respiratory failure and restrictive PFTs suggests neuromuscular/diaphragmatic weakness rather than primary lung disease
Nerve conduction study shows bilateral phrenic axonal neuropathy, consistent with amyloid-related peripheral neuropathy
Pulmonary hypertension workup negative for thromboembolism and right heart cath after diuresis shows normal wedge pressure (10 mmHg), arguing against left-sided congestion as primary cause

PMC5054479.diagnosis: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type IV (congenital insensitivity to pain with anhidrosis; NTRK1-related)
rationale_summary: Onset in infancy with recurrent fevers and absence of sweating (anhidrosis)
Severe self-mutilation beginning with tooth eruption (biting fingers/toes, tongue injuries) consistent with congenital insensitivity to pain
Repeated unrecognized trauma causing multiple fractures, recurrent joint dislocations, deformities, chronic ulcers/osteomyelitis, and eventual amputations
Consanguineous parents supports autosomal recessive disorder
Later complications (nephrotic-range proteinuria with hypoalbuminemia/ascites; inflammatory bowel disease-like colitis) fit multisystem involvement reported with HSAN IV

PMC11079415.diagnosis: Thrombosed (post-embolisation) vein of Galen aneurysmal sac
rationale_summary: Known mural-type vein of Galen aneurysmal malformation treated with transarterial Onyx embolisation and angiographic occlusion of the venous sac

PMC11481506.diagnosis: Acute heart failure due to myocarditis (myopericarditis) presenting with systemic serous effusions and ascites
rationale_summary: CT showed fluid collections in the chest, pericardial sac, and abdomen (systemic effusions)

Intraoperative findings lacked an acute surgical abdomen (no perforation, no gallbladder enlargement/cholangitis) but showed ascites and bowel wall edema consistent with congestion/third spacing
Inflammatory markers were normal (normal procalcitonin and CRP) and patient was afebrile, arguing against severe infection as the primary cause

PMC3961933.diagnosis: Cemento-ossifying fibroma (giant ossifying fibroma) of the mandible
rationale_summary: 34-year-old woman with a slow-growing, painless, hard, lobulated mandibular swelling over 6 years causing facial asymmetry and chewing difficulty

Imaging shows a well-defined, expansile mixed radiolucent-radiopaque mandibular lesion with concentric/ball-like growth and "cotton-wool" calcifications, with bowing but no erosion of the inferior border

Marked buccal and lingual cortical expansion with tooth displacement and intact mucosa; labs (including alkaline phosphatase, calcium, phosphorus) normal, supporting a benign fibro-osseous neoplasm rather than metabolic bone disease

PMC4722558.diagnosis: Bilateral accessory ossicle of the medial malleolus (os subtibiale) mimicking an acute medial malleolar fracture
rationale_summary: Initial ankle radiograph was read as a displaced medial malleolus fracture, but clinical course was rapidly improving with marked pain/swelling reduction and preserved motion within 24 hours
Comparison radiograph of the uninjured left ankle showed a similarly shaped medial malleolar fragment, suggesting a bilateral anatomic variant rather than an acute fracture
Lack of ankle laxity/instability and incongruent clinical findings prompted CT to clarify suspected accessory ossicle vs fracture

PMC11825525.diagnosis: Disseminated tuberculosis with paradoxical sarcoid-like granulomatous reaction (anti-TNF-associated)
rationale_summary: On adalimumab (anti-TNF) with systemic symptoms (weight loss, night sweats), splenomegaly, and PET-CT showing hypermetabolic splenic/hepatic lesions, pulmonary nodules, and lymphadenopathy
Biopsies from spleen and liver showed non-necrotizing granulomas; initial mycobacterial PCR/cultures negative, which can occur in paucibacillary/disseminated TB
Repeat T-SPOT.TB became positive after prior negative screening before anti-TNF therapy

Clinical course with steroid-responsive granulomatous disease but later deterioration with fever, dyspnoea, confusion, and new bilateral pulmonary infiltrates while immunosuppressed is consistent with disseminated/reactivated TB under anti-TNF and corticosteroids

PMC8895532.diagnosis: Right-sided congenital diaphragmatic hernia with intrathoracic stomach due to short esophagus (associated suspected esophageal atresia/tracheoesophageal fistula)

PMC3892129.diagnosis: Pituitary macroprolactinoma with secondary pituitary abscess (infected invasive prolactinoma causing purulent meningitis)
rationale_summary: Very high prolactin (9000 ng/mL) with panhypopituitarism and 2-year decreased libido/visual loss supports a large prolactin-secreting pituitary macroadenoma
Imaging shows a large invasive sellar/suprasellar mass eroding the sellar floor and extending into the sphenoid sinus/cavernous sinuses with optic chiasm compression

Acute febrile severe headache/vomiting with markedly elevated CRP/leukocytosis and purulent CSF (11,900 WBC/µL, high protein, low glucose, high lactate) indicates bacterial meningitis from an intracranial infectious source

Sellar floor erosion with sphenoid sinus extension provides a route for infection, consistent with an abscess complicating an invasive pituitary tumor rather than apoplexy (no intratumoral hemorrhage on MRI)

PMC6158818.diagnosis: Ribbing disease (multiple diaphyseal sclerosis) of the right femur
rationale_summary: Young adult with long-standing localized diaphyseal thigh pain and imaging showing marked cortical thickening, medullary canal narrowing/partial obliteration, and diffuse increased uptake along the femoral shaft
Normal alkaline phosphatase and other bone markers argue against high-turnover metabolic bone disease
Biopsy shows sclerotic thickened lamellar trabeculae with accentuated cement lines and no inflammation, fibrosis, malignancy, or neoplasia
Negative GNAS1 mutation testing helps exclude fibrous dysplasia/McCune-Albright; negative SQSTM1 helps exclude Paget disease

PMC11089029.diagnosis: Hepatic hemangioma arising in a simple liver cyst (cystic hepatic hemangioma with enhancing mural nodule)
rationale_summary: Previously simple hepatic cyst enlarged over 1 year and developed an irregular mural nodule
Mural nodule shows progressive/gradual contrast enhancement with a distinct feeding artery and portal-venous enhancement comparable to intrahepatic vessels, favoring vascular lesion
MRI shows peripheral low and central high signal on heavily T2-weighted images and diffusion hyperintensity, compatible with hemangioma-like vascular spaces/thrombosis
No biliary duct dilation and no FDG uptake on PET/CT, arguing against aggressive biliary cystic neoplasm

PMC8367851.diagnosis: Disseminated sterile neutrophilic disease (pyoderma gangrenosum with extracutaneous neutrophilic involvement of lungs/spleen/pericardium)
rationale_summary: Long history of biopsy-proven pyoderma gangrenosum with dense neutrophilic infiltrate and recurrent flares
New cavitary pulmonary lesions, splenic "abscesses," and pericardial effusion with marked neutrophilic leukocytosis but afebrile
Extensive lung evaluations (BAL, transbronchial biopsy, wedge resections) showed abscess formation without organisms on stains, cultures, or molecular studies
Clinical deterioration after stopping immunosuppression and lack of response to broad-spectrum antibacterial/antifungal therapy support a noninfectious neutrophilic process
ANCA negative, arguing against ANCA-associated vasculitis as the cause of cavitary lung disease

PMC6544423.diagnosis: Bilateral subclavian artery occlusion causing coronary-subclavian steal syndrome (compromising LIMA/RIMA graft flow) leading to cardiogenic shock and pulmonary edema
rationale_summary: Prior CABG used both internal mammary arteries (LIMA and RIMA), making myocardial perfusion dependent on subclavian inflow
Known bilateral subclavian artery stenosis (50%) two years earlier with progressive systemic hypotension and ischemic ECG changes over months
Acute presentation with pulmonary edema, positive troponin/CPK rise, and diffuse ST depression consistent with myocardial ischemia causing pump failure
Markedly low/undetectable arm blood pressures with easily palpable femoral pulses suggests proximal upper-extremity arterial obstruction rather than global low-flow state alone
Bilateral arm involvement fits bilateral subclavian disease, which can reduce IMA graft perfusion and precipitate ischemia/shock

PMC7937576.diagnosis: Aggressive vertebral hemangioma of T10 with epidural/paraspinal extension causing spinal cord compression
rationale_summary: 13-year-old with progressive myelopathic symptoms and cord T2 hyperintensity from severe canal narrowing at T10
MRI: avidly enhancing T10-centered lesion with serpentine T2 hypointense flow-voids and facilitated diffusion, consistent with a hypervascular lesion
CT: thickened, striated trabeculae (hemangioma pattern) with associated osteolysis and prominent segmental arteries/paravertebral veins supporting an aggressive hemangioma

No additional lesions on staging CT, making multifocal malignancy less likely; key alternatives to consider for a hypervascular vertebral mass with epidural extension include vertebral arteriovenous malformation/fistula, aneurysmal bone cyst, Langerhans cell histiocytosis, Ewing sarcoma/other primary bone tumor, and metastatic/neuroblastoma-related disease

PMC10551103.diagnosis: Areca nut (betel nut) poisoning causing acute intravascular hemolysis with hemoglobinuria
rationale_summary: Symptoms began immediately after ingestion of two tablespoons of areca nut powder (with ibuprofen), followed by pallor, cold extremities, seizures, and gross hematuria
Marked hemolysis markers: very high LDH (3570 U/L) with negative direct/indirect Coombs and sufficient G6PD activity, supporting non-immune hemolysis
Urinalysis with 4+ blood and only 2+ albumin is consistent with hemoglobinuria from intravascular hemolysis

Severe systemic illness with metabolic acidosis (HCO3 9.6 mEq/L) and markedly elevated transaminases/INR with normal bilirubin suggests toxic injury rather than viral hepatitis or sepsis (cultures negative)

PMC8577092.diagnosis: Cerebral salt wasting syndrome (CSW) after intracerebral hemorrhage causing hypovolemic shock
rationale_summary: Recent spontaneous intracerebral hemorrhage followed by several days of marked polyuria and 6 kg weight loss
Clinical hypovolemia with shock: hypotension, tachycardia, mottling, dry mucosa, collapsible IVC; improves with large-volume saline
Hyponatremia with low plasma osmolality plus inappropriately high urine sodium (62 mmol/L) and urine chloride (64 mmol/L) indicating renal salt loss despite hypovolemia
Hemoconcentration and prerenal azotemia (elevated urea/creatinine) support volume depletion rather than SIADH
Associated hypochloremic metabolic alkalosis and hypokalemia consistent with severe extracellular volume depletion from renal salt wasting

PMC6241168.diagnosis: Ticagrelor-induced Cheyne-Stokes respiration with cyclic severe bradyarrhythmias (sinus bradycardia and transient complete atrioventricular block)
rationale_summary: Cheyne-Stokes respiration and cyclic sinus tachycardia/bradycardia with transient complete AV block began a few hours after loading with ticagrelor 180 mg (with aspirin) post-PCI
No overt heart failure and preserved LVEF (~50%) make new decompensated HF less likely as the cause of Cheyne-Stokes breathing
Metabolic/endocrine evaluation (electrolytes, renal/hepatic function, thyroid) was unrevealing, supporting a drug-related effect temporally linked to ticagrelor

PMC10189604.diagnosis: Fetus-in-fetu (parasitic twin) with two parasitic fetuses (one acardiac and one with weak cardiac activity) in the host fetus's right retroperitoneum
rationale_summary: Late-gestation ultrasound shows fetal-like structures within a right retroperitoneal mass, including skin-covered limb(s) with long bone, footpad and toe-like echoes, and buttock-like contour-features favoring organized fetal parts over teratoma
Two separate fetal-like signals/masses are described: one acardiac with no Doppler flow and one with faint heartbeat, Doppler flow, and movements

Vascular connection to the host is demonstrated: an umbilical cord-like structure and blood supply to the live mass arising from the host fetus's abdominal aorta, with mass effect on major vessels and organ displacement

PMC3423776.diagnosis: Foreign-body granuloma (textiloma/gossypiboma) due to retained/implanted absorbable hemostatic material mimicking meningioma recurrence
rationale_summary: New enhancing right falx lesion 7 months after Simpson grade I complete meningioma resection
Persistent elevated hs-CRP without fever, leukocytosis, procalcitonin rise, or CSF infection argues against abscess/meningitis
Intraoperative use of absorbable hemostatic agents and dural graft provides a nidus for foreign-body inflammatory reaction
Imaging shows irregular enhancing mass with edema and T2/FLAIR/DWI hyperintensity consistent with inflammatory granuloma that can mimic tumor recurrence

PMC6709319.diagnosis: Bucillamine-induced pneumonitis (organizing pneumonia pattern) superimposed on rheumatoid arthritis-associated usual interstitial pneumonia
rationale_summary: Progressive dyspnea developed 3 months after starting bucillamine for rheumatoid arthritis
HRCT shows new bilateral ground-glass opacities and consolidations sparing the lung bases on a background of pre-existing UIP (reticulation, honeycombing, traction bronchiectasis)
BAL demonstrates mixed inflammatory alveolitis with lymphocytosis and neutrophilia; infectious cultures negative
Transbronchial biopsy shows organizing pneumonia with septal thickening and inflammatory infiltration, consistent with drug-induced lung injury/OP

PMC3564269.diagnosis: Systemic AL (light-chain) amyloidosis with amyloid myopathy and neuropathy
rationale_summary: Subacute progressive proximal weakness with muscle atrophy and biopsy-proven inflammatory myopathy without vasculitis
Diffuse predominantly axonal length-dependent sensorimotor polyneuropathy with areflexia and distal sensory symptoms; CSF albuminocytologic dissociation
New nephrotic-range proteinuria (3+) with anemia, weight loss, and fatigue suggesting systemic infiltrative/plasma-cell-related process

PMC8377818.diagnosis: Pineal parenchymal tumor of intermediate differentiation (PPTID)
rationale_summary: Pineal region mass causing obstructive hydrocephalus with central calcification

High proliferative index (Ki-67 60-70%) consistent with higher-grade pineal parenchymal tumor
Cytokeratin positivity with EMA/TTF-1/GFAP negativity and intact INI1 supports PPTID over metastatic carcinoma, ependymoma, or atypical teratoid/rhabdoid tumor
PET/CT shows FDG avidity confined to pineal region, arguing against an extracranial primary

PMC7546246.diagnosis: Left median nerve compression in the proximal forearm consistent with pronator syndrome (median neuropathy proximal to the anterior interosseous branch)
rationale_summary: Progressive median-distribution sensory loss including thenar triangle with marked thenar wasting and loss of thumb/index flexion (FPL, FDP/FDS index) and failed O-sign, indicating involvement proximal to AIN
Tinel sign 12 cm distal to medial epicondyle localizes irritation to proximal forearm rather than wrist
All carpal tunnel provocative tests negative and no nocturnal symptoms
Neurophysiology: absent median motor responses with preserved sensory responses at the wrist, supporting a proximal median mononeuropathy rather than carpal tunnel or plexopathy

PMC8032522.diagnosis: Pituitary macroadenoma (prolactinoma) causing secondary adrenal insufficiency and hypogonadotropic hypogonadism
rationale_summary: Marked hyperprolactinemia (prolactin 90.8 ng/mL after dilution) with low FSH/LH and secondary amenorrhea suggests pituitary pathology with hypogonadotropic hypogonadism

Severe hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 110 mmol/L; serum osmolality 223) with inappropriately concentrated urine (urine osmolality 266) and low urine sodium (14) is compatible with cortisol deficiency-related impaired free-water excretion
Low random cortisol (105 nmol/L) supports adrenal insufficiency as a contributor to recurrent vomiting, weakness, hypotension, and hyponatremia
Low TSH with low free T3 and low-normal free T4 suggests central hypothyroidism, supporting hypopituitarism from a pituitary mass

PMC11573733.diagnosis: Cardiac amyloidosis (restrictive cardiomyopathy, most consistent with AL amyloidosis)
rationale_summary: Progressive right- and left-sided heart failure symptoms with preserved LVEF and restrictive filling pattern on Doppler (E/A 11, low e′, high E/e′)

Echo shows classic infiltrative features: marked biventricular wall thickening, biatrial enlargement, interatrial septal thickening, thickened valves, small pericardial effusion, and granular "sparkling" myocardium
ECG demonstrates low-voltage limb leads with pseudoinfarction pattern despite marked LVH on echo, supporting amyloid infiltration
Elevated troponin without regional wall motion abnormalities is compatible with myocardial infiltration/injury seen in amyloidosis

PMC7850611.diagnosis: Isolated cardiac sarcoidosis presenting as an inferobasal septal inflammatory mass causing high-degree atrioventricular block
rationale_summary: New conduction disease with intermittent 2:1 and exercise-induced high-degree AV block in a 49-year-old
Imaging shows a focal inferobasal septal mass with late gadolinium enhancement on cardiac MRI and intense focal FDG uptake on PET, consistent with active inflammatory infiltrate
No coronary disease and no pulmonary pathology on CT to explain wall-motion abnormality/mass
Extensive infectious and systemic inflammatory workup negative and no extracardiac FDG uptake, supporting isolated cardiac involvement
Endomyocardial biopsies non-diagnostic, which can occur due to patchy myocardial involvement

PMC7924145.diagnosis: Right adrenal hemorrhage (adrenal hematoma) in pregnancy
rationale_summary: Acute right flank/back/chest pain with systemic inflammatory response and retroperitoneal inflammatory tracking on MRI around a 7.9 cm adrenal mass
MRI shows mildly heterogeneous T2-hyperintense adrenal lesion not typical for adenoma, compatible with hemorrhage/hematoma
Initial mild elevations of plasma/urine metanephrines during acute illness normalized on repeat outpatient testing, arguing against pheochromocytoma
No biochemical evidence of cortisol, aldosterone, or androgen excess to support a functional adrenal tumor

PMC9260791.diagnosis: Autoimmune inner ear disease (immune-mediated sudden sensorineural hearing loss) temporally associated with BNT162b2 mRNA COVID-19 vaccination
rationale_summary: Two episodes of sudden, progressive unilateral (left) sensorineural hearing loss beginning 1 day after second BNT162b2 dose and recurring within a month
Audiograms showed progressive low-frequency SNHL with slight decline in word recognition; tympanograms type A and masked bone conduction support sensorineural (not conductive) loss
Vestibular testing showed marked unilateral vestibular hypofunction (93% left caloric weakness) consistent with inner ear pathology
Routine labs (CBC, RF, CMP, lipids) were normal, supporting an immune-mediated inner ear process rather than systemic disease; anti-SARS-CoV-2 antibodies positive indicating vaccine response

PMC11380166.diagnosis: Postoperative common peroneal nerve palsy (ischemic/position-related neuropathy) after aortobifemoral bypass
rationale_summary: Acute postoperative foot drop with 0/5 ankle dorsiflexion and eversion plus numbness over dorsum of foot localizes to common peroneal nerve
Distal limb perfusion preserved (warm foot, biphasic distal pulses), arguing against acute arterial occlusion
Spine MRI normal, making radiculopathy/spinal cord lesion unlikely
Partial recovery over 48 hours supports a transient peripheral nerve injury; underlying diabetic axonal polyneuropathy on NCS is a risk factor

PMC6101595.diagnosis: Primary enteric (colonic-type) adenocarcinoma of the urinary bladder with prostatic invasion

Histology shows adenocarcinoma resembling colorectal carcinoma
Immunophenotype CK20+/CDX2+ with CK7− and PSA− supports enteric/colonic-type and excludes prostatic origin

PMC9789769.diagnosis: Pulmonary eosinophilic granulomatosis (eosinophilic bronchopneumopathy with mass-like pulmonary infiltrates)
rationale_summary: 4-week progressive cough with marked eosinophilia and basophilia at presentation

Thoracic imaging shows a large lobulated left cranial lung lobe mass with heterogeneous enhancement/fluid pockets plus multifocal irregular nodular airway wall thickening nearly occluding bronchi in other lobes
Mediastinal lymphadenopathy with no abdominal mass lesions

Heartworm antigen and fecal parasite tests negative; eosinophilia resolved after empiric therapy while the radiographic mass persisted, supporting inflammatory eosinophilic pulmonary disease that can mimic neoplasia

PMC11671661.diagnosis: Neuropsychiatric systemic lupus erythematosus presenting as lupus cerebritis/aseptic meningitis (inflammatory temporal lobe lesion)
rationale_summary: Known pediatric SLE with current flare markers: very high ANA (1:2560), anti-dsDNA >300 IU/mL, and markedly low complements (C3 0.27 g/L, C4 <0.1 g/L)

Meningitic presentation with raised CSF opening pressure (29 cm H2O) and neutrophilic pleocytosis/protein elevation, but gram stain/cultures and extensive infectious PCR/antigen/mycobacterial/fungal studies all negative and no response to empiric antimicrobials

Neuroimaging shows focal inflammatory brain involvement: left temporal T2 hyperintensity with mild cortical swelling and petechial hemorrhages; MRA/MRV normal (argues against large-vessel stroke/venous thrombosis) and MR spectroscopy without lactate peak (argues against abscess)
Antiphospholipid tests negative, reducing likelihood of thrombotic APS-related infarct as primary cause

PMC8536869.diagnosis: Twiddler syndrome (pacemaker lead dislodgement with diaphragmatic/phrenic nerve stimulation)
rationale_summary: Dual-lead pacemaker implanted 3 months prior, followed by sudden symptoms after arm/blanket pulling while supine
Chest radiograph showed both leads displaced (atrial lead to SVC, ventricular lead in right atrium)
ECG showed ventricular lead undersensing with pacing spikes after QRS and no ventricular capture, consistent with lead dislodgement
Rhythmic abdominal wall contractions at 60 bpm resolved when pacemaker was deactivated, indicating diaphragmatic/phrenic stimulation from malpositioned leads

PMC6745400.diagnosis: Cervical spinal epidural hemorrhage secondary to immune-mediated thrombocytopenia (ITP)
rationale_summary: Acute onset nonambulatory tetraplegia with intact spinal reflexes localizes to C1-C6
Marked petechiae/ecchymoses and smear-confirmed severe thrombocytopenia (<10,000/µL; reported 0 ×10^9/L) indicate a major bleeding diathesis

Cervical MRI shows a crescentic dorsolateral extradural lesion at C3 with signal characteristics and mild peripheral enhancement most consistent with intraspinal hemorrhage, plus intramedullary hyperintensity compatible with cord injury from compression/hemorrhage
Tick-borne screening negative and no other systemic cause identified in the case data, supporting primary immune-mediated thrombocytopenia as the likely etiology of hemorrhage

PMC10199808.diagnosis: Lymphogranuloma venereum (LGV) proctocolitis due to Chlamydia trachomatis (L1-L3) mimicking Crohn's disease
rationale_summary: MSM with rectal tenesmus, hematochezia, mucous discharge and ulcerative proctitis
Rectal biopsy PCR positive for Chlamydia trachomatis; other STI/viral causes (GC, HSV, CMV) negative
Only partial/transient response to short doxycycline courses; persistent disease despite IBD therapies (steroids, adalimumab, ustekinumab) suggests misdiagnosed infectious proctocolitis
Discontinuous ulcerations including rectum and ileocecal/terminal ileum can occur with LGV and mimic Crohn's

PMC6519488.diagnosis: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT) with thrombosis due to dalteparin
rationale_summary: On therapeutic dalteparin for recent PE/DVT
Developed rapid, marked platelet fall from 468×10^9/L to 29×10^9/L over ~3 weeks
New and progressive thromboses (worsening PE plus acute aortic mural thrombi) despite anticoagulation
Peripheral smear with fibrin stranding consistent with consumptive thrombotic process rather than lab artifact

PMC9327270.diagnosis: Infantile GM3 synthase deficiency (ST3GAL5-related salt-and-pepper developmental regression syndrome)
rationale_summary: Onset at 3 months with frequent focal seizures (right clonic jerks, chewing, blinking) and developmental regression (loss of head control, decreased reactivity/feeding)
Consanguineous parents suggests autosomal recessive neurodevelopmental disorder
MRI shows hypomyelinating leukoencephalopathy (diffuse T2 white-matter hyperintensity, absent PLIC myelination, thin corpus callosum)
Extensive metabolic screening and chromosomal microarray were normal, supporting a genetic hypomyelinating epilepsy syndrome such as ST3GAL5-related GM3 synthase deficiency

PMC5484965.diagnosis: Erythema gyratum repens-like mycosis fungoides (cutaneous T-cell lymphoma)

Multiple osteolytic bone lesions on humerus and CT (spine/pelvis/femur) with back pain

PMC11249453.diagnosis: Primary jejunal diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (small-bowel lymphoma)
rationale_summary: 23-year-old with 5 months of epigastric pain, nausea/vomiting, weight loss, and severe iron-deficiency anemia with hemoccult-positive stool suggesting GI blood loss
CT shows markedly thickened heterogeneous jejunal loop with mesenteric adenopathy and ascites, consistent with an infiltrative small-bowel malignancy with nodal involvement
Enteroscopy shows a large ulcerated malignant-appearing proximal jejunal mass; biopsy reported a high-grade malignant neoplasm
PET-CT shows a hypermetabolic multilobulated mass involving multiple jejunal loops, supporting lymphoma over a focal epithelial tumor
Tumor markers (CEA, CA19-9, AFP) normal and HIV/EBV PCR nonreactive, fitting primary intestinal lymphoma presentation

PMC11154768.diagnosis: Primary mediastinal nonseminomatous germ cell tumor (yolk sac tumor) with tumor thrombus into SVC/right atrium and pulmonary metastases
rationale_summary: Large lobulated anterior mediastinal mass on CT with multiple pulmonary nodules suggests malignant mediastinal tumor with lung metastases
Markedly elevated alpha-fetoprotein (5000 ng/mL) strongly supports a nonseminomatous germ cell tumor, particularly yolk sac tumor
Contiguous thrombosis/tumor extension from brachiocephalic veins/SVC into the right atrium with an attached right atrial mass fits tumor thrombus from mediastinal malignancy
Endocarditis is less likely given afebrile state, negative blood cultures, and no IV drug use

PMC3544661.diagnosis: Phenytoin-induced DRESS syndrome (drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms) with hepatitis
rationale_summary: Started phenytoin 35 days before onset, consistent with delayed hypersensitivity timing
Fever (40.2 °C), diffuse pruritic maculopapular rash, and marked periorbital/facial edema
Severe transaminitis (AST 778, ALT 1274) indicating systemic organ involvement (hepatitis)
Infectious and autoimmune hepatitis workup negative (HAV/HBV/HCV, EBV/CMV/HIV, ANA) and acetaminophen/salicylate undetectable
Eosinophils not markedly elevated but DRESS can occur without prominent eosinophilia early

PMC5237164.diagnosis: Paradoxical immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome (IRIS) due to toxoplasmosis with cerebral relapse and toxoplasma myelitis
rationale_summary: Advanced HIV with cerebral toxoplasmosis improved on anti-toxoplasma therapy, then developed new/worsening ring-enhancing brain lesions and longitudinal thoracic cord T2 lesions after HAART initiation

Extensive CSF infectious workup negative and PET/CSF phenotype not supportive of CNS lymphoma despite EBV PCR positivity

PMC11985889.diagnosis: Secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (macrophage activation syndrome) triggered by systemic lupus erythematosus

Bone-marrow aspiration showing numerous macrophages with cell lysis consistent with hemophagocytosis

PMC9022340.diagnosis: Metastatic neuroendocrine tumor (carcinoid syndrome) causing thyrotoxicosis and right-sided heart disease
rationale_summary: New hot flashes with tremor and hyperreflexia plus elevated T4/free T4 and suppressed TSH, but negative TSI/TRAb and normal-sized thyroid
Diffuse increased uptake on technetium thyroid scan indicates thyroid hyperfunction not driven by Graves antibodies
FDG-PET shows multiple large FDG-avid liver masses with gastric antrum uptake and mesenteric lymphadenopathy, consistent with metastatic GI malignancy
Right atrial/ventricular dilation with tricuspid regurgitation and pulmonary hypertension fits carcinoid-related right-sided cardiac involvement in the setting of extensive liver metastases

PMC6421145.diagnosis: Mid-ventricular obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with left ventricular apical aneurysm and apical thrombus
rationale_summary: Transthoracic echo showed asymmetric LV hypertrophy with midventricular high-velocity flow (2.00 m/s systolic, 3.67 m/s diastolic) and paradoxical early-diastolic jet from apex to base, consistent with midventricular obstruction
Imaging (echo and enhanced CT) demonstrated an LV apical aneurysm containing a 21×14 mm intracavitary thrombus
Coronary CT showed no significant stenosis, supporting a non-ischemic cause of apical aneurysm
ECG abnormalities (ST elevation V5-V6, T-wave inversion in lateral leads) and frequent ventricular ectopy fit HCM with apical aneurysm

PMC3657244.diagnosis: Lucio phenomenon (necrotizing vasculitis reaction in diffuse lepromatous leprosy)
rationale_summary: Diffuse non-nodular lepromatous leprosy features: saddle-nose, diffuse infiltration, shiny infiltrated back with intact sensation, symmetric nerve thickening with glove-and-stocking anesthesia, multiple deformities
High bacillary load on slit-skin smear (globi; BI 4+) supports lepromatous spectrum associated with Lucio phenomenon
Acute painless hemorrhagic bullae with purpuric macules on extremities/ears rapidly rupturing into deep irregular angulated ulcers is characteristic of Lucio phenomenon
Alternative causes less supported: urine porphyrins negative; cryoglobulins/RF/ANA negative; no systemic symptoms and radiographs show no osteitis/osteomyelitis

PMC8633751.diagnosis: Primary cardiac leiomyosarcoma of the left atrium with metastatic spread to the spine
rationale_summary: Initial left atrial lesion was a fibrotic, densely adherent sheet-like mass extending into pulmonary vein orifices and toward mitral leaflets, not a typical myxoma on frozen section (no myxoma cells).

Later presentation with aggressive lytic vertebral fractures and paravertebral mass; biopsy showed malignant spindle cell tumor with smooth muscle differentiation (desmin+, SMA+), consistent with leiomyosarcoma.
No extracardiac primary malignancy found on CT chest/abdomen/pelvis, mammography, or Pap testing, supporting a cardiac primary with metastases.

PMC8138282.diagnosis: Cerebral fat embolism syndrome secondary to sickle cell vaso-occlusive bone marrow necrosis
rationale_summary: Known sickle cell β-thalassemia with recent MRI-confirmed bone infarcts/avascular necrosis and current severe diffuse limb pain consistent with vaso-occlusive crisis and marrow necrosis
Abrupt neurologic deterioration with hyperthermia and respiratory failure plus a new lung infiltrate suggests systemic embolic/inflammatory syndrome
Brain MRI shows extensive punctate diffusion-restricted lesions in deep gray nuclei and bilateral white matter, a pattern characteristic of cerebral fat embolism in sickle cell disease
Thrombocytopenia and coagulopathy without schistocytes and only mildly reduced ADAMTS13 (42%) argue against TTP and support a consumptive process seen with fat embolism syndrome

Severe premature calcific valvular and arterial disease (aortic stenosis with calcified aortic/mitral valves, aortic root and coronary/arch vessel ostial calcification) in an adolescent with normal inflammatory/autoimmune markers

PMC4754484.diagnosis: Inflammatory pseudotumour (idiopathic orbital inflammatory disease) involving the cavernous sinus/orbit
rationale_summary: Young man with progressive visual loss, headache, nausea, and unilateral deafness with MRI showing lesions adjacent to the cavernous sinus and retroocular orbit suggesting inflammatory vs neoplastic mass
Biopsy showed fragmented soft tissue with chronic inflammatory cells; pathology favored inflammatory pseudotumour with meningioma only as a differential (single whorled focus)
No systemic features of malignancy or infection (normal ESR, normal chest/sinus radiographs, no lymphadenopathy) supporting a localized inflammatory mass

PMC8545633.diagnosis: Immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy (anti-signal recognition particle [anti-SRP] myopathy) with associated cardiomyopathy
rationale_summary: Progressive proximal limb weakness with very high CK (4409 U/L) and muscle MRI STIR hyperintensity in biceps/triceps consistent with inflammatory/necrotizing myopathy
Severe dilated cardiomyopathy/heart failure (EF 20%, LV dilation) with persistently elevated troponin and BNP suggesting ongoing myocardial involvement
Cardiac MRI shows late gadolinium enhancement (endocardial and spotty transmural) indicating myocardial injury/fibrosis not explained by coronary disease
Coronary angiography shows only moderate LAD stenosis, arguing against ischemic cardiomyopathy as the primary cause and supporting myopathy-associated cardiomyopathy
PMC8545633.raw_output_text: {"diagnosis": "Immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy (anti-signal recognition particle [anti-SRP] myopathy) with associated cardiomyopathy", "rationale_summary": ["Progressive proximal limb weakness with very high CK (4409 U/L) and muscle MRI STIR hyperintensity in biceps/triceps consistent with inflammatory/necrotizing myopathy", "Severe dilated cardiomyopathy/heart failure (EF 20%, LV dilation) with persistently elevated troponin and BNP suggesting ongoing myocardial involvement", "Cardiac MRI shows late gadolinium enhancement (endocardial and spotty transmural) indicating myocardial injury/fibrosis not explained by coronary disease", "Coronary angiography shows only moderate LAD stenosis, arguing against ischemic cardiomyopathy as the primary cause and supporting myopathy-associated cardiomyopathy"]}

parsed_output.diagnosis: Immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy (anti-signal recognition particle [anti-SRP] myopathy) with associated cardiomyopathy
rationale_summary: Progressive proximal limb weakness with very high CK (4409 U/L) and muscle MRI STIR hyperintensity in biceps/triceps consistent with inflammatory/necrotizing myopathy
Severe dilated cardiomyopathy/heart failure (EF 20%, LV dilation) with persistently elevated troponin and BNP suggesting ongoing myocardial involvement
Cardiac MRI shows late gadolinium enhancement (endocardial and spotty transmural) indicating myocardial injury/fibrosis not explained by coronary disease
Coronary angiography shows only moderate LAD stenosis, arguing against ischemic cardiomyopathy as the primary cause and supporting myopathy-associated cardiomyopathy
Liver mass biopsy showed neuroendocrine morphology with synaptophysin positivity and high proliferative index (Ki-67 30%), supporting neuroendocrine carcinoma.
Gallium-68 dotatate PET showed no uptake in liver/lymph nodes, arguing against well-differentiated somatostatin-receptor-positive metastatic NET.
No extrahepatic disease identified on chest CT and only the hepatic segment 4 lesion was FDG-avid, supporting a primary hepatic origin rather than metastasis.

Imaging features (T2-hyperintense lesion with peripheral rim arterial enhancement and persistent venous enhancement) and non-cirrhotic background are not classic for hepatocellular carcinoma despite markedly elevated AFP/AFP-L3%.

PMC6402948.diagnosis: Conjunctival granuloma due to an embedded foreign body (conjunctival/episcleral foreign body granuloma)
rationale_summary: 2-week non-traumatic, painless unilateral red eye with chemosis and a conjunctival granuloma attached to sclera with central epithelial necrosis

Ultrasound biomicroscopy shows a high-reflectivity echo embedded in conjunctiva/episclera/superficial sclera consistent with a foreign body, surrounded by low-reflective granulomatous tissue and pseudocysts
Infectious/systemic work-up unrevealing (normal QuantiFERON-TB and chest CT; negative syphilis serology), supporting a local foreign-body reaction

PMC10577159.diagnosis: Odontogenic cutaneous sinus tract (cutaneous fistula) secondary to chronic apical periodontitis of the right maxillary first molar
rationale_summary: Chronic, tender, erythematous, fluctuant cheek nodule persisting ~1 year and unresponsive to antibiotics/intralesional steroid suggests a draining sinus rather than primary skin lesion
Panoramic radiograph shows right maxillary sinus mucosal thickening adjacent to the palatal root of the right maxillary first molar with prior poor-quality endodontic treatment
Palpable firm cord-like tract from the mucogingival junction of the involved molar extending through the cheek to the facial nodule supports an odontogenic sinus tract

PMC8062124.diagnosis: Infected peritoneal (mesothelial) inclusion cyst / benign multicystic peritoneal mesothelioma presenting as a solitary pelvic cyst
rationale_summary: Acute fever, right lower abdominal pain with guarding, vomiting and dysuria suggests infection/inflammation from a pelvic mass

Imaging shows a solitary, well-defined intraperitoneal pelvic cyst with low-attenuation fluid and a nonenhancing wall, compressing the bladder, with no communication to bowel or bladder and no ascites
Normal pancreas and no other organ pathology argues against pancreatic pseudocyst or organ-derived cyst
Long-term occupational asbestos exposure supports a mesothelial/peritoneal mesothelioma-related cystic lesion

PMC11832257.diagnosis: Intramuscular myxoma of the iliopsoas muscle
rationale_summary: Middle-aged man with chronic hip pain worsened by hip flexion and a deep iliopsoas-region mass on CT/MRI

Imaging shows a well-circumscribed lobulated intramuscular lesion extending along fat planes, T1 hypointense and T2 hyperintense with marked postcontrast enhancement-typical for a myxoid tumor such as intramuscular myxoma
No inflammatory lab abnormalities and no osseous involvement on radiograph support a benign soft-tissue tumor rather than infection or bone pathology
Amorphous calcifications can be present within a long-standing soft-tissue mass in the psoas compartment

PMC9127846.diagnosis: Osteoarticular (wrist) tuberculosis causing tuberculous septic arthritis/osteomyelitis
rationale_summary: >3 months of progressive wrist pain with minimal systemic symptoms and only mild leukocytosis/elevated inflammatory markers
Imaging shows diffuse carpal destruction/osteopenia with cystic bony changes, suggesting chronic granulomatous infection rather than acute pyogenic arthritis
Purulent synovial fluid and extensive synovitis with severe bony erosion/cartilage loss at surgery
Epidemiologic risk: immigrant from Afghanistan; HIV negative; chest radiograph without pulmonary disease consistent with possible isolated extrapulmonary TB

PMC4506815.diagnosis: Diabetic muscle infarction (diabetic myonecrosis) of the anterior compartment with cystic degeneration
rationale_summary: Long-standing type 2 diabetes with marked hyperglycemia
Painless enlarging intramuscular mass in tibialis anterior/extensor hallucis longus mimicking tumor
Normal/low inflammatory markers and near-normal CK (CRP 0.05, WBC 4,580, CK 164) argues against infection
CT/MRI show intramuscular mass with nonenhancing cystic/necrotic areas and only slight heterogeneous enhancement, without surrounding edema or calcification
Normal ankle-brachial index and well-visualized anterior tibialis artery make large-vessel ischemia less likely, supporting diabetic myonecrosis

PMC9009014.diagnosis: Nonarteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy (ischemic optic neuropathy of the left optic nerve)
rationale_summary: Sudden unilateral severe vision loss with eye pain, marked RAPD, and profound color vision loss OS
Normal fundus initially can occur in posterior/early ischemic optic neuropathy; VEP delayed/low amplitude and visual field generalized depression support optic nerve dysfunction
MRI shows restricted diffusion in the left optic nerve (DWI hyperintensity with ADC hypointensity), favoring acute ischemia over demyelinating optic neuritis

Strong vascular risk context: severe uncontrolled hypertension, chronic renal failure, hyperlipidemia, anemia; brain MRI shows microhemorrhages and periventricular white-matter disease consistent with hypertensive small-vessel disease

PMC9721246.diagnosis: Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma arising in a mesenchymal hamartoma of the liver
rationale_summary: Childhood diagnosis of a large multiseptated cystic right-lobe hepatic mass consistent with mesenchymal hamartoma, with long interval without resection
Now shows interval growth plus a new peripheral enhancing solid component within the cystic lesion and regional (hepatoduodenal/portocaval) lymphadenopathy
Markedly elevated CA 19-9 (1075 U/mL) with normal AFP and CEA supports biliary-type malignancy rather than hepatocellular tumor
MRI and FDG-PET show heterogeneous signal and focal hypermetabolism, raising concern for malignant transformation within the pre-existing lesion

PMC8379665.diagnosis: Cavernous sinus syndrome (likely iatrogenic carotid-cavernous fistula) after mechanical thrombectomy
rationale_summary: New ipsilateral ocular motor deficits (ptosis with mild limitation of abduction and supraduction) localize to cranial nerves III/VI in the cavernous sinus/orbital apex region
Mild conjunctival chemosis supports venous congestion consistent with a carotid-cavernous fistula
No anisocoria or RAPD and normal fundus make primary optic nerve/retinal pathology less likely
Head CT showed no new intracranial lesion to explain symptoms; orbital CT was unremarkable, fitting a vascular cavernous sinus process after endovascular intervention

PMC2396655.diagnosis: Metastatic melanoma with dedifferentiated/undifferentiated (marker-negative) gastric metastasis
rationale_summary: History of high-risk cutaneous melanoma with subsequent widespread metastatic disease (liver, lung nodules, subcutaneous recurrences, bone metastases)
New confluent upper abdominal mass involving stomach/pancreas with endoscopic 3 cm hard gastro-fundal lesion consistent with metastatic involvement

Biopsy shows undifferentiated neoplasm; broad immunohistochemistry negative for carcinoma, GIST, and lymphoma markers, supporting a dedifferentiated metastasis in the context of known melanoma despite loss of melanocytic markers

PMC11843684.diagnosis: Left brachial plexus neuropraxia from intraoperative positioning/traction (perioperative peripheral nerve injury)
rationale_summary: New painless unilateral upper-limb weakness noted shortly after surgery with no headache, dysarthria, altered consciousness, or leg weakness, making central neurologic event less likely
Deficits involve shoulder and multiple distal upper-limb muscle groups, fitting a peripheral plexus-level pattern rather than a single nerve

Surgery performed supine with arms alongside body and slight neck extension; patient had preexisting left shoulder impingement and limited elbow extension, increasing susceptibility to traction/compression injury
Bilateral superficial cervical plexus block is superficial and typically sensory; the motor pattern and unilateral involvement are more consistent with brachial plexus injury than block effect

PMC4140119.diagnosis: Charcot spinal arthropathy (neuropathic spinal arthropathy) at L2-L3

rationale_summary: Hypocalcemia with hyperphosphatemia and inappropriately low intact PTH indicates hypoparathyroidism

PMC8294152.diagnosis: Burkitt lymphoma (high-grade B-cell lymphoma) of the mandible

PMC6176474.diagnosis: Wound botulism (Clostridium botulinum) from skin-popping heroin use
rationale_summary: Acute progressive cranial nerve palsies (marked ptosis, diplopia, dysarthria, dysphagia with pooling secretions) followed by descending weakness (neck, proximal upper extremities) and impending respiratory failure
High-risk exposure: daily heroin use via skin popping with multiple deep-cavity abscesses requiring incision and drainage
No history suggesting foodborne botulism (denies bulging canned food ingestion) and presentation fits botulism more than GBS or myasthenia gravis

PMC8896953.diagnosis: Spinal tuberculosis (Pott disease) involving L4 with paravertebral/psoas abscess
rationale_summary: Chronic inflammatory low back pain with night sweats and marked weight loss

MRI shows destructive L4 vertebral lesion with cortical disruption, adjacent disc involvement, and large paravertebral collection extending into the psoas-typical of tuberculous spondylodiscitis with cold abscess
Persistent retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy supports ongoing extrapulmonary TB despite lack of pulmonary lesions

PMC7036447.diagnosis: Retinal neovascularization secondary to chronic central serous chorioretinopathy (CSCR) with vitreous hemorrhage

No diabetes, infectious cause, peripheral ischemia, or other retinal vascular disease; peripheral retina and remaining vasculature are normal, supporting CSCR-related neovascularization

PMC9069601.diagnosis: Infective endocarditis with immune complex-mediated glomerulonephritis (endocarditis-associated GN)
rationale_summary: Constitutional symptoms for 2 years (subjective fevers, night sweats) with very high ESR/CRP and anemia
Palpable purpura with skin biopsy showing leukocytoclastic vasculitis with IgM/C3 deposition, consistent with immune-complex vasculitis
Microscopic hematuria with acanthocytes and rising creatinine indicating glomerulonephritis
Renal biopsy shows mesangial proliferative GN with mesangial electron-dense deposits and C3/IgM/C1q staining, fitting immune-complex GN and mimicking lupus despite atypical lupus serologies
Marked rheumatoid factor elevation and steroid-responsive/relapsing course support an immune-complex process such as endocarditis-associated disease even with initially negative blood cultures/echo

PMC9361795.diagnosis: Borderline tuberculoid leprosy (Hansen disease) presenting with granulomatous cheilitis
rationale_summary: Persistent lower-lip swelling initially labeled cheilitis granulomatosa but unresponsive to intralesional corticosteroids
New erythematous plaques on face developed subsequently
Biopsies from lip and plaque show epithelioid granulomas with periadnexal and perineural lymphocytic infiltration, a characteristic pattern for leprosy
Tuberculosis workup (Mantoux, chest radiograph) and slit-skin smear were unremarkable, supporting paucibacillary leprosy over TB or multibacillary disease

PMC8962241.diagnosis: Cerebral air embolism secondary to Staphylococcus aureus-infected necrotic lung cancer invading the left atrium (systemic arterial air emboli causing acute ischemic stroke)
rationale_summary: Acute focal neurologic deficits (aphasia, left weakness, neglect) with NIHSS 7
Head CT showed multiple small low-density areas in cortex/subcortex interpreted as air emboli
Brain MRI DWI cortical/subcortical hyperintensities consistent with acute ischemia; SWI dot-like low signals compatible with embolic phenomena
Chest CT showed left hilar tumor invading left atrium/left main bronchus with small low-density areas suggesting necrosis/air within tumor
Blood cultures grew Staphylococcus aureus with shock/fever, supporting infected tumor/abscess as source of systemic air embolization via left atrial invasion

PMC3608321.diagnosis: Ectopic ACTH syndrome from an occult neuroendocrine tumor
rationale_summary: ACTH-dependent hypercortisolism: very high urinary free cortisol, loss of diurnal cortisol rhythm, and measurable plasma ACTH (30-45 ng/L)
No suppression with low- or high-dose dexamethasone supports nonpituitary ACTH source
Pituitary MRI negative and inferior petrosal sinus sampling after CRH shows no central-to-peripheral ACTH gradient and no ACTH rise, arguing against Cushing disease
Mildly elevated chromogranin A suggests neuroendocrine tumor despite negative octreotide scan and CT only showing mild adrenal enlargement (likely ACTH-driven hyperplasia)

PMC8186929.diagnosis: Anomalous right coronary artery arising from the pulmonary artery (ARCAPA)
rationale_summary: Coronary angiography showed markedly ectatic left main/LAD/LCx without obstructive stenosis, arguing against ACS from atherosclerotic occlusion
RCA ostium could not be engaged from the aorta, and the RCA filled retrograde via collaterals from the left coronary system
Angiographic review suggested the RCA was draining into the pulmonary artery/right ventricular outflow tract, consistent with ARCAPA physiology (left-to-right coronary steal)
Presentation with cardiogenic shock and biventricular dysfunction fits severe ischemia/steal from a congenital anomalous coronary connection rather than flow-limiting CAD

PMC10191493.diagnosis: Isolated duodenal Crohn disease causing duodenal stricture (gastric outlet obstruction)
rationale_summary: 12-year-old with 2 months of weight loss, abdominal pain, bilious vomiting and imaging showing gastric/duodenal (1st-2nd portion) dilation with decompressed 3rd portion, consistent with an obstructing duodenal stricture
Endoscopy demonstrated complete obstruction at the second portion of the duodenum
Labs with thrombocytosis and elevated inflammatory markers support inflammatory bowel disease

Biopsies showed chronic gastritis and mildly active duodenitis proximal to the stricture with no malignancy, viral inclusions, or granulomas, compatible with Crohn disease despite absence of granulomas

PMC10901261.diagnosis: Severe secondary (functional) mitral regurgitation due to ischaemic cardiomyopathy with left ventricular dilatation and leaflet tethering (Carpentier I/IIIb)
rationale_summary: Transoesophageal echo: severe centrally directed MR jet from A2-P2 attributed to annular dilatation and leaflet tethering (Carpentier I and IIIb)
Quantification shows severe/disproportionate MR: EROA 80 mm² and regurgitant volume 76 mL
Advanced ischaemic cardiomyopathy with markedly dilated LV (EDD 72 mm) and EF ~20% on dobutamine supports secondary/functional mechanism
Haemodynamics consistent with severe MR: giant left atrial V waves up to 65 mmHg and pulmonary hypertension (sPAP 60 mmHg)

PMC6257925.diagnosis: Iris melanocytoma involving the trabecular meshwork (angle melanocytoma)
rationale_summary: Left eye gonioscopy showed densely hyperpigmented dark brown raised mounds in the trabecular meshwork resembling iris tissue
Anterior segment OCT confirmed elevated tissue within the angle with an open angle recess and no ciliary body/iris mass
No signs of pigment dispersion (no retrocorneal pigment, no iris transillumination defects) and the lesion remained stable over 6 months

PMC2934912.diagnosis: Acute brucellosis with cholestatic hepatitis and SIADH
rationale_summary: Sheep farmer consuming nonpasteurized cheese with 2 weeks of nocturnal fever and arthralgia (zoonotic exposure consistent with brucellosis)
Marked cholestatic liver injury (very high alkaline phosphatase and GGT) with hepatomegaly, RUQ pain, and direct hyperbilirubinemia; no biliary dilatation on imaging
Hyponatremia with low plasma osmolality, inappropriately concentrated urine, and high urine sodium in a euvolemic patient with normal thyroid/adrenal/pituitary tests (SIADH)
Mild splenomegaly and pericardial effusion can occur with systemic brucellosis; viral hepatitis/EBV/CMV serologies negative

PMC5807253.diagnosis: Metformin-associated lactic acidosis (MALA) precipitated by acute kidney injury from dehydration/shock
rationale_summary: On metformin 1000 mg twice daily with sudden severe AKI (creatinine 8.07 mg/dL from 0.86) after 3 days of vomiting/diarrhea and hypotension
Profound high-anion gap metabolic acidosis (pH 6.57, HCO3 2, anion gap 30) with marked hyperlactatemia (lactate 16.3)
Shock with PEA arrest and vasopressor requirement consistent with severe lactic acidosis; no alternative abdominal catastrophe on CT

PMC5896219.diagnosis: Acute compartment syndrome of the right forearm/hand secondary to severe crystal arthropathy (gout with concomitant CPPD/pseudogout)
rationale_summary: Progressive severe forearm/hand pain and swelling with tense compartments, pain on passive stretch, sensory changes, and decreased capillary refill
Elevated compartment pressures (20-24 mm Hg volar forearm; 45-48 mm Hg carpal tunnel/palm) prompting urgent fasciotomy

Crystal-proven inflammatory arthritis: elbow aspirate with monosodium urate crystals and very high synovial WBC; wrist aspirate with calcium pyrophosphate crystals; intraoperative flexor tendon sheath tophi
No evidence of vascular occlusion on CTA/Doppler and no intraoperative pus/necrosis to suggest primary infection

PMC4100269.diagnosis: Obstructive jaundice from a porta hepatis neurofibrosarcoma (malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor) in neurofibromatosis type 1 causing secondary extreme hypercholesterolemia (lipoprotein X)
rationale_summary: Known NF1 with multiple masses (porta hepatis, splenic, retroperitoneal) suggesting NF1-associated malignancy

Imaging/MRCP/ERCP show abrupt mid-CBD occlusion and long proximal CBD stricture with marked intra/extrahepatic biliary dilatation, consistent with malignant extrahepatic obstruction at the porta hepatis
Cholestatic liver profile with pruritus and progressive jaundice

Total cholesterol and LDL >1,000 mg/dL with only modest triglyceride elevation, and cholesterol rapidly falls after biliary stenting, consistent with cholestasis-related lipoprotein X hypercholesterolemia rather than primary dyslipidemia

PMC9723480.diagnosis: Sporotrichoid cutaneous nontuberculous mycobacterial infection (likely Mycobacterium marinum)
rationale_summary: Painful tender erythematous nodules in a linear (sporotrichoid) distribution along the left upper extremity with hand edema
Skin biopsy shows granulomatous lymphohistiocytic inflammation with neutrophils and acid-fast bacilli
Slit-skin smear for M. leprae and sputum AFB are negative, arguing against leprosy or pulmonary TB
Tissue culture negative but AFB-positive granulomatous skin lesions support cutaneous nontuberculous mycobacteria

PMC6342725.diagnosis: Flecainide-induced acute hypersensitivity pneumonitis (drug-induced interstitial lung disease)
rationale_summary: Acute onset of fever, chills, dry cough, leukocytosis, and rapidly worsening hypoxemia within hours of starting flecainide, with recurrence/worsening after the second dose
Chest CT with diffuse bilateral ground-glass opacities and multifocal consolidation consistent with acute drug-induced pneumonitis
Findings argue against cardiogenic pulmonary edema: preserved LVEF, no mitral stenosis, small/collapsed IVC, and only mildly elevated BNP
Clinical deterioration stopped prompting discontinuation of flecainide, implicating the drug temporally

PMC8785026.diagnosis: COVID-19-associated varicella-like (papulovesicular) exanthem with progression to severe COVID-19 pneumonia/ARDS and shock
rationale_summary: Confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection with fever/cough after known exposure
Developed trunk/extremity maculopapular rash evolving within 24-48 h to papulovesicular eruption, sparing mucosae/palms/soles-pattern reported with COVID-19 varicella-like exanthem

Subsequent rapid respiratory deterioration with new CT ground-glass opacities/consolidations typical of COVID-19 and marked drop in oxygenation index (490→97) consistent with severe COVID-19 pneumonia/ARDS
Concurrent hypotension requiring high-dose vasopressors consistent with COVID-19-related shock in severe disease

PMC10699832.diagnosis: Malignant gastrointestinal neuroectodermal tumor (GNET) with EWSR1-ATF1 fusion (extra-gastrointestinal/cervical presentation)
rationale_summary: Cervical tumor with nested epithelioid/oval cells separated by thin fibrous septa and clear eosinophilic cytoplasm
Immunophenotype: S100 and SOX10 positive with synaptophysin/CD57 positivity; melanocytic markers (HMB45, Melan-A, MITF) negative; CD99 negative (argues against Ewing sarcoma)
Molecular confirmation: EWSR1 rearrangement with EWSR1-ATF1 t(12;22) fusion on NGS, characteristic of GNET/clear cell sarcoma-like tumors
Clinical course with local recurrence on PET-CT supports malignant soft-tissue neoplasm

PMC2394532.diagnosis: Pentazocine-induced myopathy with soft-tissue calcification (fibrous myopathy causing contractures)
rationale_summary: 6-year painless progressive stiffness, woody induration, wasting and severe restriction of proximal/back muscle movements with preserved strength within limited range and intact sensation
History of prolonged intramuscular pentazocine (with promethazine) dependence preceding symptom onset

Imaging shows multiple soft-tissue calcifications and muscle hyperdensity; CPK and EMG are normal, supporting a non-inflammatory fibrosing/calcifying myopathy rather than primary myositis or neuropathy

PMC10113930.diagnosis: Paradoxical embolic renal infarction from infective endocarditis in unrepaired cyanotic congenital heart disease
rationale_summary: Acute right renal infarct with sudden severe abdominal pain
Unrepaired tricuspid atresia with unrestricted ASD/VSD and chronic cyanosis provides a right-to-left shunt allowing systemic (paradoxical) embolization
Recent flu-like illness and poor dentition raise concern for bacteremia/infective endocarditis as an embolic source despite lack of peripheral stigmata and being afebrile
Cerebral CT negative, supporting isolated systemic embolic event to kidney

PMC6169107.diagnosis: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system (PACNS)
rationale_summary: Progressive multifocal supratentorial cortical and deep gray matter lesions with gadolinium enhancement and later hemorrhagic components
Recurrent focal-to-bilateral tonic-clonic seizures with subacute headaches, cognitive decline, and transient behavioral change consistent with inflammatory CNS process
CSF repeatedly inflammatory (pleocytosis, elevated protein) with later development of oligoclonal bands

Extensive infectious, systemic autoimmune, sarcoidosis, and paraneoplastic/autoimmune encephalitis evaluations negative; no evidence of large-vessel vasculitis or retinal vasculitis/Susac, prompting brain biopsy for suspected CNS-limited vasculitis

PMC9533799.diagnosis: Hypertriglyceridemia-induced acute pancreatitis associated with diabetic ketoacidosis

Lipase is elevated (257 IU/L) and ultrasound shows no gallstones or biliary obstruction

History of prior DKA complicated by hypertriglyceridemia and pancreatitis supports recurrent hypertriglyceridemic pancreatitis in this setting

PMC6073079.diagnosis: Pembrolizumab-induced hypertrophic lichen planus (lichenoid immune-related adverse event)
rationale_summary: Rash began ~6 weeks after starting pembrolizumab (immune checkpoint inhibitor timing).
Exam shows innumerable hyperkeratotic papulonodules/ulcerations consistent with hypertrophic lichen planus and concurrent annular erythematous plaques.
Known history of oral lichen planus with simultaneous flare after pembrolizumab supports lichenoid exacerbation/induction rather than de novo unrelated dermatosis.

PMC2914667.diagnosis: Paraneoplastic parkinsonism (autoimmune/paraneoplastic basal ganglia encephalitis) secondary to metastatic colon adenocarcinoma
rationale_summary: Subacute onset of severe symmetric parkinsonism (mask facies, resting tremor, bradykinesia, rigidity with cogwheeling, freezing/festinating gait, postural instability) developing over days
Associated autonomic symptom (new nighttime urinary incontinence) and hyperreflexia without focal cranial nerve palsies, suggesting a diffuse central process rather than idiopathic Parkinson disease
Occurs in the setting of newly diagnosed stage IV colon adenocarcinoma, supporting a paraneoplastic etiology
Marked clinical improvement after high-dose IV corticosteroids supports an immune-mediated/paraneoplastic syndrome

PMC10310912.diagnosis: Transient duodenal microperforation/serosal injury during difficult cholecystectomy (sealed, causing brief contrast extravasation on IOC)
rationale_summary: Severe acute/gangrenous cholecystitis with dense adhesions to duodenum requiring difficult dissection and conversion to open
Duodenum had patchy bile discoloration suggesting local injury/ischemia despite no obvious serosal tear
IOC showed an unexpected streak of contrast inferior to the cystic duct consistent with brief extraluminal contrast

No leak seen with saline flush and pressurized methylene blue, and repeat IOC 10 minutes later showed resolution of the streak, supporting a small self-sealing injury rather than persistent bile duct leak

PMC5870216.diagnosis: Osteochondrolipoma (osteochondroid lipoma) of the submandibular region
rationale_summary: 32-year-old man with a slow-growing, soft, slightly movable right submandibular mass for 1 year

PMC10407195.diagnosis: Pituitary metastasis from sublingual adenocarcinoma (sellar metastasis)
rationale_summary: History of metastatic sublingual adenocarcinoma with prior lung and bone metastases

Secondary adrenal insufficiency and secondary hypothyroidism consistent with pituitary involvement by an infiltrative/neoplastic process

PMC6050456.diagnosis: Vagus nerve stimulator-induced sinus arrest (symptomatic sinus pauses causing syncope)
rationale_summary: New random sudden falls with brief loss of consciousness and no postictal period, differing from prior complex partial seizures
Cardiac workup (Ziopatch, ECG, echo, stress test) initially unrevealing
Implantable loop recorder documented 10-12 second sinus pauses that exactly matched the times of falls/injuries
History of VNS with gradual output current increases preceding onset of spells, supporting VNS-related bradyarrhythmia/asystole
