PMC9006328.diagnosis: Retained intraocular foreign body (lens/metal fragment) causing chronic postoperative anterior uveitis after phacoemulsification
rationale_summary: Post-op day 1 slit-lamp showed a poorly defined intraocular foreign body at 12 o'clock with anterior chamber cells and medium keratic precipitates
Inflammation repeatedly recurred whenever topical steroids were tapered and improved when steroids were restarted, persisting over 6 months
Systemic/rheumatologic workup was unrevealing (normal CRP/ANA; rheumatologic disease excluded), supporting a local postoperative cause
Planned gonioscopy to further evaluate the anterior chamber foreign body implies suspected retained fragment as the driver of chronic uveitis

PMC10971827.diagnosis: Macrophage activation syndrome (secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis) complicating new-onset systemic lupus erythematosus
rationale_summary: Newly diagnosed SLE (ANA ≥1:10,000, anti-dsDNA 1:3200, low C3/C4, cytopenias, proteinuria) with persistent fever and systemic inflammation
Progressive pancytopenia with hyperferritinemia, hypertriglyceridemia, and hypofibrinogenemia (to 94 mg/dL) despite antibiotics and negative cultures/procalcitonin

HScore 195 indicating ~80-88% probability of macrophage activation syndrome/secondary HLH

PMC4054582.diagnosis: Neuroleptic malignant syndrome (NMS) due to perospirone
rationale_summary: Recent antipsychotic dose escalation (perospirone to 40 mg/day) followed by acute onset of severe hyperthermia (39.8°C), rigidity/tremor, akathisia, autonomic instability (tachycardia, fluctuating blood pressure, heavy sweating), and altered mental status (restlessness, confusion).
Laboratory evidence of muscle injury and systemic stress: elevated CPK (813 U/L) and myoglobin (486 µg/L) with leukocytosis (18.3×10^9/L).
Infectious/structural CNS causes less likely: normal CSF, unremarkable neuroimaging; EEG shows diffuse slowing consistent with metabolic/toxic encephalopathy.

PMC9976724.diagnosis: Langerhans cell histiocytosis (eosinophilic granuloma) involving the T10 costovertebral junction
rationale_summary: CT shows focal costovertebral joint widening with scalloping/sclerosis involving T10 vertebral body/pedicle and adjacent rib
MRI shows marrow edema and nonspecific enhancement without spinal canal invasion
Biopsy shows fibrous/myxoid tissue with chronic inflammatory infiltrate and rare histiocytic elements; CD1a immunostain ordered to characterize histiocytes, supporting Langerhans cell histiocytosis
Normal inflammatory markers and lack of FDG uptake make aggressive infection or malignancy less likely

PMC9523897.diagnosis: Primary cutaneous cryptococcosis of the lower lip (Cryptococcus neoformans/gattii)
rationale_summary: Chronic (10-month), slowly enlarging, painful, thick-crusted ulcer on the lower lip with serous discharge and no response to topical antifungal/antimicrobial agents, antibiotics, or empiric immunosuppression
Biopsies showed inflammatory/reactive epidermal changes and mixed dermal infiltrate without malignancy, supporting an infectious/inflammatory etiology over squamous cell carcinoma
Workup excluded key mimics: negative leishmania smear, negative direct immunofluorescence for immunobullous disease, and no evidence of immunodeficiency (HIV negative; normal NBT)

PMC5745655.diagnosis: Benzodiazepine withdrawal syndrome (protracted withdrawal with rebound anxiety)

PMC12016228.diagnosis: Relapsing polychondritis-associated chronic aseptic lymphocytic meningitis (autoimmune meningoencephalitis with leptomeningeal enhancement/hydrocephalus)
rationale_summary: Known relapsing polychondritis (auricular/nasal/laryngotracheal chondritis; auricular deformity on exam) with no treatment/follow-up
Recurrent episodes of lymphocytic (aseptic) meningitis with progressive cognitive decline
Current presentation with headache, fever, disorientation, gait difficulty, transient sphincter loss and CSF lymphocytic pleocytosis
MRI shows nodular leptomeningeal enhancement with ventricular enlargement (hydrocephalus) and parenchymal nodular enhancement, supporting inflammatory meningoencephalitis

Extensive infectious PCR/cultures negative (including ruling out Mollaret), cytology/flow negative for malignancy, and systemic autoimmune/infiltrative workup (sarcoid, IgG4, Sjögren, vasculitis, etc.) negative, making RP-related CNS inflammation most consistent

PMC10567328.diagnosis: Pediatric Crohn's disease (small-bowel/terminal ileal Crohn's with extraintestinal manifestations)
rationale_summary: Chronic, recurrent large oral ulcers with intermittent fever and later bloody stools and malnutrition
Initial endoscopies negative but later colonoscopy showed large terminal ileal ulcers with chronic inflammatory infiltrates/granulation tissue (no granulomas required for Crohn's)
Prometheus IBD sgi serology/genetics pattern reported as consistent with Crohn's disease with elevated CRP
Extraintestinal manifestations developed (hip effusions/arthritis)
Cerebral venous sinus thrombosis occurred with negative hypercoagulability workup, consistent with inflammatory bowel disease-associated thrombosis
Clinical improvement in gastrointestinal symptoms after switching to ustekinumab (IBD therapy)

Current hemostatic profile shows severe hypofibrinogenemia (fibrinogen 0.34 g/L; RI 1.30-4.80) confirmed on repeat testing and heat-precipitation assay with almost absent band
Platelet count and buccal mucosal bleeding time are within reference intervals, supporting a coagulation factor defect rather than primary hemostasis
D-dimers and antithrombin are within reference intervals, arguing against active DIC as the cause of low fibrinogen

PMC4610771.diagnosis: Atypical hemolytic uremic syndrome (complement-mediated thrombotic microangiopathy)
rationale_summary: Microangiopathic hemolysis and thrombocytopenia (schistocytes, elevated LDH, platelets 71×10^9/L) with acute kidney injury (creatinine 159 µmol/L) and neurologic involvement (seizures)
Severe ocular thrombotic microangiopathy (bilateral retinal hemorrhages with central retinal artery and vein occlusion)
ADAMTS13 activity 61% argues against TTP (not severely deficient) despite initial presumptive TTP diagnosis and plasma exchange

PMC8719119.diagnosis: Focal seizure with postictal deficits (Todd's paralysis) mimicking acute ischemic stroke
rationale_summary: Acute onset confusion, severe aphasia, right facial droop, and right hemiparesis with normal glucose/electrolytes and no hemorrhage or acute infarct on noncontrast CT
CT angiography showed no large-vessel occlusion despite high NIHSS
CT perfusion showed left hemispheric hyperperfusion (reduced TTP/Tmax with increased CBV/CBF), interpreted as compatible with seizure activity rather than ischemia

Obstruction of the tricuspid valve with hypotension, tachycardia, distant heart sounds, and severe pericardial effusion

PMC11509490.diagnosis: Uterine lipoleiomyoma with MDM2/CDK4 expression (benign smooth muscle tumor mimicking sarcoma)
rationale_summary: Postmenopausal bleeding with a large intracavitary uterine mass extending into the cervix but no myometrial invasion on MRI
Biopsy shows spindle-cell proliferation beneath non-atypical epithelium, supporting a mesenchymal uterine tumor
Immunoprofile indicates smooth muscle differentiation (h-caldesmon+, α-SMA+) and excludes common mimics (CD34−, CD99−, ALK−)
Imaging shows heterogeneous mass with focal slight T1 high-signal areas consistent with fatty component, compatible with lipoleiomyoma
Moderate FDG uptake (SUVmax 6.2) and low ADC can occur in benign leiomyoma variants, contributing to sarcoma-like appearance

PMC9589461.diagnosis: Primary syphilis (Treponema pallidum) presenting as a penile frenulum ulcer/tear
rationale_summary: Superficial 5-mm shallow partial-thickness lesion on the penile frenulum with a yellow base and minimal discharge, without surrounding erythema or lymphadenopathy, can mimic a traumatic tear but is compatible with a syphilitic chancre variant
Man who has sex with men with multiple casual partners; last syphilis test was 6 months prior, so new infection is possible despite prior nonreactive testing
Tissue from foreskin/frenulum was sent for histology, consistent with concern for an underlying infectious etiology rather than simple trauma

PMC11491757.diagnosis: Spontaneous cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) rhinorrhea from a sphenoid sinus skull base defect (spontaneous CSF leak)
rationale_summary: Copious persistent clear unilateral (left-predominant) rhinorrhea refractory to antibiotics/antihistamines/ipratropium
Drainage worsens with bending/lifting and on waking, consistent with positional CSF leak
Prior severe frontal/bitemporal headache with tinnitus and visual disturbances that resolved when clear rhinorrhea began, suggesting pressure relief after leak
CT shows isolated left sphenoid sinus air-fluid level without sinonasal mass/obstruction, supporting CSF in sphenoid sinus
Obesity is a risk factor for spontaneous skull base CSF leaks/idiopathic intracranial hypertension

Inflammatory markers mildly elevated without MIS-C features; clinical improvement without a specific alternative diagnosis identified

PMC6388103.diagnosis: Diffuse hemispheric glioma, H3 G34-mutant (diffuse infiltrative glioma)
rationale_summary: Young adult with progressive focal neurologic deficits and behavioral change

PMC2329657.diagnosis: Reactive (secondary) thrombocytosis due to severe iron-deficiency anemia
rationale_summary: Profound microcytic anemia with depleted iron stores (Hb 4.6 g/dl, MCV 58 fl, ferritin <1.5 ng/ml, very low serum iron and transferrin saturation)
Extreme thrombocytosis (1703×10^3/μl) with marrow showing increased megakaryopoiesis but normal cytogenetics, supporting a reactive process rather than clonal MPN
No evidence of bleeding, inflammation, or malignancy (normal CRP/ESR; negative endoscopy/gynecologic evaluation)
Platelet count normalized rapidly after iron repletion and transfusions, without cytoreductive therapy, consistent with iron-deficiency-driven reactive thrombocytosis

PMC10982020.diagnosis: Systemic lupus erythematosus presenting with lupus peritonitis/perihepatitis (Fitz-Hugh-Curtis-like perihepatic capsulitis)
rationale_summary: Diffuse perihepatic capsular enhancement with RUQ pain and fever persisted despite broad-spectrum antibiotics and negative Chlamydia/gonorrhea testing and no sexual activity, arguing against infectious Fitz-Hugh-Curtis syndrome
Autoimmune features: very high ANA (speckled), new anti-dsDNA positivity, facial erythema, and serositis on CT (bilateral pleural effusions and ascites)

PMC10408629.diagnosis: Intracranial lipoma of the right Sylvian fissure encasing the right MCA, with an associated unruptured fusiform MCA aneurysm

PMC8050037.diagnosis: Cystic lymphangioma (subserosal lymphatic malformation) of the gallbladder wall
rationale_summary: Imaging showed a low-attenuation, non-contrast-filling cystic lesion within the gallbladder wall
Grossly, multiple small (3-5 mm) intramural/subserosal cysts containing clear serous (non-bilious) fluid
Histology: cysts in subserosa lined by bland cuboidal/low columnar epithelium without atypia, surrounded by dense fibrous tissue
Absence of Rokitansky-Aschoff sinuses or mucosal invagination argues against adenomyomatosis; lesion is subserosal rather than mucosal
Associated small tubular structures around cysts support a lymphatic malformation pattern

PMC2958973.diagnosis: Rectovestibular fistula (low anorectal malformation) with a perineal lipoma
rationale_summary: Term female with imperforate anus and meconium passing through a small opening in the posterior vaginal vestibule consistent with a rectovestibular fistula
Contrast study shows a long narrow fistulous tract to a distended colorectal pouch ~3-3.5 cm from the skin, supporting anorectal malformation anatomy
Perineal mass is skin-covered, pedunculated, predominantly fat-intensity on MRI with a single central vessel and no presacral extension, consistent with lipoma rather than teratoma
No associated vertebral/renal/cardiac anomalies and normal sacrum/spinal cord imaging, fitting an isolated low ARM presentation

PMC5743514.diagnosis: Wernicke encephalopathy due to thiamine deficiency (TPN-associated) after liver transplantation

MRI with symmetric lesions involving thalami, pons, and periventricular regions, a pattern consistent with Wernicke encephalopathy
No alcohol use, normal ammonia/electrolytes/ABG, and tacrolimus trough in therapeutic range, making alternative metabolic or drug-toxicity causes less supported by case facts

PMC6510746.diagnosis: Giant cell tumor of soft tissue (osteoclast-like giant cell tumor) of the breast
rationale_summary: Breast biopsies twice showed spindle/oval mononuclear cells admixed with osteoclast-like multinucleated giant cells, stromal hyalinization, lymphocytic infiltrate, and focal hemorrhage without necrosis
Giant cells were strongly CD68-positive and mononuclear cells weakly CD68-positive, supporting a histiocytic/giant-cell tumor rather than epithelial carcinoma
No atypical epithelial cells identified and tumor cells were ER/PR/HER2-negative, arguing against conventional invasive breast carcinoma despite malignant-appearing imaging
Axillary node FNA showed only normal lymphocytes, not metastatic carcinoma

Marked isolated LDL elevation (LDL-C 357 mg/dL; total cholesterol 429 mg/dL) with near-normal triglycerides

PMC5602830.diagnosis: Dermatitis herpetiformis flare with purpuric/hemorrhagic lesions (IgA-mediated)
rationale_summary: Known 7-year history of dermatitis herpetiformis with worsening after stopping dapsone and relaxing gluten-free diet
Biopsy shows subepidermal blister with neutrophils in papillary dermal tips and marked dermal hemorrhage without leukocytoclastic vasculitis

PMC6090673.diagnosis: Antiphospholipid syndrome-associated cutaneous thrombotic vasculopathy (microvascular thrombosis causing skin necrosis/ulcers)
rationale_summary: Longstanding SLE with secondary antiphospholipid syndrome and persistent lupus anticoagulant positivity
Painful, progressive, non-healing ulcers with black eschar developing at sites of minor trauma/pressure and spreading to multiple areas
Therapeutic warfarin INR (2.3-3.6; 2.5 on admission) yet ongoing lesion progression suggests active thrombotic process
Skin biopsies show thrombo-occlusive material and numerous microthrombi/recanalizing venous thrombus without vasculitis
Biopsies specifically show no calciphylaxis, supporting thrombotic APS skin vasculopathy as cause

PMC6984423.diagnosis: Invasive parasellar meningioma causing bilateral internal carotid artery occlusion with MCA-territory ischemic stroke
rationale_summary: MRI shows a large solid parasellar mass with uniform strong gadolinium enhancement, cavernous sinus expansion, and sphenoid sinus/greater wing invasion-features typical of meningioma
Angiography shows a parasellar tumor blush supplied by the right internal maxillary artery (external carotid supply), supporting meningioma
Both ICAs are occluded at their origins with collateral flow via enlarged posterior communicating arteries, consistent with chronic ICA compromise related to the parasellar lesion
Patient has an MCA-territory infarct on CT with acute right hemiparesis, fitting ischemic stroke secondary to ICA occlusion
No pituitary hormonal abnormalities and no suprasellar extension, arguing against a pituitary adenoma as the primary lesion

PMC10047052.diagnosis: Secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH), likely triggered by culture-negative prosthetic valve endocarditis
rationale_summary: Fever with hepatosplenomegaly and acute hypoxemic respiratory failure
Cytopenias (Hgb 9.4 g/dL, platelets 42k) with hypofibrinogenemia (73 mg/dL) and hypertriglyceridemia (383 mg/dL)
Marked hyperferritinemia (9335 ng/mL) and very elevated soluble IL-2 receptor/CD25 (15,504 U/mL) supporting HLH
Evidence of systemic inflammatory/consumptive process (AKI, transaminitis, coagulopathy)
Potential infectious trigger present: mobile echodensity on bioprosthetic mitral valve consistent with endocarditis despite negative blood cultures

PMC11460033.diagnosis: Central diabetes insipidus due to partial empty sella syndrome
rationale_summary: Marked polydipsia/polyuria with low urine specific gravity and osmolality
Water deprivation increased serum osmolality (298 mOsm/kg) without appropriate rise in urine osmolality (210 mOsm/kg), supporting diabetes insipidus
Urine osmolality and specific gravity normalized on desmopressin (600 mOsm/kg; SG 1.020), indicating central (ADH-deficient) DI

MRI shows partial empty sella with flattened pituitary, providing a structural correlate for central DI; other secondary causes screened (βhCG/AFP, IgG4, sIL-2R) and anterior pituitary hormones were unremarkable

PMC11497508.diagnosis: Acute renal allograft rejection precipitated by immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy (nivolumab/ipilimumab) after withdrawal of immunosuppression

Infectious workup negative (blood/urine cultures, chest radiograph) and no tumor lysis evidence, supporting inflammatory rejection over infection or TLS

rationale_summary: Temporal relationship: acute febrile inflammatory illness beginning ~2-3 weeks after starting and titrating clozapine to 200 mg/day
Marked inflammatory markers and leukocyte changes (CRP up to 197 mg/L, neutrophilia, lymphopenia) with persistent tachycardia progressing to hypotension
Cardiopulmonary workup did not support myocarditis/pericarditis or pneumonia (normal troponin, unchanged ECGs, normal echocardiogram, normal chest radiograph)
Infectious evaluation unrevealing (multiple negative COVID-19 PCRs; no focal exam findings; symptoms partially self-resolving)

PMC7288532.diagnosis: Fraser syndrome (cryptophthalmos-syndactyly syndrome) with congenital high airway obstruction sequence (CHAOS) causing nonimmune hydrops
rationale_summary: Prenatal and autopsy findings of bilateral anophthalmia, cutaneous syndactyly of hands/feet, low-set ears/flat nasal bridge, and ambiguous genitalia fit Fraser syndrome spectrum
Associated renal anomaly present (right renal agenesis) and abnormal internal genital/Müllerian structures

Marked lung enlargement with cardiac compression, hyperechogenic lungs, and dilated pleural lymphatics with hydrops/ascites support congenital airway obstruction sequence as the mechanism for hydrops and stillbirth

PMC6252232.diagnosis: Spontaneous remission of myelodysplastic syndrome temporally associated with intravesical BCG immunotherapy
rationale_summary: Longstanding refractory anemia-type MDS with macrocytic anemia, low reticulocytes, marrow dysplasia with blasts <5%, and later thrombocytopenia requiring regular RBC/platelet transfusions
Bladder cancer resection reduced but did not eliminate transfusion needs, suggesting persistent marrow failure
After starting intravesical BCG, hemoglobin and platelet counts normalized (Hgb 13.7 g/dL, Plt 317,000/µL) and he became transfusion independent, indicating remission coincident with BCG exposure

PMC8133846.diagnosis: Renal cell carcinoma (right kidney) with renal vein tumor thrombus and pulmonary metastases
rationale_summary: 56-year-old man with flank pain, intermittent hematuria, weight loss, and palpable flank mass
CT shows a very large heterogeneous mass replacing the right kidney
Tumor extends into the right renal vein lumen with IVC compression (tumor thrombus)
Chest CT shows multiple nodular lesions consistent with metastases
Managed with radical nephrectomy and renal vein thrombus resection, typical for advanced RCC

PMC6259034.diagnosis: Renal artery stenosis from fibromuscular dysplasia causing bilateral renovascular hypertension with ischemic nephropathy (Pickering syndrome)
rationale_summary: Young woman with abrupt, severe, refractory hypertension
CTA: near-complete occlusion of left renal artery and significant right renal artery stenosis with otherwise normal aorta/branches, consistent with fibromuscular dysplasia
Progressive kidney size reduction and rising creatinine with episodes of malignant hypertension and flash pulmonary edema
Preserved urine output and viable renal biopsy tissue with collateral supply support potentially reversible ischemic renal failure from renovascular disease

PMC4082171.diagnosis: Synthetic cannabinoid ("K2/Spice") intoxication causing multiorgan failure (seizure, acute kidney injury, cardiomyopathy, ARDS)
rationale_summary: Young man with witnessed tonic-clonic seizure and rapid lactate normalization consistent with postictal lactic acidosis

Routine tox screen only positive for cannabinoids/benzodiazepines (lorazepam given) and negative for other common stimulants/hallucinogens, consistent with exposure to substances not detected on standard screens

Subsequent agitation/confusion, severe acute kidney injury (creatinine 1.4→4.3 mg/dL), hypoxic respiratory failure with bilateral alveolar infiltrates requiring high PEEP (ARDS), and new global LV dysfunction (EF 30%) represent acute multisystem toxicity
Clinical improvement in oxygenation and renal function after supportive care and hemodialysis fits reported severe synthetic cannabinoid toxicity with AKI and cardiopulmonary complications

PMC10688243.diagnosis: Primary endometrial gastric-type mucinous adenocarcinoma (gastric-type endometrial carcinoma)
rationale_summary: Postmenopausal bleeding with a 2 cm polypoid endometrial mass and minimal residual superficial endometrial lesion on ultrasound, without myometrial/adnexal disease
Histology shows high-grade mucinous adenocarcinoma with clear/pale eosinophilic cytoplasm, distinct borders, intra-/extra-glandular necrosis, high mitotic activity
Immunoprofile is gastrointestinal/gastric-type (diffuse CK20 and CDX2, ER/PR negative, wild-type p53, intact MMR) with only focal CK7/PAX8 supporting endometrial origin
Extensive GI workup (EGD, colonoscopy) and PET/MRI negative, arguing against metastatic gastrointestinal primary despite GI-marker expression

PMC6088471.diagnosis: IgG4-related disease (IgG4-related otitis media with effusion)

PMC10300258.diagnosis: Tumefactive demyelinating disease (acute disseminated encephalomyelitis-spectrum) with optic pathway involvement
rationale_summary: Subacute course with headache, seizure, and rapidly progressive bilateral visual loss with optic disc swelling and absent VEPs suggests inflammatory/demyelinating optic pathway involvement

MRI shows large bilateral T2/FLAIR hyperintense lesions involving temporal/insular regions with extension into corpus callosum, deep gray nuclei, periventricular white matter, and optic chiasm, with mild expansion but no enhancement, no diffusion restriction, and no hemorrhage-pattern compatible with demyelination rather than HSV encephalitis or infarct
CSF has markedly elevated opening pressure but otherwise normal counts/chemistry and HSV PCR is negative, arguing against infectious encephalitis

PMC11388267.diagnosis: Intermittent ileo-ileal intussusception due to a terminal ileal intramural lipoma (lead point)
rationale_summary: Acute severe cramping periumbilical/right iliac pain with vomiting and abdominal radiograph showing small-bowel distension and absent ileocecal gas suggests intermittent small-bowel obstruction
CT angiography identified an intramural lipoma in the terminal ileum, a typical lead point for adult intussusception and can be missed as obstruction if transient
Colonoscopy could not be advanced beyond the terminal ileum, supporting a mechanical process at/near the terminal ileum despite otherwise normal endoscopy and labs

PMC5625581.diagnosis: Metastatic pulmonary sarcomatoid carcinoma (spindle/pleomorphic carcinoma of lung) with skull and chest-wall metastases
rationale_summary: CT chest shows a heterogeneously enhancing right lower-lobe lung mass with vascular invasion plus multiple bilateral spiculated pulmonary nodules, supporting a primary lung malignancy with metastases
Destructive skull (frontal bone) mass compressing frontal lobe and a rib-destructive pleura-based chest-wall mass indicate aggressive metastatic disease

FNAC from both forehead and chest-wall lesions shows pleomorphic hyperchromatic/bizarre tumor cells with pink matrix material, consistent with a poorly differentiated sarcomatoid-type carcinoma rather than a benign recurrent forehead lesion

PMC3130996.diagnosis: Herpes simplex virus type 2-associated lumbosacral radiculitis (Elsberg syndrome) with post-herpetic neuralgia
rationale_summary: Acute bilateral vulval blisters diagnosed as genital herpes with rapid response to valacyclovir
Subsequent persistent burning pain and marked hyperaesthesia in lumbosacral dermatomes (S1-S3), consistent with radicular neuropathic pain after HSV infection
No motor deficit and no recurrent ulcers during prolonged symptoms, fitting post-herpetic neuralgia/radiculitis rather than ongoing mucocutaneous disease
Recurrence of vulval blisters followed by recurrence of identical dermatomal hyperaesthesia supports HSV-triggered radiculitis
Neuropathic pain resolved with amitriptyline on both episodes

PMC11146635.diagnosis: Pituitary stalk germinoma (suprasellar germ cell tumor) causing panhypopituitarism and central diabetes insipidus
rationale_summary: Adolescent with new central diabetes insipidus (very low urine osmolality, 5-6 L/day intake/output) plus multiple anterior pituitary deficits: central adrenal insufficiency, central hypothyroidism, hypogonadotropic hypogonadism (amenorrhea, undetectable estradiol), and hyperprolactinemia (stalk effect).
MRI shows a solid enhancing sellar-suprasellar mass with pituitary stalk thickening and loss of posterior pituitary bright spot, a classic pattern for suprasellar germinoma.
CSF normal and no sinus disease on CT make infectious/inflammatory sinus-related causes less likely; presentation and imaging fit a germ cell tumor involving the stalk/suprasellar region.

PMC7802700.diagnosis: Lemierre syndrome (Fusobacterium necrophorum septic thrombophlebitis following oropharyngeal infection) with sepsis-associated myocarditis
rationale_summary: Acute febrile illness with painful unilateral neck swelling and posterior palate erythema suggests recent oropharyngeal infection with cervical involvement
CT neck shows subcutaneous edema and multiple enlarged cervical lymph nodes consistent with deep neck infection/complication
Marked inflammatory markers (CRP 230→344 mg/L, procalcitonin 2.1→12.9 µg/L) with relatively normal WBC supports severe bacterial sepsis pattern seen in Lemierre syndrome
New cardiac involvement over 24-48 h (troponin T 90 ng/L, NT-proBNP elevated, LVEF drop from normal to 40%) consistent with sepsis-related myocarditis in this setting

PMC4109228.diagnosis: Rapidly destructive hip arthropathy due to calcium pyrophosphate deposition disease (CPPD)
rationale_summary: Progressive atraumatic hip/groin pain with radiographic progression over 5 months to complete femoral head destruction, acetabular erosion, and subluxation
Hip MRI shows extensive synovial hypertrophy and capsular enlargement with minimal marrow edema, supporting aggressive inflammatory arthropathy rather than osteonecrosis

PMC10810688.diagnosis: X-linked hypophosphatemia (FGF23-mediated renal phosphate wasting) misdiagnosed as ankylosing spondylitis
rationale_summary: Chronic enthesopathy/enthesophytes and spinal ligament calcification with sacroiliac fusion can mimic ankylosing spondylitis, but he had no benefit from NSAIDs and failed adalimumab and is HLA-B27 negative
Persistent hypophosphatemia (phosphate 1.9 mg/dL) with elevated alkaline phosphatase and secondary hyperparathyroidism (PTH 115 pg/mL) suggests osteomalacia from phosphate wasting
24-hour urine phosphate 1300 mg is inappropriately normal (not suppressed) for hypophosphatemia, indicating renal phosphate wasting
Atypical skeletal features (bowed femurs, subtrochanteric insufficiency fractures) plus lifelong hearing loss and severe dental disease support a hereditary hypophosphatemic disorder such as XLH

PMC4010020.diagnosis: Late-onset urea cycle disorder (ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency) causing noncirrhotic hyperammonemic encephalopathy
rationale_summary: Severe hyperammonemia with encephalopathy (ammonia 202→516 µmol/L) and EEG triphasic waves
No evidence of cirrhosis or significant hepatic failure: near-normal AST/ALT, bilirubin, INR; abdominal CT shows fatty liver without cirrhosis
Routine toxicology and neuroimaging unrevealing, making primary hepatic/toxic/structural CNS causes less likely
Progressive fatigue then coma over ~2 weeks with refractory hyperammonemia despite lactulose suggests impaired ammonia detoxification consistent with a urea cycle defect in an adult

PMC4857264.diagnosis: Autoimmune myelofibrosis secondary to systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE)
rationale_summary: Transfusion-dependent severe anemia with bone marrow showing hypercellularity with panhyperplasia and moderate reticulin fibrosis (grade 2) but without atypical/dysplastic megakaryocytes or other typical primary myelofibrosis features
JAK2 (V617F/exon 12) and MPL mutation testing negative, arguing against a myeloproliferative neoplasm-associated myelofibrosis
Clinical context suggests connective tissue disease: family history of SLE plus photosensitivity and arthralgias
Anemia showed waxing/waning course and steroid responsiveness (dexamethasone led to resolution), supporting an autoimmune etiology rather than clonal PMF
No evidence of hemolysis (negative Coombs, normal reticulocyte count and haptoglobin), pointing away from autoimmune hemolytic anemia as the primary cause and toward marrow-based autoimmune process

PMC6263045.diagnosis: Acute infectious purpura fulminans due to acquired protein S deficiency (post-viral)
rationale_summary: Fever with retiform violaceous patches and true purpura on lower extremities with toe duskiness suggests a thrombotic/ischemic purpura rather than simple vasculitis
Skin biopsy showed ischemia, vascular congestion, purpura, and eccrine gland necrosis without vasculitis; direct immunofluorescence was negative, supporting a noninflammatory occlusive process

Extensive autoimmune/vasculitis and antiphospholipid work-up (cryoglobulins, lupus anticoagulant, cardiolipin, beta-2-glycoprotein, complements) was unremarkable, favoring purpura fulminans from a coagulation inhibitor deficiency

PMC8780476.diagnosis: COVID-19-associated autonomic dysfunction causing neurocardiogenic (vasovagal) syncope with sinus node dysfunction
rationale_summary: Recurrent syncope triggered by moving from supine to sitting with initial sinus tachycardia and preserved blood pressure followed over minutes by progressive sinus bradycardia leading to presyncope/syncope, consistent with reflex/autonomic-mediated syncope rather than primary orthostatic hypotension
Holter showed sinus pauses (up to 3.4 s) and bradyarrhythmias without AV block or PR prolongation, supporting sinus node/autonomic involvement

Acute SARS-CoV-2 infection with fever, hypoxemia, CT ground-glass opacities, and inflammatory markers; normal troponin and normal echocardiography argue against myocarditis/structural heart disease as the primary cause
CSF/lumbar puncture unremarkable, making alternative central neurologic causes less likely while still compatible with COVID-related dysautonomia

36-year-old with 3-year progressive painful enlarging posterior thoracic mass and evolving myelopathy (bilateral weakness, sensory level below xiphoid, Babinski/Romberg, constipation).

Imaging shows a large expansile sclerotic lesion centered in right posterolateral T5-T6 with a chondroid matrix and calcifications, extending to transverse process/costovertebral junction and ribs 5-6 with soft-tissue component.
MRI demonstrates aggressive local extension (T4-T8), necrosis, spinal canal stenosis, and intradural/intramedullary involvement, supporting malignant cartilaginous tumor over benign osteochondroma.

10-year continuous vismodegib exposure with progressive chronic watery diarrhea and negative infectious workup

PMC6172599.diagnosis: Radiation-induced leukoencephalopathy with giant Virchow-Robin (perivascular) spaces
rationale_summary: Remote history of high-dose cranial radiotherapy (6000 cGy) in childhood for right parieto-occipital astrocytoma
Prior MRI already documented giant Virchow-Robin spaces attributed to radiotherapy, and current CT/MRI shows multiple similar periventricular/posterior subcortical cystic lesions
MRI shows surrounding nonspecific T2 hyperintensity without post-contrast enhancement, supporting a non-neoplastic, non-inflammatory post-radiation process
New focal deficit (dense left homonymous hemianopia) localizes to previously treated right parieto-occipital region, consistent with delayed radiation-related injury

Progressive 1.5-year asymmetric limb weakness (left > right) involving multiple myotomes with no sensory loss
Upper motor neuron signs: diffuse hyperreflexia and bilateral positive Hoffman signs
Lower motor neuron features noted: fasciculations and weakness
Cervical MRI shows only foraminal stenosis without central canal stenosis, not explaining diffuse UMN signs and bilateral multilevel weakness
No improvement after carpal tunnel release despite prior CTS on EMG/NCS, suggesting an alternative neurodegenerative process

PMC11925369.diagnosis: Terrien's marginal degeneration (inflammatory variant) associated with juvenile idiopathic arthritis
rationale_summary: Bilateral circumferential peripheral corneal thinning with subepithelial stromal infiltrates/scarring and an overhanging epithelial edge, without fluorescein staining, vascularization, scleral involvement, or uveitis
AS-OCT shows peripheral epithelial thinning over focal stromal hyperreflectivity with sharp demarcation, consistent with marginal corneal degeneration

Chronic migratory inflammatory arthralgia with positive ANA and elevated ESR (RF/anti-CCP negative) supports an associated juvenile idiopathic arthritis/systemic inflammatory condition rather than infection or trauma

PMC9623980.diagnosis: Thoracic lymphatic malformation (cystic hygroma/lymphangioma) presenting as a large multiloculated extrapulmonary cystic mass
rationale_summary: 16-month-old with acute respiratory distress and reduced right chest expansion with dullness and markedly diminished air entry, suggesting a space-occupying pleural/extrapulmonary process
Chest radiograph shows a well-demarcated right mid/lower hemithorax opacity with mediastinal shift, atypical for simple pneumonia/effusion
Ultrasound interpreted as septate pleural effusion, consistent with a multiloculated cystic lesion
CT shows a large multiloculated extrapulmonary cystic lesion compressing the right lung and shifting mediastinum, with otherwise normal lung parenchyma
Minimal inflammatory features (afebrile, low CRP) argue against parapneumonic effusion/empyema and support a congenital cystic lesion such as lymphatic malformation

PMC6495497.diagnosis: Miller Fisher syndrome (Guillain-Barré syndrome variant) triggered by acute dengue infection
rationale_summary: Acute febrile illness with confirmed dengue (NS1 antigen and RT-PCR positive)
Classic triad: ophthalmoplegia (complete bilateral external), ataxia (broad-based gait, dysmetria), and areflexia (absent tendon reflexes throughout)
Cranial nerve involvement with bulbar features (ptosis, palatal weakness, dysphagia, tongue deviation) without limb weakness or sensory loss
Normal brain CT/MRI supports peripheral neuropathic process; nerve conduction shows mild axonal polyneuropathy and no decrement on repetitive stimulation (argues against myasthenia)

PMC9748917.diagnosis: Bickerstaff brainstem encephalitis (anti-GQ1b antibody syndrome) following herpes zoster
rationale_summary: Subacute hypersomnolence with fluctuating confusion plus brainstem signs (bilateral upward gaze palsy, horizontal nystagmus)
Symmetric lower-limb weakness with extensor plantar responses suggests central involvement rather than peripheral neuropathy alone
CSF without pleocytosis and normal brain/spine MRI do not exclude anti-GQ1b spectrum disease

PMC10460928.diagnosis: Adult-onset Still's disease complicated by macrophage activation syndrome (secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis)
rationale_summary: Daily high-grade fevers with inflammatory arthritis of wrists/PIP joints and diffuse pruritic maculopapular rash
Marked hyperinflammation with ferritin >15,000 ng/mL and very elevated soluble IL-2 receptor and IL-18
Cytopenias (anemia, thrombocytopenia) with coagulopathy/hypofibrinogenemia (fibrinogen 81) and extreme D-dimer consistent with MAS/HLH physiology
Hepatic injury/cholestasis (AST 535, alk phos 986, bilirubin 2.1) seen in AOSD/MAS
Negative infectious workup (cultures, viral testing, TEE) and negative ANA/ANCA/RF supporting a seronegative autoinflammatory etiology

PMC7053453.diagnosis: Gradenigo syndrome complicated by septic cavernous sinus thrombosis and infectious internal carotid arteritis (from acute otitis media with petrous apicitis)
rationale_summary: Suppurative acute otitis media with CT/MRI evidence of mastoiditis and petrous apicitis

Marked neutrophilic leukocytosis and elevated CRP consistent with severe bacterial otogenic infection

PMC6942755.diagnosis: Post-traumatic trigeminal neuropathic pain (infraorbital nerve injury) after immediate maxillary implant placement

PMC10187858.diagnosis: Pancreatic lymphatic malformation (cystic lymphangioma) causing recurrent pancreatitis and portal hypertension
rationale_summary: Toddler with recurrent acute pancreatitis and a persistent/enlarging 3-cm cystic lesion in the pancreatic head connected to the main pancreatic duct with distorted ductal anatomy
Lesion shows no internal enhancement and the rest of the pancreas is normal, arguing against solid tumor and favoring a benign cystic malformation

Progressive enlargement leads to portal vein compression with gastric varices and splenomegaly, consistent with a locally infiltrative cystic lymphatic lesion rather than a transient necrotic collection/pseudocyst

PMC4035763.diagnosis: Metformin-associated lactic acidosis (MALA) precipitated by acute kidney injury
rationale_summary: On high-dose metformin (3 g/day) with severe acute renal failure (creatinine 768 µmol/L, urea 30.8 mmol/L)
Profound high-anion gap metabolic acidosis (pH 6.58, HCO3 1-3.6 mmol/L, anion gap 55) with marked hyperlactatemia (lactate 16.7 mmol/L)
Hypoglycemia and shock without evidence of another cause on initial workup; exploratory laparotomy negative for ischemic bowel
Required renal replacement therapy (CVVHDF), consistent with toxin/renal-clearance-related lactic acidosis

PMC6304919.diagnosis: Posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES) complicated by right occipital intracerebral hemorrhage
rationale_summary: Acute severe hypertension (up to 190/110 mmHg) followed by sudden headache, confusion, and bilateral cortical vision loss
Neuro exam otherwise nonfocal, consistent with posterior circulation/cortical involvement

PMC3414798.diagnosis: Juvenile granulosa cell tumor of the ovary
rationale_summary: 4-year-old with isosexual precocious puberty (breast development, vaginal spotting) and suppressed LH/FSH with no LHRH response indicates gonadotropin-independent estrogen source
Large unilateral right ovarian mass with enlarged estrogenized uterus/endometrium supports an estrogen-secreting ovarian tumor

Elevated estradiol with associated androgen/progesterone elevations can occur with juvenile granulosa cell tumors in young girls

PMC3080319.diagnosis: IgG4-related sialadenitis (chronic sclerosing sialadenitis/Küttner tumor) of the submandibular gland
rationale_summary: Painless, elastic-hard enlargement of the submandibular gland with poor salivary flow
Imaging shows a homogeneously enhancing submandibular mass with apparent invasive extension and associated enlarged nodes, mimicking malignancy
PET uptake confined to the submandibular region without other lesions
Lymph node excision shows reactive follicular hyperplasia with polyclonal B-cells, arguing against lymphoma/metastasis
Serum IgG4 is within normal range, which does not exclude IgG4-related disease in localized salivary gland involvement

PMC8441214.diagnosis: Junctional ectopic rhythm (accelerated junctional rhythm) with retrograde ventriculoatrial conduction causing loss of CRT pacing
rationale_summary: Regular wide-QRS rhythm at ~90 bpm with no obvious P waves on ECG
Device showed ventricular sensing at V-V 700 ms while a separate regular right atrial rhythm was present, indicating the ventricular rhythm was faster than the atrial rhythm (AV dissociation)
Rate was below VT detection zone (>150 bpm), consistent with a non-tachyarrhythmic ectopic rhythm
Wide QRS with RBBB pattern and left axis deviation is compatible with ventricular activation from the AV junction/His-Purkinje system rather than CRT pacing

PMC4971312.diagnosis: Granular cell tumor (Abrikossoff tumor) of the tongue

Often clinically mistaken for a benign fibrous lesion, prompting excisional biopsy for diagnosis

PMC10311712.diagnosis: Hepatic involvement by recurrent nodular lymphocyte-predominant Hodgkin lymphoma (lymphomatous hepatic infiltration causing acute liver failure)
rationale_summary: Acute severe hepatocellular injury with jaundice, INR 1.9 and hypoglycemia consistent with acute liver failure physiology
Extensive negative evaluation for viral hepatitis, autoimmune hepatitis (negative ANA/SMA/AMA/LKM), and metabolic causes (normal A1AT, ceruloplasmin/copper)
Imaging shows heterogeneous hepatic enhancement with splenomegaly, splenic lesions, and multiple enlarged upper abdominal lymph nodes, supporting lymphoproliferative relapse with hepatic involvement
History of prior Hodgkin lymphoma increases likelihood of recurrent lymphoma presenting with hepatic infiltration

PMC6141727.diagnosis: Acrodermatitis enteropathica-like eruption due to niacin (vitamin B3) deficiency (pellagra)
rationale_summary: Severe burning oral and esophageal mucositis with crusted cheilitis and weight loss, plus vulvar/anal involvement
Hyperkeratotic follicular plaques with comedones/papulopustules and well-demarcated erythematous palmoplantar plaques
Infectious (antifungals ineffective), autoimmune blistering/paraneoplastic pemphigus (negative DIF/IIF and autoantibodies), and glucagonoma (normal glucagon and pancreatic imaging) were excluded
No B12/iron deficiency and normal zinc argue against other nutritional causes, supporting niacin deficiency presenting with AE-like dermatitis and mucositis

PMC3371498.diagnosis: Post-dural puncture intracranial hypotension (CSF leak) causing postpartum seizures
rationale_summary: Difficult lumbar epidural followed by classic postural headache starting postoperative day 1 (worse upright, relieved supine)
Re-presentation day 4 with severe postural headache and new generalized tonic-clonic seizures
CT head and MRI/MR venography reported normal, arguing against hemorrhage or cerebral venous sinus thrombosis
No hypertension, proteinuria, or end-organ dysfunction; magnesium stopped after recovery, making eclampsia unlikely
Lumbar puncture showed clear CSF with elevated protein and no cells, supporting a noninfectious process consistent with CSF leak/intracranial hypotension

PMC10557406.diagnosis: Heyde syndrome (aortic stenosis-associated angiodysplasia with acquired von Willebrand factor deficiency)

Bleeding persisted despite warfarin reversal, suggesting an additional bleeding diathesis beyond supratherapeutic INR

Pattern is consistent with angiodysplasia-related GI bleeding with systemic mucosal bleeding tendency seen in acquired von Willebrand factor deficiency associated with aortic stenosis (Heyde syndrome)

PMC9096471.diagnosis: Transient perivascular inflammation of the carotid artery (TIPIC syndrome, formerly carotidynia)
rationale_summary: Acute unilateral pain and focal tenderness/swelling over the carotid bifurcation radiating to the ipsilateral ear, triggered by neck movement
Minimal systemic inflammation (near-normal CRP, mild ESR elevation) and no neurologic deficits
Ultrasound shows eccentric hypoechoic wall/perivascular thickening at distal CCA/proximal ICA without plaque, stenosis, or abnormal Doppler flow
MRI shows pericarotid rim hyperintensity on STIR with diffusion restriction and marked post-contrast enhancement, without significant luminal narrowing-typical of TIPIC

PMC9898808.diagnosis: Bilateral acute iris transillumination (BAIT) syndrome with secondary pigmentary glaucoma (postinfectious)
rationale_summary: Acute bilateral onset after presumed conjunctivitis treated with topical moxifloxacin/steroid
Severe anterior chamber pigment dispersion with diffuse iris transillumination defects and mydriatic atonic pupils in both eyes
No keratic precipitates, no anterior chamber cells, normal corneal sensation, and fluorescein angiography without inflammation (argues against uveitis)
Wide-open angles with heavy trabecular meshwork pigmentation and markedly elevated IOP despite therapy, consistent with pigment-related secondary glaucoma

Development of distal ischemia (cool/cyanotic fingertips) with angiographic occlusion of ulnar/palmar/digital arteries indicates systemic medium/small-vessel vasculitis

PMC11802228.diagnosis: Herpes zoster ophthalmicus-associated ocular motor neuropathy (zoster-related multiple cranial neuropathy causing partial ophthalmoplegia)
rationale_summary: Recent HZO with Hutchinson sign and VZV DNA detected in CSF, followed by VZV meningitis
Abrupt onset diplopia with right abduction restriction and mild adduction/elevation limitation without pupillary involvement, consistent with ocular motor cranial neuropathy
MRI showed no orbital apex lesion, no cranial nerve enhancement, no extraocular muscle swelling, and no brainstem abnormalities, arguing against compressive/inflammatory mass or myositis
Repeat CSF: normal cell count, elevated protein, VZV PCR negative, supporting post-infectious/immune-mediated zoster neuropathy rather than active viral replication
Blink reflex abnormalities (absent right R1, prolonged bilateral R2) indicate trigeminal/facial brainstem reflex pathway involvement consistent with zoster-related cranial neuropathy

PMC10445075.diagnosis: Drug-induced liver injury (DILI) from herbal "green juice" (watercress/garlic/ginger) causing acute mixed hepatocellular-cholestatic hepatitis
rationale_summary: Acute symptomatic hepatitis with jaundice and dark urine plus very high AST/ALT (3159/2500) and elevated alkaline phosphatase (714) consistent with mixed liver injury
Extensive infectious (HAV/HBV/HCV/HEV, CMV/EBV/HSV, HIV) and autoimmune serologies (ANA, SMA, AMA, LKM-1, SLA) negative
Imaging (US Doppler, MRCP) shows no biliary obstruction or vascular cause
Liver biopsy shows active hepatitis with confluent necrosis and predominantly lymphocytic infiltrate with some eosinophils, supporting toxic/drug-related hepatitis
Temporal exposure to new herbal intake ("green juice" with watercress/garlic/ginger) shortly before presentation and no alcohol/other supplements reported

PMC5051304.diagnosis: Gingival extranodal marginal zone B-cell lymphoma (MALT lymphoma)
rationale_summary: Progressive, generalized firm gingival enlargement over 4 months without bleeding or purulence, making inflammatory enlargement unlikely
Bilateral tender submandibular lymphadenopathy suggesting a lymphoproliferative process
Panoramic radiograph shows severe localized bone resorption with 'floating teeth' appearance and generalized alveolar bone loss, inconsistent with simple drug-induced/idiopathic enlargement
Elevated ESR with otherwise normal blood counts/biochemistry supports chronic inflammatory/neoplastic etiology prompting biopsy

PMC8638666.diagnosis: Cutaneous mast cell sarcoma (localized, KIT-wild type)
rationale_summary: Solitary yellowish plaque with dermal sheets of mast cells (Giemsa metachromatic granules) expressing CD117 and tryptase
Marked cytologic atypia and progression on excision with high mitotic rate (up to 9/mm2) and elevated Ki-67 (29.5%), exceeding typical mastocytoma
Aberrant immunophenotype with heterogeneous CD30 and focal CD25 positivity supports neoplastic/aggressive mast cell tumor
Normal serum tryptase and no clinical/imaging evidence of systemic involvement indicate a localized cutaneous process

PMC6258140.diagnosis: Bilateral ocular ischemic syndrome from chronic carotid/ophthalmic artery hypoperfusion (occlusive retinal vasculopathy)

PMC10302296.diagnosis: Cranial mediastinal abscess (septic mediastinitis) causing right-sided cardiac compression with pleural septic exudate
rationale_summary: Thoracic ultrasound showed a large echogenic mass in the right thorax severely compressing the right heart with adjacent pleural fluid; pericardium/valves appeared normal
Clinical signs of right-sided venous congestion (dewlap edema, bilateral jugular distension, positive jugular compression) consistent with extrinsic right heart compression
Pleural fluid was a septic exudate, supporting an infectious intrathoracic process rather than primary cardiac disease
Systemic inflammatory changes (neutrophilia, lymphopenia, hyperproteinemia with hypoalbuminemia) and persistent illness despite amoxicillin/dexamethasone

PMC7883144.diagnosis: Nitrofurantoin-induced acute hypersensitivity reaction (systemic inflammatory response/sepsis-like reaction)
rationale_summary: Twice developed abrupt fever, chills, myalgias/weakness, pleuritic chest pain, tachycardia and marked hypotension within <24 hours of starting nitrofurantoin
Rechallenge reproduced a more severe, similar reaction after a 2-month interval, supporting drug causality
Leukocytosis with neutrophilia/bandemia accompanied both episodes; lactate was not elevated and symptoms resolved rapidly after stopping nitrofurantoin
Blood/urine cultures were obtained and she improved quickly without evidence provided of a confirmed infection; no rash/urticaria despite systemic reaction

PMC4632472.diagnosis: Struma ovarii (functioning ovarian teratoma causing thyrotoxicosis)
rationale_summary: Recurrent thyrotoxicosis shortly after total thyroidectomy with elevated free T4 and suppressed TSH
Very low radioiodine uptake in the thyroid bed (0.2%) argues against residual thyroid tissue as the source
I-131 whole-body scan shows intense pelvic uptake with star artifact, indicating ectopic iodine-avid thyroid tissue
SPECT/CT localizes uptake to a large right adnexal mixed cystic-solid mass with calcification, consistent with ovarian teratoma containing thyroid tissue

PMC2740172.diagnosis: Intravenous leiomyomatosis with intracardiac extension (right atrium/right ventricle) from uterine leiomyoma
rationale_summary: History of uterine fibroids with prior hysterectomy
CT shows a hypodense filling defect extending from inferior vena cava into right atrium/right ventricle, suggesting a contiguous intravascular mass rather than isolated PE
Pelvic CT shows a lobulated pelvic mass inseparable from the internal iliac vein, consistent with venous origin/extension
Echocardiography confirms a large tissue-density mass occupying the right atrium to the tricuspid annulus
No pulmonary embolism on chest CT despite shock/cyanosis, supporting obstructive intracardiac/intravascular tumor thrombus

PMC6186763.diagnosis: Guillain-Barré syndrome (acute inflammatory demyelinating polyradiculoneuropathy) with concurrent posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES)
rationale_summary: Rapidly progressive ascending weakness with bulbar and respiratory involvement leading to quadriplegia and ventilatory failure, with areflexia
CSF shows albuminocytologic dissociation (protein 92 mg/dL with 8 cells/mm³) and negative infectious studies/antiganglioside antibodies, supporting GBS
Brain MRI shows bilateral parietooccipital cortical/subcortical T2/FLAIR hyperintensities consistent with PRES; MRA negative for vasospasm/aneurysm

PMC6287356.diagnosis: Diabetic ketoacidosis due to new-onset autoimmune type 1 diabetes mellitus (SLE-associated)
rationale_summary: Severe hyperglycemia (glucose 1240 mg/dL) with high anion gap metabolic acidosis (pH 7.07, HCO3 3.0 mmol/L, anion gap 43.56) and ketonuria (80 mg/dL) consistent with DKA
Symptoms of polydipsia, nausea/vomiting, abdominal pain, dehydration, tachypnea and drowsiness support DKA presentation
Normal BMI and absence of insulin-resistance stigmata favor insulin-deficient (type 1/autoimmune) diabetes rather than type 2
Occurs in context of systemic autoimmune disease (SLE) and recent immunomodulatory therapy, compatible with autoimmune diabetes onset/precipitation

PMC10336356.diagnosis: Diffuse hepatic metastases from melanoma with cystic/necrotic transformation (pseudocystic liver metastases)
rationale_summary: History of recurrent stage IIIc cutaneous melanoma with new inguinal lymphadenopathy shortly before presentation
Rapid interval increase in number and size of innumerable hepatic lesions over weeks with new marked cholestatic jaundice and mixed hepatocellular injury
MRCP shows mildly complex cystic lesions with diffusion restriction and internal fluid-fluid levels (suggesting necrotic/tumoral content rather than simple cysts)
Lack of postcontrast enhancement and T2-bright/T1-dark appearance consistent with cystic/necrotic metastases rather than solid tumors or biliary obstruction

PMC5956711.diagnosis: CACNA2D4-associated cone-rod dystrophy with electronegative ERG (progressive cone dysfunction with early rod involvement)
rationale_summary: Early-onset nystagmus and abnormal visual behavior with stable fundus/OCT structure but foveal hypoplasia
Full-field ERG shows rod responses more attenuated than cone responses with electronegative mixed rod-cone response (reduced b-to-a ratio) and normal implicit times
Progressive decline in cone ERG amplitudes (20-30%/year) with stable rod responses over follow-up, consistent with progressive cone-rod dystrophy rather than stationary night blindness
Family history suggests inherited night blindness/retinal dysfunction; TRPM1 testing for CSNB was negative, supporting an alternative genetic cause such as CACNA2D4

PMC7792557.diagnosis: COVID-19-associated thrombotic microangiopathy (complement-mediated/aHUS-like) causing acute kidney injury
rationale_summary: Severe AKI with persistent proteinuria and microscopic hematuria without obstruction or autoimmune serologies
Microangiopathic hemolytic anemia: Coombs-negative hemolysis with schistocytes, high LDH, undetectable haptoglobin, transfusion dependence
ADAMTS13 activity unremarkable, arguing against TTP
Systemic inflammatory state in severe SARS-CoV-2 infection (ARDS, very high ferritin/CRP) consistent with COVID-triggered TMA

PMC6878082.diagnosis: Recurrent malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor (MPNST)
rationale_summary: History of MPNST in the same limb with prior recurrence and radiotherapy, now with a rapidly growing nodule on the surgical scar
Histology shows atypical spindled/epithelioid cells in myxoid stroma with very high mitotic activity (PHH3 >30/HPF) and high Ki-67, consistent with high-grade sarcoma
Focal S-100 positivity supports peripheral nerve sheath differentiation
MRI shows a large dumbbell-shaped soft-tissue mass displacing the ulnar nerve, compatible with a nerve-associated sarcoma recurrence

PMC10125835.diagnosis: Idiopathic granulomatous mastitis (chronic noninfectious granulomatous mastitis with sterile abscesses/fistula)
rationale_summary: Core-needle biopsy showed granulomatous mastitis after mammography concerning for malignancy
Chronic course (>1 year) with recurrent complex abscesses and a fistulous tract on ultrasound
Repeated aspirations and intraoperative cultures were negative, including despite broad antibiotic exposure
Minimal/transient response to antibiotics and only transient relief with prednisone, supporting a noninfectious inflammatory process

PMC7806373.diagnosis: Syndrome of inappropriate antidiuretic hormone secretion (SIADH) due to adrenal insufficiency from hypopituitarism
rationale_summary: Severe hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 117 mMol/L, serum osmolality 245 mOsm/kg) with inappropriately concentrated urine (urine osmolality 309 mOsm/kg) and high urine sodium (68 mEq/L) in a clinically euvolemic patient supports SIADH physiology
Chest CT ruled out lung malignancy as an ectopic ADH source, prompting consideration of non-malignant causes
Feminine facial features and marked short stature suggest longstanding pituitary/hormonal disorder, consistent with secondary adrenal insufficiency causing SIADH-like hyponatremia

PMC10713186.diagnosis: Hepatic sarcoidosis (granulomatous sarcoidosis presenting as multiple hepatic lesions)
rationale_summary: Multiple new hypodense hepatic lesions on CT/MRI initially mimicking metastases with negative malignancy workup (AFP, CA-125, CA 19-9, CEA; colonoscopy negative)
Systemic inflammatory presentation with 3 weeks of high fevers, marked leukocytosis, elevated ESR/CRP
Cholestatic-pattern liver enzyme abnormality (isolated alkaline phosphatase elevation with normal AST/ALT/bilirubin) consistent with hepatic granulomatous disease
Infectious evaluation negative and leukocytosis resolved without antibiotics (negative blood/urine cultures; negative hepatitis B/C and QuantiFERON-TB; no endocarditis on echocardiogram)
History of pulmonary nodule (previously biopsied nondiagnostic) compatible with multisystem sarcoidosis involvement

PMC11469885.diagnosis: Secondary syphilis with syphilitic hepatitis and nephrotic-range proteinuria (syphilitic nephropathy)
rationale_summary: Diffuse papular rash involving palms and soles with high-risk unprotected sex history
Cholestatic jaundice symptoms (tea-colored urine, pale stools, pruritus) with elevated bilirubin and alkaline phosphatase
Nephrotic-range proteinuria (UPCR 6,667 mg/g; 24-hour protein 13.3 g) with preserved creatinine, consistent with syphilis-associated nephropathy
Negative hepatitis A/B/C serologies and no medication/supplement exposure; positive AMA/ASMA can be false-positive in infections, prompting repeat testing and systemic infectious workup

PMC8564683.diagnosis: Caseous calcification of the mitral annulus (caseous mitral annular calcification)
rationale_summary: Echocardiography showed an echo-lucent, partially calcified mass at the posterolateral/posterior mitral annulus without valve dysfunction

CMR showed a heterogeneous peri-mitral mass with no first-pass perfusion and only peripheral late gadolinium enhancement, consistent with avascular central caseous material with a fibrous/calcified rim
Transesophageal echocardiography excluded vegetations and there were no clinical signs of infection, supporting a noninfectious degenerative annular process in an elderly patient

PMC7040145.diagnosis: Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) of the left breast, triple-negative
rationale_summary: Screening mammography showed clustered pleomorphic calcifications and imaging revealed a small irregular enhancing breast lesion with focal PET uptake
Core-needle biopsy demonstrated ductal carcinoma in situ, nuclear grade 2
Immunohistochemistry was ER-, PgR-, HER2- with elevated Ki-67 (32.4%), supporting triple-negative DCIS
No palpable mass, nipple discharge, abnormal tumor markers, or metastatic uptake elsewhere; sentinel node biopsy planned with mastectomy

PMC11785469.diagnosis: Spinal metastasis from follicular thyroid carcinoma causing thoracic cord compression
rationale_summary: Progressive thoracic myelopathy with T4 sensory level and sphincter dysfunction indicates high-grade thoracic cord compression
Imaging shows expansile osteolytic collapse of T2-T3 with residual trabeculae, sclerotic rim, kyphosis, and enhancing vertebral/posterior element lesion consistent with destructive tumor

Palpable solitary thyroid nodule with ultrasound confirmation raises thyroid malignancy as a primary source; other systemic workup (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis, tumor markers) did not identify an alternative primary
Biopsies were obtained from both the thoracic lesion and thyroid nodule to evaluate metastatic thyroid carcinoma

PMC7642761.diagnosis: Disseminated cystic echinococcosis (hydatid disease) with musculoskeletal, spinal canal, pulmonary, and osseous involvement
rationale_summary: Multiloculated multiseptated cystic lesions with echogenic fluid in bilateral psoas muscles extending into subcutaneous tissues
CT shows cystic masses with wall/septal calcifications and no fat, compatible with hydatid cysts
Multiple sharply marginated osteolytic lesions in spine (lumbar/sacral and thoracic) consistent with osseous echinococcosis
MRI shows T1 low/T2 high multiloculated cysts with peripheral/septal enhancement and no restricted diffusion, supporting parasitic cysts rather than abscess/tumor
Extension into spinal canal/foramina with cauda equina compression fits invasive spinal hydatid disease
Chest CT with interstitial thickening and nodules supports disseminated involvement including lungs

Large posterior thigh multiloculated cystic mass with fluid-fluid levels and thick fibrous rim, present and slowly enlarging for 12 years, then acutely painful with hemorrhagic expansion
Sciatic nerve seen proximal and distal to the lesion but not at the level of the mass, suggesting a nerve-sheath origin

Repeated aspiration and operative evacuations yielded large volumes of bloody fluid with rapid reaccumulation and diffuse capillary oozing, inconsistent with a simple chronic hematoma in a patient without trauma or anticoagulation
Peripheral solid hypertrophic tissue within a rind-like capsule suggests a long-standing tumor with cystic/hemorrhagic degeneration rather than a purely fluid collection

PMC8167300.diagnosis: Multisystem inflammatory syndrome in children (MIS-C) associated with prior SARS-CoV-2 infection
rationale_summary: Persistent high fever with elevated inflammatory markers (CRP 11 mg/dL, ESR 26 mm/hr)

Multisystem involvement: gastrointestinal symptoms/abdominal pain and vomiting with CT showing mesenteric edema and diffuse fat stranding; mucocutaneous findings (bilateral conjunctival injection); systemic symptoms (headache, myalgias, cough)
Evidence of recent SARS-CoV-2 infection: positive PCR one month earlier and positive SARS-CoV-2 IgG
Cytopenias supportive of MIS-C (platelets 102×10^3/µL) and presentation felt not consistent with acute appendicitis despite mildly dilated appendix

PMC3974450.diagnosis: Juvenile ossifying fibroma (psammomatoid type) involving the jaws
rationale_summary: 6-year-old with rapidly developing, firm bilateral jaw expansion and large well-demarcated expansile lesion on panoramic imaging

Radiology shows mixed radiolucent-radiopaque lesion with central heterogeneous mineralization, thin radiolucent rim and sclerotic border, with tooth displacement and root resorption-features of ossifying fibroma
Histology shows fibrocellular stroma with spherical calcifications (psammoma-like bodies), more abundant in maxillary lesion
Normal calcium/phosphorus/ALP/PTH and negative GNAS/HRPT2 sequencing argue against fibrous dysplasia and hyperparathyroidism-related lesions

PMC7026587.diagnosis: Atrial septal hematoma (intramural left atrial/interatrial septal hematoma) after recent percutaneous coronary intervention
rationale_summary: New 4×4 cm homogeneous echo-dense mass within/attached to the superior interatrial septum extending toward the aortic root and moving with the atrial wall, consistent with an intramural lesion rather than a free intracavitary tumor
Not present on echocardiogram one year earlier, indicating an acute/subacute process
Recent PCI 2 weeks prior and current dual antiplatelet therapy (aspirin + clopidogrel) increase risk of atrial wall/septal bleeding leading to hematoma

PMC11273566.diagnosis: Ascending bacterial cholangitis/cholecystitis with extrahepatic biliary obstruction due to migrating grass awns (vegetal foreign bodies) in the common bile duct
rationale_summary: Acute vomiting/anorexia/lethargy with marked ALT increase and hyperbilirubinaemia supports hepatobiliary disease with cholestasis

Ultrasound showed intrahepatic biliary dilation and two hyperechoic spindle-shaped linear structures in the common bile duct/duodenal papilla, described as consistent with vegetal foreign bodies (grass awns)
Bile cytology showed septic suppurative inflammation and culture grew E coli, consistent with ascending bacterial infection
Gallbladder wall thickening consistent with cholecystitis
Follow-up ultrasound suggested retrograde migration of the spindle-shaped structures within the biliary tract, supporting foreign body migration causing ongoing obstruction/inflammation

PMC11783208.diagnosis: Torsion of a pedunculated subserosal uterine leiomyoma with secondary right adnexal (ovarian) torsion
rationale_summary: Known uterine leiomyomas and MRI shows a solid mass arising from the right uterine cornua via a broad pedicle with bridging-vessel sign, supporting pedunculated subserosal fibroid origin
Thickened pedicle twisted in a spiral configuration indicates torsion
Predominant nonenhancement with only a thin rim and small enhancing zone near attachment suggests ischemia/infarction of the torsed fibroid
Right ovary enlarged with peripheral follicles and stromal hemorrhagic signal changes supports concomitant ovarian torsion
Right ureter kinked at the twisted pedicle with ipsilateral ureterohydronephrosis indicates mass/pedicle torsion effect

PMC5918180.diagnosis: Antidromic atrioventricular reentrant tachycardia using a decremental right-sided accessory pathway (Mahaim-type atriofascicular pathway)
rationale_summary: Regular WCT with LBBB morphology and left superior axis, terminated by adenosine
No baseline preexcitation but atrial pacing with fast-to-slow AV nodal switch produced fully preexcited QRS with His displaced into ventricular electrogram
Progressive stimulus-delta prolongation with incremental atrial pacing indicates a decremental accessory pathway (Mahaim physiology)
Induced WCT showed AV dissociation with fusion beats and terminated with ventricular pacing/adenosine, consistent with reentry involving an accessory pathway rather than ventricular tachycardia

PMC10959544.diagnosis: Optic nerve sheath meningocele (cystic dilatation of the optic nerve sheath) in an anophthalmic socket
rationale_summary: Young man with prior enucleation for retinoblastoma and new difficulty fitting prosthesis
Exam shows translucent, vascularized conjunctival bulge with symblepharon suggesting an underlying cystic process
CT/MRI show a bilobated/ovoid cyst-like orbital structure with hypodense content, hyperdense walls, no contrast enhancement
MRI T1 signal identical to an atrophied optic nerve, supporting origin from optic nerve/sheath rather than a new tumor
Lesion absent on CT 6 years earlier, consistent with an acquired cystic dilatation rather than congenital mass

PMC5368394.diagnosis: Mycobacterium marinum infection (fish-tank/soil-associated nontuberculous mycobacterial tenosynovitis with sporotrichoid nodules)
rationale_summary: Initial hand laceration while working in soil and exposure to hobby aquariums
Chronic progressive swelling/stiffness of hand, wrist, elbow with firm nodules tracking along extensor surfaces (sporotrichoid pattern)

PMC3224435.diagnosis: Pulmonary artery vasculitis (isolated large-vessel pulmonary arteritis) causing pulmonary artery stenosis and pulmonary hypertension
rationale_summary: CTPA and MRI show bilateral proximal main pulmonary artery concentric soft-tissue/intimal thickening with smooth plaque-like narrowing rather than thromboembolic webs
Inflammatory markers markedly elevated (CRP 77 mg/L, ESR 79 mm/hr) with negative ANA/ANCA, supporting non-ANCA large-vessel vasculitis
Persistent proximal pulmonary artery obstruction despite 5 months of therapeutic anticoagulation argues against CTEPH
FDG-PET shows intermediate uptake in pulmonary artery wall/lesion, consistent with inflammatory activity and used to distinguish from sarcoma
Hemodynamics show pulmonary hypertension (mPAP 32 mmHg) with low cardiac index, consistent with flow-limiting pulmonary artery stenosis

PMC5735624.diagnosis: Sunitinib-induced acute interstitial nephritis (drug-induced tubulointerstitial nephritis)
rationale_summary: Acute kidney injury (creatinine 2.7 mg/dL from baseline 1.0) with fever and elevated inflammatory markers occurring 2 weeks after starting sunitinib
Urinalysis with blood and protein but only subnephrotic proteinuria (484 mg/24 h), supporting tubulointerstitial process rather than primary glomerulonephritis
Workup for immune-mediated glomerulonephritis/vasculitis negative (ANCA, complements, ANA/dsDNA, cryoglobulins, hepatitis serologies)
No evidence of thrombotic microangiopathy/hemolysis (normal haptoglobin, no schistocytes, normal coagulation studies)

PMC8473659.diagnosis: Chronic recurrent multifocal osteomyelitis (CRMO) with associated relapsing optic neuritis and retinal vaso-occlusive event
rationale_summary: Childhood onset of recurrent multifocal bone pain with periosteal reactions and longitudinal linear uptake on bone scan involving multiple long bones and ribs
Inflammatory labs (elevated ESR/CRP, leukocytosis, thrombocytosis) with negative infectious evaluation (negative tuberculin test) and unremarkable bone marrow biopsy
MRI findings centered on periosteal reaction/soft-tissue rind and marrow edema/enhancement without typical infectious osteomyelitis features, consistent with sterile autoinflammatory osteitis
Later development of recurrent bilateral optic neuritis with optic nerve/chiasm enhancement and negative aquaporin-4-IgG and MOG-IgG, supporting a non-MS/NMOSD inflammatory association
Subsequent central retinal artery occlusion with unrevealing vascular/cardiac and hypercoagulable workup, compatible with inflammatory/vasculitic complication reported with autoinflammatory disease

PMC9939044.diagnosis: Noninsulinoma pancreatogenous hypoglycemia syndrome (adult nesidioblastosis) after Roux-en-Y gastric bypass
rationale_summary: Recurrent symptomatic hypoglycemia relieved by carbohydrates in a post-Roux-en-Y patient
Endoscopic ultrasound and CT showed no discrete pancreatic mass, arguing against insulinoma
Selective arterial calcium stimulation test showed a fourfold insulin rise localized to the gastroduodenal artery, consistent with diffuse/islet hyperfunction rather than a focal tumor

72-hour fast did not produce hypoglycemia (glucose stayed >60 mg/dL), supporting postprandial hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia typical of nesidioblastosis rather than fasting hypoglycemia from insulinoma

PMC8752187.diagnosis: FGF23-mediated tumor-induced osteomalacia (phosphaturic mesenchymal tumor causing renal phosphate wasting)
rationale_summary: Severe hypophosphatemia with inappropriate renal phosphate wasting (FEphos 29%, low TmP/GFR)
Chronic bone pain, proximal muscle weakness, kyphosis and fractures with very low BMD consistent with osteomalacia
Elevated alkaline phosphatase with secondary hyperparathyroidism and low-normal calcium
Fanconi syndrome and other common secondary causes excluded; persistent hypophosphatemia despite phosphate/calcitriol suggests ongoing FGF23-driven phosphaturia

PMC11109351.diagnosis: Cervical spinal schwannoma (dumbbell nerve sheath tumor)
rationale_summary: Progressive cervical myelopathy with quadriplegia, hyperreflexia, clonus, and extensor plantar responses localizes to cervical cord

No fever, normal blood tests, and no vertebral destruction/marrow abnormality argue against infection or malignant bony processes

PMC9400789.diagnosis: Pseudoprogression (immune-related tumor flare) after pembrolizumab in PD-L1-high lung adenocarcinoma
rationale_summary: Rapid apparent enlargement and inflammation of the supraclavicular nodal mass with clinical deterioration occurred a few days after first pembrolizumab dose
Symptoms and performance status then spontaneously improved over ~3 weeks with visible regression of the mass
CT before the second dose showed reduction of lymphadenopathy consistent with partial response by RECIST 1.1, supporting transient immune-related enlargement rather than true progression

PMC4532938.diagnosis: Primary intracranial malignant melanoma (parenchymal temporal lobe melanoma)
rationale_summary: Teen with seizures, headache, visual symptoms and a left temporal lobe mass

CT shows heterogeneous hyperdense lesion with enhancement; MRI shows intrinsic T1 hyperintensity and T2 hypointensity with mild heterogeneous gadolinium enhancement-signal pattern typical of melanin-containing tumors
No dural attachment, supporting a primary intraparenchymal melanocytic tumor rather than meningioma
Congenital scattered melanocytic nevi raise suspicion for melanocytic disease despite no family history

PMC7082872.diagnosis: Nutritional vitamin D deficiency (nutritional rickets/osteomalacia) causing secondary hyperparathyroidism
rationale_summary: 15-week-old kitten with bowed long bones, marked osteopenia and abnormal metaphyseal mineralization on CT consistent with rickets/osteomalacia
Ionized and total hypocalcaemia with elevated PTH indicates secondary hyperparathyroidism
Low 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 supports vitamin D deficiency; high 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D3 is consistent with PTH-driven conversion in deficiency
Normal magnesium and normal bile acids reduce support for hypocalcaemia from hypomagnesemia or hepatic dysfunction

PMC9297594.diagnosis: Ustekinumab-induced acute interstitial pneumonitis (drug-induced lung injury)
rationale_summary: Acute febrile illness with rapid progression to cough, dyspnea, and hypoxemic respiratory failure shortly after restarting ustekinumab
Imaging evolved from normal CXR to diffuse reticular infiltrates; CT showed bilateral ground-glass opacities and consolidations without pulmonary embolism
Marked inflammatory response (WBC 16.3×10^9/L with neutrophilia, CRP 296.8 mg/L)
Extensive infectious evaluation including BAL cultures and respiratory pathogen panel was negative
Serologies for connective tissue disease-associated ILD were negative, supporting a drug-related pneumonitis

PMC4666013.diagnosis: Primary pigmented nodular adrenocortical disease (PPNAD) causing ACTH-independent Cushing syndrome (Carney complex spectrum)
rationale_summary: Both siblings have clinical Cushing syndrome with repeatedly elevated cortisol that fails low-dose dexamethasone suppression and suppressed ACTH (<5 pg/mL), indicating ACTH-independent hypercortisolism
Brother's Liddle test shows paradoxical increase in urinary free cortisol, characteristic of PPNAD
Adrenal imaging shows small nodule/micronodular or nodular/enlarged adrenal changes rather than a solitary large cortisol-secreting adenoma
Young age, familial occurrence, and severe osteoporosis/fragility fracture support a genetic micronodular adrenal disease such as PPNAD

PMC6443550.diagnosis: Starvation-induced hepatitis (severe malnutrition/anorexia nervosa) with transient transaminase surge during early refeeding, not classic electrolyte-based refeeding syndrome

rationale_summary: Marked primary hypothyroidism: free T4 0, free T3 low, TSH >100 with classic hypothyroid symptoms/signs and bradycardia
Autoimmune thyroiditis supported by elevated anti-TPO antibodies and atrophic hypoechoic thyroid on ultrasound

Mild hyperprolactinemia (22.5) compatible with TRH-mediated prolactin elevation in primary hypothyroidism
Visual field defect (bitemporal hemianopsia) explained by suprasellar pituitary enlargement compressing optic chiasm

PMC11462391.diagnosis: Metastatic intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (biliary tract adenocarcinoma presenting as cancer of unknown primary)
rationale_summary: Biopsy from liver lesion shows adenocarcinoma with CK7+/CK19+ and CK20−/TTF1− profile, supporting pancreatobiliary/biliary origin and arguing against colorectal or lung primary
Markedly elevated CA19-9 with normal CEA is typical of pancreatobiliary/cholangiocarcinoma rather than colorectal cancer
Widespread metastases with liver-dominant disease and no primary found on PET-CT, EGD, or EUS of pancreas/biliary tree fits occult cholangiocarcinoma presenting as CUP
Mismatch-repair proteins intact and only variable SATB2 positivity make metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma less likely despite prior small tubular adenoma

PMC8927845.diagnosis: Gallbladder adenocarcinoma with pulmonary lymphangitic carcinomatosis (metastatic disease)
rationale_summary: Abdominal CT shows a 3 cm gallbladder mass with adjacent hepatic low-density lesion and porta hepatis/para-aortic lymphadenopathy, supporting gallbladder malignancy with metastasis.
Tumor markers are elevated (CA 19-9 395 U/mL, CEA 9.7 ng/mL, CA-125 160 U/mL), favoring malignancy over infection alone.

Chest CT demonstrates interlobular septal thickening and irregular bronchovascular bundle thickening with diffuse ground-glass/reticular opacities and widespread lymphadenopathy, a pattern concerning for lymphangitic carcinomatosis rather than simple bronchitis.
Right hilar enlargement and supraclavicular/mediastinal/hilar lymphadenopathy support metastatic spread.
Peripheral eosinophilia can be paraneoplastic and is noted alongside imaging and marker evidence of advanced cancer.

rationale_summary: Abdominal CT shows a 3 cm gallbladder mass with adjacent hepatic low-density lesion and porta hepatis/para-aortic lymphadenopathy, supporting gallbladder malignancy with metastasis.
Tumor markers are elevated (CA 19-9 395 U/mL, CEA 9.7 ng/mL, CA-125 160 U/mL), favoring malignancy over infection alone.

Chest CT demonstrates interlobular septal thickening and irregular bronchovascular bundle thickening with diffuse ground-glass/reticular opacities and widespread lymphadenopathy, a pattern concerning for lymphangitic carcinomatosis rather than simple bronchitis.
Right hilar enlargement and supraclavicular/mediastinal/hilar lymphadenopathy support metastatic spread.
Peripheral eosinophilia can be paraneoplastic and is noted alongside imaging and marker evidence of advanced cancer.

PMC3440930.diagnosis: Multilocular thymic cyst (HIV-associated benign thymic cyst)
rationale_summary: HIV-positive adult with anterior mediastinal mass on CT
Mass is lobulated, low-attenuation, predominantly cystic with nonenhancing cystic areas and enhancing septations (multiloculated cystic lesion)
Located draped across the anterior mediastinum without invasion of adjacent structures, favoring a benign thymic cystic process

PMC9208679.diagnosis: Pulmonary tuberculosis with mediastinal tuberculous lymphadenitis (primary TB complex)
rationale_summary: 3-month-old with 2 weeks of cough/tachypnea and minimal systemic symptoms (no fever; low CRP 7 mg/L)

CT shows right paratracheal peripherally enhancing lobulated lesion contiguous with lung and extending into posterior mediastinum causing tracheal compression-compatible with necrotic/enhancing tuberculous lymphadenitis
Associated right upper lobe and superior right lower lobe consolidation with multiple cystic nonenhancing areas suggesting necrosis/cavitation rather than simple bacterial pneumonia
Radiographic severity disproportionate to clinical presentation and no visible BCG scar, supporting TB over routine community-acquired pneumonia/CPAM
