PMC8922707.diagnosis: Eosinophilic (hypersensitivity) myocarditis with perimyocarditis leading to fulminant heart failure and complete heart block
rationale_summary: Previously healthy man with acute febrile illness and very high hs-troponin with initial preserved LV function, small pericardial effusion, and rapid progression to severe LV dysfunction and cardiogenic shock
Cardiac MRI showed myocardial edema with multifocal subepicardial and subendocardial LGE plus pericardial inflammation, consistent with inflammatory myocarditis/perimyocarditis
Developed complete heart block and low-output state requiring inotropes and temporary pacing, a pattern seen in fulminant inflammatory myocarditis

Infectious workup and vasculitis-associated autoantibodies were negative, supporting a noninfectious inflammatory etiology; recent macrolide/NSAID exposure raises hypersensitivity myocarditis despite absent peripheral eosinophilia

PMC5120475.diagnosis: ROHHAD syndrome (Rapid-onset Obesity with Hypothalamic dysfunction, Hypoventilation, and Autonomic Dysregulation)
rationale_summary: Abrupt rapid-onset obesity with severe hyperphagia beginning at 4 years
Prominent autonomic dysregulation: decreased lacrimation/nasal secretions/sweating, cold blue extremities, alternating diarrhea/constipation, polyuria/polydipsia, nocturnal enuresis
Neurobehavioral and sleep changes: mood lability/aggression, fatigue, social withdrawal, prolonged sleep and daytime somnolence
Endocrine labs largely normal despite symptoms; mild pituitary enlargement on MRI supports hypothalamic-pituitary involvement without another structural lesion

PMC8065272.diagnosis: Page kidney (compression-induced renin-mediated hypertension from renal subcapsular/perirenal hematoma after blunt trauma)
rationale_summary: Young man developed new severe paroxysmal hypertensive crises after blunt abdominal trauma and massive resuscitation
Hypertension was refractory to multiple antihypertensives and not explained by pain/sedation after extubation
Workup excluded aortic dissection and renal artery obstruction/dissection on CTA and Doppler
Trauma-related renin-driven hypertension from renal compression (Page kidney) fits the episodic extreme BP surges despite initially negative CT for hematoma

PMC4898247.diagnosis: Radioiodine uptake in chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) lymphadenopathy (false-positive metastatic thyroid cancer on I-131 scan)
rationale_summary: Known CLL with enlarged axillary and retroperitoneal lymph nodes on CT before ablation
Pretherapy I-131 scan showed no definite metastatic uptake, only very faint questionable axillary activity
Post-therapy WBS showed new bilateral axillary and inguinal uptake
SPECT/CT localized I-131 concentration to enlarged axillary lymph nodes rather than thyroid bed or other typical metastatic sites

PMC11522587.diagnosis: Vinyl chloride-associated acro-osteolysis (occupational vinyl chloride disease)
rationale_summary: Decades-long progressive, painless shortening of fingers with radiographic complete resorption of distal phalanges (acro-osteolysis) and phalangeal fusion/sclerosis
Occupational exposure history: worked at a PVC factory transferring/cleaning chemicals without gloves
Associated findings reported with vinyl chloride disease: Raynaud-like episode (bluish finger), hypopigmented "map-like" patches, neuropathy with distal sensory loss and hand muscle atrophy
No sclerodactyly, telangiectasias, or salt-and-pepper changes to support systemic sclerosis despite contractures

PMC5173514.diagnosis: Sarcoidosis (systemic, presenting with granulomatous lymphadenopathy and pulmonary involvement)
rationale_summary: Multifocal FDG-avid lymph nodes and organ lesions with very low PSA (0.02 ng/mL) argue against prostate cancer metastasis
Transbronchial lung biopsy and EBUS-TBNA both show non-caseous epithelioid granulomas
AFB smear/culture/PCR from sputum and biopsy specimens are negative, making active tuberculosis unlikely despite positive IGRA/PPD
BAL shows lymphocytosis with markedly elevated CD4/CD8 ratio (7.69), supporting sarcoidosis

PMC7934766.diagnosis: Vascular dementia (subcortical ischemic vascular disease) presenting with late-onset manic/psychotic symptoms
rationale_summary: Late-life new behavioral syndrome with disinhibition, irritability, impulsive spending, grandiose/paranoid delusions, and intermittent olfactory hallucinations
Prominent executive dysfunction with relatively preserved memory/orientation (FAB 9/18; MMSE 27/30) plus perseveration and impaired abstraction on exam
Broad-based shuffling gait consistent with subcortical/frontal involvement
MRI shows advanced small-vessel ischemic disease with confluent periventricular/subcortical white-matter hyperintensities, frontal-predominant atrophy, and microbleeds
High vascular risk profile (hypertension, heavy smoking, elevated LDL, high ASCVD risk) supports vascular etiology

PMC10832606.diagnosis: Desmoid-type fibromatosis (extra-abdominal desmoid tumor) of the groin compressing the common femoral vein
rationale_summary: Progressive unilateral non-pitting edema extending from foot/ankle to entire leg suggests lymphatic/venous outflow obstruction rather than inflammatory arthritis or DVT (duplex negative).
CT/duplex/MRI show a ~3-5 cm soft-tissue mass in the right groin dorsal to and compressing the common femoral vein with focal venous narrowing.

MRI signal (T1 isointense to muscle, mildly T2 hyperintense) with slight enhancement and biopsy showing proliferating spindle cells fit a fibrous spindle-cell lesion such as desmoid-type fibromatosis.

PMC3978404.diagnosis: Constrictive pericarditis causing hepatic congestion (cardiac cirrhosis)
rationale_summary: Incidentally noted nodular liver with biopsy showing cirrhosis and sinusoidal dilatation, consistent with congestive hepatopathy
No viral, autoimmune, or metabolic liver disease on extensive serologies; no alcohol use
Echocardiography showed enlarged inferior vena cava and right heart cath showed elevated right atrial pressure (12 mm Hg) with elevated filling pressures
CT pulmonary angiography showed scattered pericardial calcifications, supporting constrictive pericarditis
Progressive exertional dyspnea with unrevealing chest radiograph and no pulmonary embolism or intracardiac shunt

PMC10233214.diagnosis: Multisystem inflammatory syndrome in adults (MIS-A) associated with prior SARS-CoV-2 infection/vaccination
rationale_summary: Young adult with recent PCR-confirmed COVID-19 (8 weeks prior) and positive SARS-CoV-2 IgG with current PCR negative
Acute febrile illness progressing to shock, hypoxemic respiratory failure, and multiorgan dysfunction (AKI/oliguria, hepatitis, thrombocytopenia, coagulopathy)
Marked hyperinflammation (very elevated CRP, ferritin, D-dimer, IL-6) with negative bacterial cultures and negative respiratory/other infectious and autoimmune workup
No evidence of primary cardiogenic cause (normal echo and cardiac enzymes), supporting systemic inflammatory syndrome rather than myocarditis/ACS

PMC6751912.diagnosis: Late-onset urea cycle disorder (most consistent with ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency) causing hyperammonemic encephalopathy mimicking non-convulsive status epilepticus, precipitated by valproate/topiramate
rationale_summary: Recurrent episodic akinetic mutism/unresponsiveness with post-episode amnesia over 5 years suggests metabolic encephalopathy rather than primary psychiatric disease
EEG showed generalized 2.5 Hz sharp-and-slow discharges with transient suppression after diazepam but no sustained clinical/electrographic response to multiple antiseizure drugs or steroids
Mild hyperammonemia with normal liver-function tests points to a non-hepatic cause of hyperammonemia
Clinical deterioration with rising ammonia after valproate/topiramate, requiring dextrose, sodium benzoate/phenylbutyrate, and hemodialysis, is typical of urea cycle failure
Progression to diffuse cerebral edema and near-isoelectric EEG is consistent with severe hyperammonemic encephalopathy

PMC6416959.diagnosis: Post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD) involving the mesocolon
rationale_summary: Renal transplant recipient 10 months post-cadaveric transplant on prednisone, mycophenolate, and tacrolimus (high PTLD risk setting)
CT shows two separate, ill-defined moderately dense mesocolonic masses; proximal lesion is separate and contains no air, making a single perforated diverticulitis process less cohesive
Alternative considerations raised on imaging included PTLD in addition to perforated neoplasm/diverticulitis, and patient lacks fever or leukocytosis despite presumed perforation

PMC7403879.diagnosis: Parasitic (autoamputated) ovarian mature cystic teratoma in the posterior cul-de-sac
rationale_summary: Acute right lower quadrant pain with imaging showing a rim-calcified component consistent with a dermoid/teratoma
Mass is in the posterior cul-de-sac and appears adjacent to but separate from the right ovary, suggesting an ectopic/parasitic adnexal lesion rather than an ovarian mass
Longstanding heterogeneous, echogenic right adnexal mass on ultrasound 9 years earlier supports a slow-growing dermoid that could undergo torsion and autoamputation
Negative hCG and normal CA-125/CEA/CA19-9 support a benign process

PMC11116953.diagnosis: Idiopathic left fascicular ventricular tachycardia (verapamil-sensitive fascicular VT)
rationale_summary: Wide-complex tachycardia with right bundle branch block morphology and superior axis in a structurally normal heart
Subtle beat-to-beat QRS variation on precordial leads consistent with fascicular VT
No response to adenosine and initial cardioversion attempts, arguing against typical SVT with aberrancy
Post-conversion ECG shows ventricular pre-excitation with a different (LBBB) morphology than the tachycardia, supporting VT rather than antidromic AVRT

PMC8426609.diagnosis: Diabetic hemichorea-hemiballismus (nonketotic hyperglycemia-related basal ganglia syndrome)
rationale_summary: Acute unilateral chorea/ballismus of left arm and leg in a patient with severe uncontrolled type 2 diabetes (glucose 776 mg/dL, HbA1c 15.8%) without ketoacidosis (pH 7.37, bicarbonate 23, anion gap 13).

Neuroimaging pattern typical for this entity: initial CT hyperattenuation of the contralateral (right) lentiform nucleus with internal capsule sparing; later MRI with right lentiform/caudate T1 hyperintensity.
Symptoms fluctuated with glycemic changes and recurred despite negative autoimmune/paraneoplastic and normal copper studies, supporting a metabolic (hyperglycemia-related) basal ganglia disorder.

PMC4217353.diagnosis: Pulmonary tuberculosis (reactivation) associated with etanercept therapy
rationale_summary: 66-year-old man on TNF-α inhibitor (etanercept) for 10 months, a risk factor for TB reactivation
CT shows a thick-walled cavitary lesion in the right lung apex, a classic location/morphology for reactivation TB
Systemic symptoms (malaise, weakness, anorexia, weight loss) without prominent respiratory symptoms can occur in TB
FDG-PET hypermetabolism (SUV 7.4-12.8) can mimic malignancy and is not specific
Bronchoscopy/BAL negative for malignancy and fungal studies, leaving mycobacterial infection as a key consideration

PMC7016271.diagnosis: Gadolinium contrast-induced noncardiogenic pulmonary edema (acute lung injury)
rationale_summary: Acute hypoxemic dyspnea began <30 minutes after gadolinium-enhanced MRI
CT angiography excluded pulmonary embolism but showed diffuse perihilar ground-glass opacities consistent with pulmonary edema/acute lung injury
Cardiac workup did not support cardiogenic edema (normal troponin, preserved LVEF, no pulmonary hypertension, only mildly elevated NT-proBNP)
Infectious and rheumatologic evaluations were negative, leaving contrast-triggered acute lung injury as the most likely cause

PMC9254356.diagnosis: Primary central nervous system lymphoma (with ocular involvement) misdiagnosed as Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada syndrome
rationale_summary: Bilateral exudative retinal detachments with choroidal thickening and pinpoint leakage initially mimicked VKH
Neurologic symptoms (dizziness, confusion, altered mental status) with progressive subcortical white-matter hyperintensities on MRI are atypical for VKH
Development of new hemorrhagic foci and radiologic progression despite high-dose systemic corticosteroids argues against VKH and suggests an alternative infiltrative CNS process such as lymphoma
Initial steroid-associated transient improvement followed by rapid relapse/progression is compatible with steroid-responsive CNS lymphoma

PMC4712557.diagnosis: Hemosuccus pancreaticus from a splenic artery aneurysm (bleeding into the pancreatic duct)
rationale_summary: Six-month intermittent relapsing melena with repeated endoscopies failing to localize a source suggests an obscure, intermittent upper GI bleed
CT shows a saccular aneurysm of the splenic artery in the pancreatic segment projecting into pancreatic parenchyma and contacting the pancreatic duct

Associated mild distal pancreatic duct dilatation and distal pancreatic swelling/low density support ductal involvement adjacent to the aneurysm, consistent with bleeding via the pancreatic duct (hemosuccus pancreaticus)

PMC4799621.diagnosis: Intraoperative bladder distension from arthroscopic irrigation fluid extravasation (autonomic hyperreflexia-like sympathetic surge)
rationale_summary: After initial hypotension/bradycardia treated with phenylephrine, he developed an abrupt, severe hypertension (229-234/111-116) with sinus tachycardia (115-125) immediately after arthroscopic irrigation began

Hypertension/tachycardia persisted despite stopping phenylephrine and despite deepening anesthesia and giving propofol, midazolam, and esmolol, suggesting a strong ongoing sympathetic stimulus rather than inadequate anesthetic depth
Temporal linkage to initiation of irrigation during shoulder arthroscopy supports irrigation-fluid-related distension/extravasation as the trigger for the sympathetic surge

PMC5038105.diagnosis: Lepromatous leprosy with Lucio phenomenon (necrotizing vasculopathy)
rationale_summary: Slit-skin smear with very high bacillary load (BI 6+) supports multibacillary/lepromatous leprosy

Diffuse facial/ear infiltration with ciliary/supraciliary madarosis, nasal septal perforation, and symmetric peripheral nerve thickening with glove-and-stocking anesthesia are classic for lepromatous leprosy

Painful purpuric patches progressing to hemorrhagic bullae, angular infarcts, gangrene, and deep necrotic ulcers with palpable pulses and normal arterial Doppler is consistent with Lucio phenomenon (leprosy-associated necrotizing vasculopathy)

Major vascular risk factors present: uncontrolled diabetes (glucose 219 mg/dL, HbA1c 11.0%), hyperlipidemia (LDL 189 mg/dL), obesity

rationale_summary: Received enoxaparin thromboprophylaxis after knee replacement
Developed marked thrombocytopenia (platelets 43,000) about 15 days after heparin exposure, confirmed on repeat sample
Concurrent new thromboses in multiple sites: ischemic strokes on MRI, pulmonary emboli on CT angiography, hepatic vein branch thrombosis, and leg DVT
Very high D-dimer and absence of alternative cardioembolic/atherosclerotic source (sinus rhythm, normal echo, normal carotid duplex)

PMC8133085.diagnosis: Chronic spontaneous urticaria triggered by Hymenoptera (Vespula/Vespa) venom sensitization after wasp stings
rationale_summary: New-onset migratory pruritic wheals for >3 weeks, each lasting <24 hours, without identifiable food/drug/physical triggers, consistent with chronic spontaneous urticaria
Onset followed multiple wasp stings with immediate acute urticaria, suggesting sting-related sensitization preceding persistent urticaria
Evidence of Hymenoptera venom allergy: positive intradermal tests to Vespula spp and Vespa crabro and elevated venom-specific IgE with component positivity (rVes v 5, rPol d 5)

Extensive evaluation for infectious, autoimmune, inflammatory, and complement-mediated causes is negative/normal (including thyroid and celiac antibodies, ESR/CRP, complement, autologous serum skin test)
Poor control despite high-dose antihistamines and prolonged low-dose prednisone supports refractory chronic urticaria rather than transient allergic reaction

PMC9549187.diagnosis: Giant almost completely thrombosed left renal artery aneurysm causing severe hydronephrosis
rationale_summary: CT/CTA demonstrated a 109×102×94 mm saccular left renal artery aneurysm with parietal calcifications and scant contrast filling consistent with near-complete thrombosis
Mass effect from the aneurysm compressed the renal pelvis, producing severe hydronephrosis and extreme left renal parenchymal thinning (2 mm)
Ultrasound showed a large heterogeneous mass adjacent to a calcified aortic wall with minimal Doppler flow, supporting a thrombosed vascular aneurysm rather than a solid tumor

PMC5845136.diagnosis: Idiopathic hypereosinophilic syndrome with eosinophilic myocarditis (Loeffler endomyocarditis) causing acute heart failure
rationale_summary: Marked hypereosinophilia (AEC 21.6×10^3/μL, 63.5%) with systemic features (fever, malaise, sweating) and hepatosplenomegaly
Cardiac involvement: acute dyspnea, pulmonary congestion, elevated BNP (1280.5 pg/mL), troponin I 35.4 ng/dL, ECG ST depressions, echo global LV hypokinesia with EF 40% and small pericardial effusion

Extensive infectious/parasitic, autoimmune, and hematologic malignancy workup negative; bone marrow shows eosinophilia without lymphoproliferation; FIP1L1-PDGFRA and BCR-ABL negative, supporting idiopathic HES with myocardial involvement

PMC5507571.diagnosis: Migrainous infarction (posterior circulation ischemic stroke) involving the right medial longitudinal fasciculus
rationale_summary: Adolescent with typical migraine symptoms (pulsatile headache, nausea, photophobia/phonophobia) followed by new focal brainstem signs (diplopia with right adduction paresis/INO, dysarthria, imbalance/ataxia).

MRI shows an acute ischemic lesion: diffusion restriction with T2/FLAIR hyperintensity in the paramedian right dorsal midbrain localizing to the medial longitudinal fasciculus, matching the ocular motor findings.
CT/CTA normal and no fever or other systemic features; persistent diplopia with imaging-confirmed infarct supports migrainous infarction rather than transient migraine aura alone.

PMC11299065.diagnosis: Severe combined pre- and post-capillary pulmonary hypertension with right ventricular failure (cardiogenic shock) secondary to left-sided valvular regurgitation/left heart disease, not active Whipple myocarditis
rationale_summary: Cardiogenic shock with anasarca and severely reduced right heart function on echocardiography
Right heart catheterization: markedly elevated RA pressure (32) and mPAP (55) with elevated PCWP (27) plus high PVR (10 WU) consistent with combined pre- and post-capillary pulmonary hypertension
Severe aortic and mitral regurgitation on echocardiography supports left heart disease as the post-capillary driver
Whipple PCR in blood negative and RV endomyocardial biopsy shows no inflammation or Whipple involvement, arguing against Whipple myocarditis as the cause

PMC6961360.diagnosis: Iatrogenic aortic root-to-left ventricular outflow tract fistula (aorto-LVOT fistula) causing paravalvular leak after surgical bioprosthetic aortic valve replacement
rationale_summary: Post-CPB TEE shows a turbulent jet originating from outside the sewing ring with flow convergence adjacent to a stent post, consistent with paravalvular flow

A second jet arises from outside the sewing ring and runs perpendicularly below the ring toward the LVOT, suggesting an abnormal extracardiac-to-LVOT communication (fistula) rather than simple transvalvular regurgitation
Jets are in close proximity to the stent post/sewing ring after supra-annular bioprosthetic AVR, supporting an iatrogenic periannular complication

PMC8436276.diagnosis: Pheochromocytoma-associated takotsubo syndrome (midventricular variant) precipitated by adrenalectomy
rationale_summary: Catecholamine-producing adrenal tumor confirmed by elevated urinary metanephrines with perioperative hypertensive surges during manipulation
Acute postoperative shock with new LBBB and severe LV systolic dysfunction
Coronary angiography without obstructive disease or vasospasm
Ventriculography/echo showing circumferential midventricular (with apical) akinesia and reduced LVEF (55% to 25%), consistent with takotsubo pattern
Marked troponin and NT-proBNP rise supporting acute stress cardiomyopathy rather than obstructive MI

PMC6831833.diagnosis: Sai-rei-to-induced drug-related pneumonitis presenting with diffuse alveolar hemorrhage
rationale_summary: Respiratory symptoms (dry cough, dyspnea, fever) began ~2 weeks after starting the herbal medicine Sai-rei-to
Imaging showed bilateral ground-glass opacities with infiltrative shadows and pleural effusions, consistent with acute drug-related lung injury
Bronchoalveolar lavage demonstrated diffuse alveolar hemorrhage with neutrophil predominance

No prior interstitial lung disease or allergy history; bacteriological tests were negative and echocardiography showed preserved EF/normal wall motion, making infection or cardiogenic edema less likely
Clinical decision to stop Sai-rei-to and treat with high-dose steroids aligns with suspected drug-induced lung injury/DAH

Immunoprofile: diffuse CD10+, ER+, PR+ supports endometrial stromal differentiation

Low proliferative activity (Ki-67 ~8%) and absence of necrosis/mitoses consistent with low-grade ESS

PMC11228663.diagnosis: Rhino-orbito-cerebral mucormycosis (invasive mucormycosis) with vascular invasion causing cavernous sinus thrombosis and cerebral infarcts
rationale_summary: Severely immunocompromised post-allogeneic HSCT with profound neutropenia and persistent/recurrent sinusitis despite broad-spectrum antibiotics and voriconazole
Progression to orbital apex/orbital cellulitis signs: proptosis, chemosis, ophthalmoplegia, nonreactive pupil, optic nerve ischemic signal on MRI
Imaging shows invasive sinonasal disease with bony demineralization/erosion and angioinvasion: absent opacification of left ophthalmic/facial vessels, cavernous sinus nonenhancement (thrombosis)

PMC7455415.diagnosis: Mediastinal thymoma (misidentified intraoperatively as ectopic parathyroid adenoma)
rationale_summary: Anterior mediastinal 18-mm mass resected for suspected ectopic parathyroid adenoma
Frozen section showed epithelial sheets/nests with pseudoglandular structures and a patchy peripheral lymphoid component-features more consistent with thymic epithelial tumor than parathyroid tissue
Intraoperative PTH failed to fall after excision and instead rose (191.5 preop; 149.3 at 10 min; 259.5 at 30 min), arguing the removed mass was not the hyperfunctioning parathyroid source

PMC6188590.diagnosis: Statin-associated immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy (anti-HMG-CoA reductase antibody-associated)
rationale_summary: Recurrent proximal myalgias with creatine kinase elevation after exposure to two different statins (atorvastatin then pravastatin)
CK continued to rise substantially (to 1400 U/L) despite statin discontinuation, arguing against simple toxic statin myopathy
Myositis panel negative and EMG/MRI lacked alternative inflammatory myopathy findings, supporting anti-HMGCR IMNM as the key statin-related autoimmune myopathy to test for

PMC4840426.diagnosis: Vildagliptin-induced interstitial pneumonia (drug-induced interstitial lung disease with organizing pneumonia/acute alveolar injury pattern)
rationale_summary: Respiratory symptoms began several days after starting vildagliptin
CT shows diffuse ground-glass opacities with irregular reticulation in basal/peribronchovascular distribution and restrictive PFTs
BAL lymphocytosis with eosinophils and negative microbiology supports drug-related pneumonitis
Very high KL-6 and IgE with biopsy showing organizing pneumonia with acute alveolar injury and eosinophilic/lymphoplasmacytic infiltration
Autoimmune serologies negative and no connective-tissue disease signs, supporting drug-induced etiology

PMC6787862.diagnosis: Primary adrenal insufficiency (Addison disease) precipitating adrenal crisis with severe hypotonic hyponatremia
rationale_summary: Severe hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 101, plasma osmolality 225) with hypovolemia/orthostasis and inappropriately high urine sodium (128) suggests renal salt wasting rather than extrarenal losses
Symptoms over 1 month (fatigue, poor appetite, weight loss, vague abdominal pain) plus new skin tanning/hyperpigmentation are classic for primary adrenal insufficiency
Mild hyperkalemia (K 5.2) and low CO2 (16) support mineralocorticoid deficiency
Autoimmune background (longstanding hypothyroidism) raises risk for autoimmune Addison disease (polyglandular syndrome)

PMC8082195.diagnosis: Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP) with CNS involvement
rationale_summary: Severe thrombocytopenia (7,000/mcL) with hemolytic anemia (drop in hemoglobin, elevated LDH, very low haptoglobin) and petechial rash
Acute fluctuating encephalopathy without focal deficits consistent with neurologic involvement of TTP
Brain MRI shows multifocal lesions with punctate microhemorrhages, compatible with microvascular thrombotic injury in TTP
CSF essentially normal and broad viral testing negative, arguing against viral encephalitis
Treated appropriately with plasmapheresis and high-dose steroids; ADAMTS13 activity sent to confirm TTP

PMC5750514.diagnosis: Pulmonary-renal syndrome due to anti-glomerular basement membrane (anti-GBM) disease (Goodpasture syndrome)
rationale_summary: Massive hemoptysis with chest radiograph showing diffuse pulmonary hemorrhage and alveolar source confirmed by tracheal aspirates
Concurrent acute kidney involvement with anuria, elevated urea, metabolic acidosis/hyperkalemia, and urinalysis showing hematuria, proteinuria, and granular casts consistent with glomerulonephritis
No rash or purpura to suggest a vasculitic purpuric process; presentation fits pulmonary-renal syndrome

PMC9464768.diagnosis: Isolated left fallopian tube torsion (without ovarian torsion)
rationale_summary: Acute severe left lower-quadrant pain with minimal pelvic exam findings and negative pregnancy test
Ultrasound showed no ovarian mass/cyst and normal bilateral ovarian Doppler flow, arguing against ovarian torsion
Distal left fallopian tube appeared bulky on ultrasound, suggesting primary tubal pathology
Clinical deterioration over days (fever and markedly rising CRP) despite initially mild findings prompted diagnostic laparoscopy, consistent with missed isolated tubal torsion

PMC10098721.diagnosis: Fatal non-accidental trauma (child physical abuse/battered infant syndrome)
rationale_summary: Multiple antemortem cranial vault fractures (14) with healing at varying stages, including a stair-step fracture, indicating repeated impacts over time
Classic metaphyseal fractures of the left humerus, radius, and ulna plus a remodeled diaphyseal ulnar fracture with malrotation, consistent with inflicted injury
Associated soft-tissue scalp hemorrhages overlying skull fractures and hemorrhagic CSF/subarachnoid hemorrhage
External petechiae and contusions support traumatic mechanism rather than a single accidental event

PMC11442918.diagnosis: Intestinal tuberculosis (ileocecal/colonic TB) mimicking Crohn's disease
rationale_summary: Origin from a TB-endemic area (Myanmar) with new systemic illness and high fevers while on high-dose steroids
CT shows calcified mediastinal/hilar nodes and right middle lobe calcified atelectasis suggestive of prior/active pulmonary TB plus widespread intra-abdominal lymphadenopathy and ascites

Endoscopy lacks typical Crohn's longitudinal ulcers/cobblestoning and instead shows circumferential transverse/round ulcers, patulous ileocecal valve with pseudodiverticular deformity, pseudopolyposis, and rectosigmoid sparing-features consistent with intestinal TB
Poor response/worsening on 5-ASA and corticosteroids supports an infectious etiology rather than Crohn's flare

PMC11667170.diagnosis: Varicella zoster virus (VZV) meningoencephalitis with VZV vasculopathy
rationale_summary: Active vesicular eruption in a dermatomal distribution preceding acute encephalopathy (hallucinations, obtundation)
CSF with very high WBC (neutrophil-predominant), markedly elevated protein, low glucose, and many RBCs; bacterial cultures negative
CSF encephalitis panel positive for VZV antibody
MRI shows corpus callosum and posterior white-matter lesions with edema and enhancement that can mimic butterfly glioma but fit infectious/inflammatory encephalitis
MRA shows intracranial arterial irregularities/stenosis consistent with VZV-associated vasculopathy

PMC8811182.diagnosis: Orbital silicone implant extrusion with replacement by a foreign-body granuloma (sterile inflammatory mass) mimicking an implant
rationale_summary: Two months post-enucleation with silicone implant, the dog developed periorbital swelling and a draining tract at the left incision, consistent with implant-related complication.
A black spherical object consistent with a silicone implant was vomited preoperatively, indicating the left orbital implant had migrated/extruded into the gastrointestinal tract.

Despite apparent palpation of an "implant," surgical exploration found a firm pinkish-white 2-cm orbital mass occupying the space, consistent with inflammatory/foreign-body granulomatous tissue rather than an implant.

Ileal biopsies show lymphocytic infiltration without malignancy or CMV; stool cultures negative and CT unremarkable

Normal gastrin and negative ANCA/ANA make Zollinger-Ellison and vasculitis less likely; capsule endoscopy showed multiple ileal aphtha-like erosions consistent with small-bowel ulcerative disease

PMC5470563.diagnosis: Congenital testicular juvenile granulosa cell tumor
rationale_summary: Male newborn with unilateral enlarged testis and scrotal ultrasound showing a heterogeneous solid intratesticular mass

PMC6081566.diagnosis: Metastatic breast carcinoma with diffuse bone and bone marrow involvement (carcinoma of unknown primary, most consistent with breast origin)
rationale_summary: Diffuse mixed osteolytic/osteosclerotic skeletal lesions with skull lytic lesions and severe normocytic anemia suggest marrow-infiltrating metastases
Bone marrow biopsy shows metastatic adenocarcinoma with cribriform/microacinar patterns

Myeloma workup negative (no monoclonal protein; normal immunoglobulins), favoring metastatic carcinoma

PMC4898367.diagnosis: Sebaceous carcinoma of the parotid gland (primary salivary gland sebaceous carcinoma)
rationale_summary: Parotid mass with cervical lymphadenopathy; resection shows lobulated carcinoma arising within salivary tissue
Histology: nests of large foamy/vacuolated clear cells with central nuclei (sebaceous differentiation) with peripheral basaloid cells; squamous islands with keratin pearls
PAS negative in foamy cells supports lipid-rich (not glycogen/mucin) clear cells
IHC: EMA and pancytokeratin positive; p63 positive; negative S100, CD10, vimentin, melan A, CD45 helps exclude myoepithelial, renal, melanocytic, and lymphoid tumors

rationale_summary: Progressive bilateral subepithelial/Bowman layer opacities with additional posterior stromal and granular endothelial deposits on AS-OCT and confocal microscopy
Confocal shows highly reflective granular endothelial deposits with preserved architecture and no perineural deposits, supporting corneal deposition rather than primary dystrophy

PMC5632850.diagnosis: Transient perivascular inflammation of the carotid artery (TIPIC syndrome, formerly carotidynia)
rationale_summary: Acute unilateral neck pain localized to the carotid bifurcation, worsened by palpation and neck movement, with normal neurologic exam
Imaging shows eccentric perivascular soft-tissue thickening and intense enhancement around the distal common carotid/carotid bulb/proximal ICA with preserved lumen/flow
No evidence of carotid dissection or aneurysm on Doppler and MRI (no intimal flap, no wall hematoma)
No systemic vasculitis or other vascular disease: normal/near-normal inflammatory markers, negative ANA/ANCA, and no abnormalities in other vessels on CT/MRI

PMC7284054.diagnosis: Paraneoplastic Raynaud phenomenon/digital ischemia secondary to advanced ovarian (primary peritoneal) serous carcinoma
rationale_summary: Acute onset severe Raynaud-like fingertip pain, paresthesia, color change progressing to digital ulcerations refractory to multiple vasodilator/antiplatelet therapies

PMC3574658.diagnosis: Pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1a (Albright hereditary osteodystrophy due to GNAS inactivation)
rationale_summary: Early-onset severe obesity with hyperphagia and round facies/central obesity suggest Albright hereditary osteodystrophy phenotype
Persistently elevated PTH with normal calcium/phosphate indicates PTH resistance

Combination of PTH and TSH resistance is characteristic of pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1a

PMC7924147.diagnosis: Disseminated histoplasmosis (Histoplasma capsulatum) with adrenal involvement causing primary adrenal insufficiency
rationale_summary: Advanced untreated HIV (CD4 27) with weight loss, cough, lymphadenopathy, and rash suggests disseminated opportunistic infection
Biopsy of maxillary mass shows granulomatous tissue with macrophages containing well-demarcated fungal spores, consistent with intracellular yeast of Histoplasma
Chest CT shows hilar lymphadenopathy and bilateral adrenal enlargement, a classic pattern for disseminated histoplasmosis

Hyponatremia, hyperkalemia, elevated ACTH with inadequate cosyntropin cortisol response and negative 21-hydroxylase antibodies indicate primary adrenal insufficiency from adrenal infiltration rather than autoimmune disease

PMC5469202.diagnosis: Factitious (exogenous) glucocorticoid exposure causing pseudo-Cushing syndrome with assay interference
rationale_summary: Markedly elevated 24-hour urinary total cortisol by chemiluminescence with normal morning serum cortisol suggests immunoassay cross-reactivity rather than true hypercortisolism
Low DHEA-S argues against ACTH-dependent Cushing syndrome and is compatible with adrenal suppression from exogenous glucocorticoids
ACTH is not elevated and adrenal CT is normal, making endogenous Cushing (pituitary/adrenal) less likely
History as a health professional and denial of steroid use raises suspicion for covert/factitious glucocorticoid intake

PMC11057788.diagnosis: Restrictive cardiomyopathy due to cardiac allograft amyloidosis (transthyretin-type)
rationale_summary: Refractory right-sided heart failure with preserved LVEF and marked right atrial enlargement
Hemodynamics show restrictive physiology: prominent y-descent and RV "square-root" sign with near-normal wedge pressure and no ventricular interdependence (argues against constrictive pericarditis)
Mitral inflow shows large E wave with absent A wave despite sinus rhythm, and CMR notes abnormal atrial contraction, supporting restrictive filling/atrial mechanical dysfunction
Rejection and coronary allograft vasculopathy were excluded by biopsies and angiography, prompting an infiltrative/restrictive etiology such as amyloidosis in a transplant recipient

PMC5005770.diagnosis: Chronic mefloquine neurotoxicity (mefloquine-induced central vestibulopathy with persistent neuropsychiatric symptoms)
rationale_summary: Neuropsychiatric symptoms (anxiety, vivid dreams/nightmares, confusion, emotional lability, cognitive/word-finding and memory problems) began within 2 weeks of starting weekly mefloquine prophylaxis and progressed while he continued it for 6 months

Persistent vestibular syndrome ("sea legs," constant spinning dizziness, frequent vertigo episodes, falls) with vestibular testing showing central vestibulopathy (hyperactive VOR gains, low phase) and normal brain/IAC MRI

PMC7491482.diagnosis: COVID-19-associated retiform purpura (acro-ischemic/livedoid vasculopathy from thrombotic microangiopathy)
rationale_summary: New asymptomatic SARS-CoV-2 infection followed 6 days later by violaceous, retiform patches on toes/feet (acral distribution)
Lesions are nontender and limited to feet/toes, consistent with COVID-related acral ischemic/retiform purpura pattern
Elevated D-dimer and fibrinogen with otherwise normal CBC and coagulation studies supports a COVID-associated prothrombotic state rather than primary coagulopathy
No systemic inflammatory cytokine elevation and no respiratory disease, fitting a cutaneous thrombotic manifestation in otherwise mild COVID-19

PMC10318311.diagnosis: Eagle syndrome (elongated styloid process/stylohyoid ligament calcification)
rationale_summary: 3-year history of jaw "popping" without TMJ clicking/crepitus and no response to night guard/exercises argues against primary TMJ dysfunction
Associated otologic and pharyngeal symptoms: right-sided tinnitus, progressive sensorineural hearing loss, dysphagia with liquids, and hoarse voice

PMC4356029.diagnosis: Smoking-related interstitial fibrosis (SRIF) with NSIP-like pattern
rationale_summary: Progressive interstitial changes and elevated KL-6 in a heavy current smoker with mild emphysema and lower-lobe ground-glass/reticular opacities consistent with NSIP on HRCT
BAL with mild lymphocytosis and nondiagnostic TBBx; no evidence of infection or defined connective-tissue disease (only low-titer ANA; specific autoantibodies negative)

Surgical lung biopsy shows temporally homogeneous interstitial fibrosis with preserved architecture and no honeycombing, plus centrilobular emphysema and focal intraluminal macrophage accumulation-features supporting smoking-related interstitial fibrosis rather than classic UIP/IPF

PMC9464104.diagnosis: Severe vitamin B12 deficiency causing pseudo-thrombotic microangiopathy (pseudo-TTP)
rationale_summary: Macrocytic anemia (MCV 116) with pancytopenia (WBC 3.5, platelets 28k) and smear showing schistocytes can mimic TTP
Hemolysis markers present (very high LDH 1647, low haptoglobin) with normal coagulation studies, supporting a non-DIC microangiopathic picture
No neurologic deficits and no response to plasmapheresis; ADAMTS13 activity normal (85%), arguing against true TTP
Absence of hypersegmented neutrophils does not exclude B12 deficiency; teardrop cells and macrocytosis support megaloblastic process

PMC4592892.diagnosis: Spontaneous renal fornix rupture (urinoma) secondary to pregnancy-related ureteral obstruction

Biochemical thyrotoxicosis (suppressed TSH with elevated free T4/T3) with negative thyroid antibodies and markedly elevated thyroglobulin

PMC8853404.diagnosis: Bilateral tibial and common peroneal mononeuropathies (COVID-19-associated mononeuritis multiplex)
rationale_summary: New focal lower-limb weakness with marked left foot drop and absent knee/ankle reflexes, with otherwise normal neurologic exam, localizes to peripheral nerves

NCS/EMG show bilateral tibial and common peroneal involvement with absent/abnormal sensory responses, slowed conduction, absent F waves/H reflexes, and denervation in peroneal-innervated muscles; upper extremities normal

Pattern is multiple acute mononeuropathies at noncompressible sites rather than radiculopathy or Guillain-Barré syndrome, occurring after severe COVID-19 treated with immunomodulation (dexamethasone, tocilizumab)

PMC8258634.diagnosis: Bilateral acute angle-closure glaucoma from medication-induced ciliochoroidal effusion (Simbrinza/brinzolamide-associated)

Ultrasound biomicroscopy shows 360° choroidal effusions with iridocorneal apposition, consistent with forward displacement of the lens-iris diaphragm

PMC10442471.diagnosis: Adult-onset Still's disease (AOSD) presenting with serositis
rationale_summary: Persistent quotidian high fevers (39-40 °C) with negative bacterial/mycobacterial cultures and no malignancy on cytology/endoscopy/biopsies
Marked systemic inflammation: neutrophilic leukocytosis, very high CRP/ESR, hyperferritinemia (3254 ng/mL), elevated LDH, transaminitis, hepatosplenomegaly
Exudative, culture-negative pleural effusions with nonspecific chronic pleuritis on VATS biopsy (serositis)
PET/CT showing diffuse inflammatory pattern (FDG-avid lymphadenopathy, spleen, bone marrow) with normal bone marrow biopsy, supporting inflammatory rather than malignant/infectious etiology

PMC2323011.diagnosis: Hypertrophic osteoarthropathy (paraneoplastic, likely from an occult lung malignancy)
rationale_summary: Progressive dyspnea with large unilateral pleural effusion and weight loss/anorexia suggests an underlying thoracic malignancy despite negative pleural cytology
Painful knees/thighs with markedly enlarged hands is consistent with clubbing/periostosis of hypertrophic osteoarthropathy
Bone scintigraphy "super scan" with diffuse skeletal uptake and elevated alkaline phosphatase can reflect diffuse periosteal new bone formation rather than prostate metastases
Normal PSA argues against diffuse metastatic prostate cancer as the cause of the bone scan findings

PMC6389478.diagnosis: Fourth branchial cleft anomaly (pyriform sinus fistula) causing recurrent cervical abscesses
rationale_summary: Recurrent left-sided cervical abscesses often triggered by upper respiratory infections
Multiple prior surgeries for presumed second branchial cleft cyst with persistent recurrences and no tract found on CT/MRI/explorations
Original pathology showed pseudoepithelium rather than true cyst lining, arguing against a true second branchial cleft cyst
Clinical pattern prompted suspicion of a third/fourth arch branchial cleft anomaly best evaluated with barium esophagogram for a pyriform sinus fistula

PMC7968274.diagnosis: Intravascular (intraluminal) meningioma of the right sigmoid sinus causing venous sinus obstruction and intracranial hypertension
rationale_summary: Bilateral papilledema with markedly elevated CSF opening pressure (500 mmH2O) and normal CSF composition indicates intracranial hypertension

CT venography demonstrates a well-defined homogeneous hypodense mass causing subtotal occlusion of the dominant right sigmoid sinus, explaining impaired venous outflow and papilledema

PMC10860510.diagnosis: Idiopathic myointimal hyperplasia of the mesenteric veins (IMHMV) causing ischemic colitis of the sigmoid/rectosigmoid
rationale_summary: Middle-aged man with weeks of constipation and later acute abdominal pain/fever with marked leukocytosis and high CRP
Colonoscopy showed marked sigmoid mucosal edema and luminal narrowing/stenosis with nonspecific biopsy (mild inflammation, no eosinophilia), arguing against IBD/eosinophilic enteritis

CT showed long-segment left-sided colonic wall thickening (splenic flexure to rectum) with a thin cord-like inferior mesenteric vein and ectatic mesenteric venous branches while the inferior mesenteric artery was patent and no thrombus/atherosclerosis-pattern consistent with nonthrombotic mesenteric venous occlusive disease such as IMHMV

PMC11101242.diagnosis: Delusional infestation (delusional parasitosis) involving the eyes
rationale_summary: 15-year fixed belief of persistent "foreign bodies" in superior fornices despite repeated normal ophthalmic exams with lid eversion/sweeping and normal orbital X-ray/MRI
Collected and presented self-extracted mixed debris (plant parts, fibers, hair, thread, metal filaments, insects) using elaborate rituals, consistent with "specimen" behavior
Ocular findings limited to mild surface irritation (trace injection, punctate staining) and floppy eyelid syndrome without any retained foreign body or lesion to explain symptoms

Biopsy showed squamous debris with acute and chronic inflammation, supporting an inclusion/epidermoid-type cyst

No alternative acute cause on CT (no PE, dissection, or pericardial effusion) and ECG without new ischemic changes

PMC6864292.diagnosis: Hypereosinophilic syndrome (HES) with eosinophil-associated thrombosis/skin necrosis
rationale_summary: Marked persistent hypereosinophilia (21.15×10^9/L; 57.8%) with bone marrow eosinophilia (27.5%)
Secondary causes of eosinophilia (allergic, parasitic, rheumatologic, immunologic) were unremarkable
Large progressive cutaneous necrosis with biopsy showing dermal necrosis/hemorrhage and eosinophil-rich perivascular inflammation

Evidence of systemic involvement and hypercoagulability (hemoptysis with bilateral pulmonary opacities/effusion, very high D-dimer 82.20 mg/L FEU, thrombocytopenia) consistent with HES-related thrombotic/vasculopathic complications

PMC9449604.diagnosis: Immune checkpoint inhibitor-related pancreatitis (pembrolizumab-induced, autoimmune pancreatitis-like)
rationale_summary: History of pembrolizumab exposure with onset 3 months after discontinuation

Imaging shows pancreatic head enlargement with delayed enhancement and diffusion restriction plus multiple main pancreatic duct stenoses and intrapancreatic bile duct stenosis, consistent with inflammatory/autoimmune-like pancreatitis rather than a focal malignancy
No abdominal symptoms and normal amylase/lipase/CRP despite marked imaging abnormalities, fitting reported ICI-related pancreatic injury
IgG4 is normal and cytology from pancreatic juice/bile/brushings is negative, making IgG4-related AIP and pancreatic/biliary malignancy less likely in this context

PMC1971059.diagnosis: Primary intramuscular carcinoid tumor (well-differentiated neuroendocrine tumor) of the thigh
rationale_summary: Slowly enlarging firm intramuscular thigh mass without other lesions on CT, MIBG, or octreotide scan supports a primary soft-tissue tumor
Histology shows nested/packet ("zellballen"-like) polygonal cells with fine fibrovascular stroma consistent with neuroendocrine neoplasm

Immunoprofile: strong synaptophysin and chromogranin positivity with cytokeratin positivity supports carcinoid/neuroendocrine tumor; S100 negative argues against paraganglioma; desmin negative argues against myogenic tumor
Electron microscopy shows abundant neurosecretory granules confirming neuroendocrine differentiation
Elevated 24-hour urinary 5-HIAA indicates serotonin-producing carcinoid activity despite lack of systemic symptoms

PMC11658845.diagnosis: Neonatal severe primary hyperparathyroidism (homozygous/inactivating CASR mutation)
rationale_summary: Severe neonatal hypercalcemia (total 14.3 mg/dL; ionized 2.32 mmol/L) with extremely elevated PTH (2116 pg/mL) indicates PTH-dependent hypercalcemia
Skeletal demineralization/osteopenia with metaphyseal lucencies and healing fractures, craniotabes, and bell-shaped chest are consistent with hyperparathyroid bone disease in utero
25-hydroxyvitamin D is normal, supporting a primary PTH-driven process rather than vitamin D excess

PMC4356045.diagnosis: Unilateral giant bullous emphysema (vanishing lung syndrome) of the left lung
rationale_summary: CT showed several bullae occupying almost the entire left hemithorax with increased lucency on chest radiograph
Young never-smoker with no biomass/dust exposure and normal α1-antitrypsin, supporting a non-smoking-related giant bullous process

Physiology suggests marked air trapping/hyperinflation: very high residual volume (156% predicted) and TLC discrepancy (low by nitrogen washout but near-normal by plethysmography) consistent with large bullae
Symptoms limited to exertional dyspnea with reduced breath sounds and dullness over the left upper chest, fitting a large unilateral bullous lesion compressing lung

PMC10509802.diagnosis: Spontaneous hemorrhage into a cervical plexiform neurofibroma (NF1) causing rapidly expanding cervicofacial hematoma
rationale_summary: Known NF1 (prior tongue neurofibroma, multiple café-au-lait spots, congenital lateral neck mass)
CT angiography shows a large ill-defined infiltrative suprahyoid neck lesion encasing major vessels, consistent with plexiform neurofibroma
After minor trauma, rapid progression over hours of unilateral temporoparietal/periorbital swelling with tense skin and punctate hemorrhagic spots suggests acute bleeding/hematoma expansion
No intracranial injury or infection on imaging; vascular rupture/aneurysm excluded on CTA

PMC9911254.diagnosis: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS) syndrome due to piperacillin-tazobactam (± amikacin)
rationale_summary: Delayed onset after ~18-19 days of antibiotic exposure with high fever and diffuse pruritic maculopapular rash with facial edema
Marked eosinophilia (1.8×10^9/L) with cytopenias (ANC 0.9×10^9/L, platelets 92×10^9/L)
Significant hepatitis (ALT 686 IU/L, AST 178 IU/L) indicating systemic organ involvement
Infectious evaluation largely negative (blood cultures negative; respiratory viral PCR negative); EBV/CMV PCR positivity can occur with DRESS-associated viral reactivation

PMC11440268.diagnosis: Metastatic parathyroid carcinoma (MIBI-avid pulmonary metastases causing severe recurrent hyperparathyroidism)
rationale_summary: Long-term hemodialysis patient with prior parathyroidectomy now has markedly elevated PTH (1891 pg/mL) with borderline hypercalcemia
Chest CT shows multiple well-defined bilateral pulmonary nodules
99mTc-MIBI demonstrates dominant uptake in the lung lesions, supporting ectopic hyperfunctioning parathyroid tissue within the nodules
Neck ultrasound/CT do not localize parathyroid tissue, making a cervical source less likely and favoring metastatic/ectopic parathyroid tissue

PMC1079925.diagnosis: Postoperative inflammatory uptake on somatostatin receptor scintigraphy (false-positive mediastinal activity after thymic carcinoid resection)
rationale_summary: In-111 pentetreotide showed focal mediastinal uptake 1 year after extensive sternotomy and mediastinal surgery (SVC graft, en bloc resection).
Follow-up CT showed no mediastinal recurrence, only poststernotomy changes/pleural thickening and atelectasis, arguing against a mass lesion.
Prior I-123 MIBG scan was negative for residual/recurrent disease, supporting that the octreotide focus could reflect inflammation rather than tumor.

PMC6120420.diagnosis: Pembrolizumab-associated acute interstitial nephritis (immune checkpoint inhibitor-related AKI)
rationale_summary: Metastatic lung adenocarcinoma treated with pembrolizumab, a known cause of immune-mediated nephritis
Severe AKI with creatinine rising from 1.0-1.2 to 7.2 mg/dL without contrast exposure, NSAID/herbal use, or new nephrotoxins
Urinalysis relatively bland (minimal proteinuria, few cells) and renal ultrasound without hydronephrosis, supporting intrinsic renal injury rather than obstruction
Fractional excretion of urea 46% argues against purely prerenal azotemia despite hypotension

PMC6207959.diagnosis: Gemcitabine-associated pyoderma gangrenosum with concurrent calcium pyrophosphate deposition (pseudogout)
rationale_summary: Painful rapidly developing necrotic calf ulcer with surrounding erythema, negative bacterial/fungal cultures, and biopsy showing ulceration/tissue necrosis with acute inflammation but no infection or malignancy
Temporal relationship to gemcitabine (occurred 2 weeks after final infusion; prior cycle associated with inflammatory ankle episode) supports a drug-associated neutrophilic dermatosis
No clinical evidence of peripheral vascular insufficiency and Doppler negative for DVT, making ischemic/venous ulcer less likely
Concurrent warm, tender knee effusion with synovial fluid calcium pyrophosphate crystals and no microorganisms indicates pseudogout rather than septic arthritis

PMC6454123.diagnosis: Kawasaki disease (incomplete/atypical) with erythema multiforme-like targetoid eruption and acral bullae
rationale_summary: 6-year-old with ≥4 days of high fever plus bilateral nonexudative conjunctival injection and strawberry tongue mucositis
Extremity involvement with pedal edema; elevated ESR (95 mm/hr) supports systemic inflammation

Generalized targetoid macules/patches without desquamation and biopsy showing subtle interface change/intraepidermal cleft can mimic erythema multiforme/SJS, but infectious workup (Mycoplasma, HSV, respiratory viruses) was negative and mucosal findings fit Kawasaki
Echocardiogram without coronary abnormalities does not exclude early/incomplete Kawasaki disease

PMC8906160.diagnosis: Emphysematous cystitis with emphysematous bladder diverticulitis and bladder perforation
rationale_summary: Urinary retention, haematuria, positive nitrites/WBCs and markedly elevated CRP suggest severe UTI in setting of long-term catheter/BPH
New hyperglycaemia/diabetes (glucose 14 mmol/L, HbA1c 7.8%) is a key risk factor for gas-forming urinary infections
Ultrasound showed gas around the bladder; CT showed intramural gas and gas-fluid level within a bladder diverticulum with thick-walled bladder
CT also showed extraluminal free gas in pelvis/right paracolic gutter, consistent with perforation/extra-vesical extension

PMC10941813.diagnosis: Giant prostatic leiomyoma (benign smooth muscle tumor of the prostate/bladder neck)
rationale_summary: Young man with years of obstructive lower urinary tract symptoms progressing to urinary retention
Imaging shows a very large, well-marginated predominantly cystic pelvic mass inferior to the bladder, appearing to communicate with the bladder neck
Needle biopsy and subsequent TURP specimens repeatedly show bland smooth muscle cells with no atypia/necrosis and very low mitotic activity, consistent with leiomyoma
No lymphadenopathy or distant metastases and PSA remains low, supporting a benign prostatic origin

PMC8626963.diagnosis: Hemolytic disease of the newborn due to maternal anti-D alloimmunization (RhD incompatibility) complicated by neonatal cholestasis and hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia
rationale_summary: Mother is RhD negative and received anti-D prophylaxis; infant has severe early anemia and jaundice
Strongly positive DAT with hemolysis markers (very high LDH, low haptoglobin, reticulocytosis) and leukoerythroblastosis
Hepatosplenomegaly and mixed hyperbilirubinemia with predominant conjugated fraction consistent with cholestasis in severe hemolysis
Persistent hypoglycemia with inappropriately present insulin and elevated C-peptide indicates hyperinsulinism requiring diazoxide/glucagon

PMC9463427.diagnosis: Spindle epithelial tumor with thymus-like differentiation (SETTLE) presenting as a right axillary soft-tissue mass synchronous with papillary thyroid carcinoma
rationale_summary: Right axillary mass is a well-defined hypoechoic soft-tissue tumor without surrounding lymphadenopathy and with poor enhancement on CT, arguing against typical nodal metastasis.

Axillary FNA showed epithelial-like cells in a mesenchymal background (biphasic epithelial/mesenchymal pattern), supporting a thymic/thymus-like spindle epithelial tumor rather than metastatic papillary thyroid carcinoma.
TTF-1 was negative in the axillary lesion, making thyroid-origin metastasis less likely despite elevated thyroglobulin and confirmed papillary thyroid carcinoma in the thyroid/neck nodes.
Breast ultrasound was normal, reducing likelihood of occult breast primary for an axillary metastasis.

PMC7374193.diagnosis: Bilateral optic nerve head hemangioblastomas (retinal capillary hemangioblastomas) causing chronic disc swelling and progressive vision loss
rationale_summary: 15-year history of bilateral optic nerve head swelling with suspected optic disc masses and progressive visual loss

Genetic testing negative for von Hippel-Lindau and other syndromic causes, supporting sporadic optic nerve head hemangioblastomas

PMC8544693.diagnosis: Multisystem inflammatory syndrome in adults (MIS-A) following SARS-CoV-2 infection
rationale_summary: Acute febrile illness occurring shortly after confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection
Shock with hypotension and tachycardia plus markedly elevated inflammatory markers and leukocytosis
Prominent gastrointestinal symptoms (vomiting, diarrhea) and diffuse rash with desquamation
Severe cardiac involvement (global LV hypokinesia, EF <30%, four-chamber dilation, valvular regurgitation, pericardial effusion) with pulmonary edema during fluid resuscitation
No respiratory symptoms and normal oxygenation, supporting a post-infectious hyperinflammatory syndrome rather than primary COVID-19 pneumonia

PMC3655491.diagnosis: Recurrent Guillain-Barré syndrome (acute motor axonal neuropathy variant)
rationale_summary: Prior severe Guillain-Barré syndrome with current acute relapse after recent upper respiratory infection
Acute limb pain, weakness, and bulbar symptoms with reduced ankle reflexes and preserved sensation
MRI shows anterior (motor) nerve-root enhancement
CSF with elevated protein (albuminocytologic dissociation pattern) despite mild lymphocytic pleocytosis after IVIG
EMG/NCS: predominantly motor polyneuropathy with mild axonal>demyelinating changes, intact sensory responses, no conduction block/temporal dispersion, consistent with AMAN-type GBS

PMC8462061.diagnosis: Insulinoma (pancreatic insulin-secreting neuroendocrine tumor) coexisting with early-stage type 1 diabetes
rationale_summary: Recurrent fasting hypoglycemia with inappropriately elevated insulin (158.35 pmol/L) and C-peptide (1.09 nmol/L) plus suppressed beta-hydroxybutyrate indicates endogenous hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia
Sulfonylurea screen was negative, supporting a non-medication-induced source of hyperinsulinism
CGM showed prolonged fasting hypoglycemia despite evolving postprandial hyperglycemia and rising A1c, suggesting an additional hypoglycemia etiology beyond early type 1 diabetes
Abdominal ultrasound was unremarkable, which does not exclude insulinoma and prompted concern for another cause

CT/MRI show heterogeneous vastus medialis lesion with edema and areas of muscle breakdown, a typical presentation that can mimic sarcoma but fits diabetic muscle infarction

PMC11066590.diagnosis: Lorlatinib-associated bilateral optic neuritis (toxic optic neuropathy) with reversible splenial lesion syndrome
rationale_summary: Subacute, painless, progressive bilateral severe vision loss to no light perception shortly after starting lorlatinib (2 months)
Exam shows large minimally reactive pupils with relative afferent pupillary defect and trace optic disc edema
Orbital MRI shows bilateral optic nerve T2 hyperintensity and postcontrast enhancement extending to the chiasm, consistent with optic neuritis/optic neuropathy
Brain MRI shows isolated splenial corpus callosum restricted diffusion/FLAIR hyperintensity, a pattern seen with drug-related reversible splenial lesions

PMC8286001.diagnosis: Central diabetes insipidus due to pituitary stalk metastasis (metastatic disease with brain metastases)
rationale_summary: Polyuria-polydipsia with low urine osmolality (138 mOsm/kg) despite normal plasma osmolality (292 mOsm/kg) and normal glucose/renal function supports diabetes insipidus
Symptomatic improvement with desmopressin indicates central (vasopressin-deficient) diabetes insipidus
MRI shows two intensely enhancing parietal lesions suggestive of metastases plus an enhancing supradiaphragmatic pituitary stalk nodule, consistent with metastatic involvement causing central DI

Marked folate deficiency (low serum and red cell folate) with macrocytosis (MCV 122.8) and normal vitamin B12
Risk factors and history consistent with nutritional deficiency: very poor diet plus smoking (20/day) and regular alcohol intake

PMC6334378.diagnosis: Acute necrotizing enterocolitis with intestinal ischemia leading to septic shock and hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy
rationale_summary: Abdominal CT shows pneumatosis intestinalis and portal venous gas with poor small-bowel perfusion, consistent with necrotizing enterocolitis/ischemic bowel
Profound shock and metabolic acidosis (BP 58/47, pH 6.76, HCO3 3.2) with leukocytosis support septic shock from bowel necrosis
Distended, guarded abdomen with absent bowel sounds and ileus on radiograph support severe intra-abdominal pathology
Brain CT shows diffuse cerebral swelling consistent with hypoxic-ischemic injury secondary to shock; no intracranial hemorrhage or fractures to suggest trauma

PMC9650838.diagnosis: Recurrent bacterial meningitis due to cerebrospinal fluid leak from an occult skull base defect (CSF rhinorrhea/otorrhea)
rationale_summary: History of repeated hospitalizations over 10 years for similar meningitic episodes
Current presentation with meningeal signs and markedly abnormal CSF (very high WBC with neutrophil predominance, high protein, low glucose) with negative initial cultures/mNGS
Clinical and CSF improvement on antibiotics followed by relapse with very high opening pressure (>300 mm H2O) and CSF pleocytosis
Relapse CSF culture grew hemolytic streptococcus, consistent with recurrent bacterial meningitis from a communication with upper airway

MRI shows signs of raised intracranial pressure (partially empty sella, distended optic nerve sheaths, papilla flattening, narrowed ventricles), a known risk factor for spontaneous skull base CSF leaks leading to recurrent meningitis

PMC4960400.diagnosis: Progressive pseudorheumatoid dysplasia (spondyloepiphyseal dysplasia tarda with progressive arthropathy)
rationale_summary: Longstanding childhood-onset progressive joint pain/swelling with early severe degenerative disease requiring bilateral total hip replacement at age 19
Seronegative/inflammatory markers normal (normal ESR/CRP, RF, anti-CCP, ANA) despite extensive joint involvement, arguing against RA/JIA

PMC4074175.diagnosis: Pheochromocytoma (catecholamine-secreting tumor) presenting as cyclic anxiety/panic symptoms
rationale_summary: Three-year history of severe anxiety with prominent autonomic symptoms (palpitations, chest "heat," occasional numbness)
Marked diurnal/cyclic pattern with worst symptoms at dawn and relief each evening, suggesting episodic physiologic surges rather than primary anxiety disorder
No meaningful response to multiple adequate SSRI/SNRI/antidepressant trials, arguing against primary OCD/anxiety as sole cause
History of hypertension, compatible with catecholamine excess presenting with anxiety-like episodes

PMC7901359.diagnosis: Cerebral salt wasting (CSW) after traumatic brain injury
rationale_summary: Severe hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 118, serum osmolality 226) with inappropriately concentrated urine (Uosm 518) and high urine sodium (83)
Clinical hypovolemia/orthostasis: low BP, dry mucous membranes, reduced skin turgor, marked orthostatic hypotension with tachycardia and dizziness
Low serum uric acid (2.6) consistent with renal salt wasting in CNS-related hyponatremia
Recent head trauma with skull fracture and new intracranial hygroma provides CNS trigger for CSW

Clinical picture atypical for acute pulmonary embolism: 2-week progressive chest pain, no dyspnoea/fever, normal oxygenation

PMC7904273.diagnosis: Celiac disease (gluten-sensitive enteropathy) with secondary bone marrow suppression
rationale_summary: 6 months of diarrhea and abdominal pain with hypoalbuminemia and elevated ESR/CRP
Duodenal mucosal congestion/edema on EGD with otherwise normal ileum/colon, fitting proximal small-bowel pathology
Cytopenias with very low reticulocyte count and hypocellular marrow without malignancy or nutrient deficiencies, suggesting systemic effect rather than iron/B12/folate deficiency
tTG IgA and duodenal biopsy were specifically pursued and are pending, consistent with suspected celiac disease

PMC11234180.diagnosis: Pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1B (PTH resistance causing hypocalcemia with basal ganglia calcifications)
rationale_summary: Severe hypocalcemia (Ca 5.5 mg/dL) with hyperphosphatemia (7.6 mg/dL) and markedly elevated intact PTH (343.1 pg/mL) indicates PTH resistance rather than deficiency
No features of Albright's hereditary osteodystrophy, supporting PHP type 1B over type 1A
Complications consistent with chronic hypocalcemia: basal ganglia/frontal cortex calcifications, cataracts, and QT prolongation
Normal renal function and normal thyroid function make secondary causes less likely

PMC6906565.diagnosis: Thymic carcinoma, micronodular with lymphoid stroma (micronodular thymic carcinoma with lymphoid hyperplasia)
rationale_summary: Anterior mediastinal thymic-region mass with adjacent residual thymic tissue and Masaoka IIb features
Histology shows multiple epithelial micronodules separated by abundant lymphoid stroma with lymphoid follicles, characteristic of micronodular thymic tumors with lymphoid stroma

PMC11264972.diagnosis: Azathioprine hypersensitivity syndrome (drug reaction with shock and acute pustular rash, with myocarditis-like cardiac injury)
rationale_summary: Severe febrile systemic illness with distributive shock and acute renal failure began shortly after starting azathioprine, with no infectious source on cultures/imaging
Developed acute erythematous pustular rash during the course, consistent with a severe drug reaction
Rechallenge with a single azathioprine dose caused rapid recurrence of vasodilatory shock within 12 hours, strongly implicating azathioprine
Associated transient ST elevations and markedly elevated troponin with normal coronary angiography supports inflammatory/drug-related myocardial injury rather than ACS

PMC9296717.diagnosis: Postoperative myofascial pain syndrome due to an internal oblique abdominal muscle trigger point
rationale_summary: Severe posture- and movement-provoked right epigastric pain with positive Carnett's sign suggests abdominal wall origin
Focal hypersensitive tender spot with taut band and palpation-induced referred pain meets myofascial trigger point criteria
Temporary relief after local anesthetic (mepivacaine) injection into the tender spot supports trigger point pain

Ultrasound shows a small hyperechoic focus with surrounding hypoechoic change within the internal oblique muscle, with intact fibers and location not consistent with port-site disruption or a vertical nerve course

PMC1808459.diagnosis: Low-grade fibromatosis-like spindle cell carcinoma of the breast (metaplastic carcinoma)
rationale_summary: Infiltrative breast spindle cell tumor with prominent keloid-like sclerosis and mild atypia/rare mitoses
Diffuse epithelial marker expression (pan-cytokeratin, CK7, high-molecular-weight CK, EMA) with coexpression of vimentin supports metaplastic/spindle cell carcinoma rather than sarcoma
Focal p63 and smooth muscle actin positivity supports myoepithelial/basal-type metaplastic carcinoma
Low proliferative index (Ki-67 <5%) and absence of DCIS/squamous differentiation fit fibromatosis-like variant
Negative CD34, S100, desmin, ER/PR, and HER2 helps exclude phyllodes/DFSP, neural or smooth muscle tumors, and typical hormone receptor-positive carcinomas

PMC7424302.diagnosis: Acute septal branch coronary artery injury/occlusion from left bundle branch pacing lead penetration causing transient anterolateral STEMI
rationale_summary: Symptoms began immediately during deep septal lead advancement with impedance rise then sudden fall, suggesting perforation/vascular entry
Acute chest pain, diaphoresis, hypotension with ST elevation in I, aVL, V1-V3 and reciprocal inferior depression consistent with acute LAD/septal territory ischemia
Urgent angiography showed a large septal branch at the initial lead site with preserved LAD TIMI 3 flow, implicating septal branch compromise rather than epicardial LAD occlusion
ST changes and symptoms resolved within 10 minutes after lead withdrawal/repositioning, supporting transient mechanical coronary injury/spasm/occlusion from the lead

PMC6169071.diagnosis: Lead poisoning (plumbism) causing hemolytic anemia with schistocytosis and cytopenias
rationale_summary: Severe normocytic anemia with hemolysis: schistocytes (10%), very high LDH, undetectable haptoglobin, Coombs-negative
Inappropriately low reticulocyte production index (0.061) with neutropenia and later thrombocytopenia suggests marrow toxicity rather than primary TTP/HUS
Abdominal pain, arthromyalgia, paresthesias, tinnitus, and headaches are compatible with lead toxicity
Renal dysfunction with azotemia that improved with hydration fits toxic nephropathy/AKI in this context

PMC10191487.diagnosis: Autosomal recessive polycystic kidney disease with congenital hepatic fibrosis (hepatoportal sclerosis/portal hypertension)
rationale_summary: Infant with persistent hepatosplenomegaly and heterogeneous hyperechoic liver parenchyma on ultrasound, suggesting chronic hepatic/portal process rather than acute infection or malignancy
Mildly elevated hepatic arterial resistive index (0.8) supports altered hepatic/portal hemodynamics consistent with portal hypertension
Intermittent pancytopenia with persistent mild thrombocytopenia fits hypersplenism from portal hypertension

PMC11256461.diagnosis: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS) due to sulfasalazine
rationale_summary: Symptoms began after ~3 weeks of sulfasalazine and progressed despite stopping: fever, diffuse blanching pruritic macular rash, tender lymphadenopathy, facial/pharyngeal edema
Systemic involvement: hepatitis (ALT 224, AST 78, INR 1.4, hypoalbuminemia), serositis/effusions (pericardial effusion, pleural effusion, ascites), hepatosplenomegaly, hypoxia
Hematologic findings consistent with DRESS: leukocytosis with eosinophilia rising to 11%, elevated ferritin
Infectious and other causes were investigated/considered and key tests were negative; case states he met all seven RegiSCAR criteria for DRESS
Imaging showed widespread lymphadenopathy with FDG-avid nodes, which can occur in DRESS and mimics lymphoma

PMC10534457.diagnosis: Hereditary hemochromatosis (iron overload) with cirrhosis and hemochromatosis arthropathy (CPPD/pseudogout)
rationale_summary: Middle-aged man with hyperpigmentation, new type 2 diabetes, low libido and gynecomastia suggesting hypogonadism, and hepatosplenomegaly with cholestatic liver tests and advanced fibrosis/cirrhosis on FibroScan/ultrasound
Symmetric painful MCP (2nd-3rd) arthropathy with osteophytes/step-up lesions and ultrasound evidence of calcium pyrophosphate deposition is characteristic of hemochromatosis arthropathy

PMC7441664.diagnosis: Hirayama disease (monomelic amyotrophy of the upper limb)
rationale_summary: Young man with insidious, slowly progressive unilateral distal upper-limb weakness and hand atrophy (first dorsal interosseous) with brachioradialis sparing
Lower motor neuron signs (atrophy, reduced tone, fasciculations/fibrillations on EMG) with no sensory, autonomic, bowel/bladder, or gait involvement
Course progressed for a few years then became static, typical of Hirayama disease
Electrodiagnostics show motor axonal involvement affecting C7-T1-innervated muscles; cervical imaging and labs exclude alternative structural/inflammatory causes

PMC9950348.diagnosis: Spontaneous tumor lysis syndrome due to aggressive metastatic cecal adenocarcinoma (solid tumor TLS)
rationale_summary: Known large tumor burden on CT (cecal mass with omental infiltration and hepatic lesions)

Meets Cairo-Bishop laboratory TLS pattern: marked hyperuricemia (18.1 mg/dL), hyperkalemia (5.6 mEq/L), hyperphosphatemia (stated), and acute kidney injury (creatinine 5.6 mg/dL) without prior therapy
Very high serum LDH (9900 U/L) supports rapid tumor turnover/lysis; clinical deterioration with dyspnea/effusions and ascites in setting of advanced malignancy

PMC8817719.diagnosis: Secondary syphilis (papular syphilid) with erythema nodosum
rationale_summary: 3-week generalized pruritic maculopapular rash on limbs/trunk with palm sparing and no fever/lymphadenopathy

No response to topical corticosteroids argues against inflammatory dermatoses treated by steroids and supports an infectious etiology like syphilis

PMC5184841.diagnosis: Early repolarization syndrome (J-wave syndrome) causing idiopathic ventricular fibrillation
rationale_summary: Syncope with documented polymorphic VT/VF initiated by frequent monomorphic PVCs in a structurally normal heart (normal echo and cardiac MRI)
12-lead ECG showed widespread J waves with normal QT interval, supporting a J-wave/early repolarization phenotype rather than long-QT
Arrhythmia burden and J-wave amplitude decreased with beta-blockade (labetalol) and electrolyte repletion, consistent with J-wave-associated VF modulation
No metabolic, toxicologic, or ischemic trigger identified; stress test did not provoke arrhythmias

PMC3920376.diagnosis: In-flight expansion of a large left apical pulmonary bulla causing transient compressive symptoms (bullous lung disease with altitude-related barotrauma)
rationale_summary: Recurrent, predictable episodes occur only at cruising altitude and resolve on descent, consistent with pressure-related gas expansion
Symptoms include sudden severe left pleuritic chest pain radiating to the back on every flight, fitting pleural stretch/barotrauma from an apical bulla
CT pulmonary angiography shows a solitary large left apical bulla (10×8×8 cm), providing an anatomic substrate for altitude-related expansion/compression
Normal exam, oxygen saturation, head MRI, and largely normal PFTs argue against chronic cardiopulmonary or primary neurologic disease as the cause

PMC10682226.diagnosis: Cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (well-differentiated) of the face/nose
rationale_summary: Progressive destructive facial plaques/ulceration over 3 years with actinic cheilitis on prior biopsy

Repeated biopsies showed pseudoepitheliomatous hyperplasia and granulomatous inflammation with negative stains/cultures/PCR for infections and no sustained response to antimicrobials/antimonials/anti-TB/steroids
Definitive nasal skin biopsy showed epidermal hyperplasia with atypia, increased mitoses, invasive squamous nests and keratin pearls consistent with well-differentiated SCC
Staging showed no metastases

PMC3113998.diagnosis: Acute hypercapnic respiratory failure causing cerebral edema and increased intracranial pressure (hypercapnic encephalopathy)
rationale_summary: ABG on controlled ventilation shows severe hypercapnia with acidemia (pCO2 85 mmHg, pH 7.22)
Head CT demonstrates diffuse cerebral swelling (effaced sulci, small ventricles, narrowed basilar cisterns) consistent with increased intracranial pressure
Neurologic signs of raised ICP/herniation physiology (unresponsiveness, unilateral fixed dilated pupil, blurred disc margins)
Presentation includes progressive shortness of breath in a patient with obesity/OSA/asthma, supporting ventilatory failure as the driver of hypercapnia

PMC7753090.diagnosis: Extraskeletal (soft-tissue) chondrosarcoma, low grade (atypical cartilaginous tumor) of the thumb/thenar region
rationale_summary: Slowly enlarging circumferential thumb/thenar soft-tissue mass over 5 years
Imaging shows a large multilobulated soft-tissue lesion encasing the flexor pollicis longus tendon with no bony erosion/scalloping
Biopsy demonstrates an atypical cartilaginous lesion with mild-to-moderate atypia, abutting but not infiltrating bone, and no high-grade malignant features

PMC7423524.diagnosis: Small intestinal lymphoma (primary intestinal lymphoma) mimicking SLE mesenteric vasculitis
rationale_summary: Persistent relapsing abdominal pain and incomplete intestinal obstruction despite high-dose steroids and cyclophosphamide, arguing against active SLE vasculitis as sole cause
Imaging and endoscopy show focal small-bowel ulcer with luminal stenosis plus segmental dilatation and multiple air-fluid levels, suggesting an obstructing structural lesion

Infection and malignancy were initially excluded by workup (normal inflammatory markers, negative blood cultures, PET-CT without abnormalities), yet symptoms persisted, supporting an occult intestinal neoplasm such as lymphoma

Young woman with subacute recurrent focal neurologic deficits and multifocal acute-subacute left-hemispheric infarcts with hemorrhagic transformation
CT angiography and catheter angiography show focal moderate-to-severe distal left M1 stenosis
Partial improvement in M1 caliber after intra-arterial verapamil supports vasoconstriction rather than fixed vasculitis/atherosclerosis

PMC8751507.diagnosis: Primary bone lymphoma (B-cell lymphoma of bone) mimicking plasmacytoma
rationale_summary: Recurrent multifocal lytic bone disease (T4 collapse with epidural mass plus pelvic and rib lesions) with persistently negative myeloma workup (no marrow plasmacytosis/clonality, no paraprotein/light-chain excess).

Second-opinion pathology favored malignant lymphoproliferative disorder with B-cell predominance; immunophenotype includes CD20 positivity (B-cell marker) along with CD3-positive background T-cells and high MIB-1.
Both L2 and T4 lesions share identical histology/immunophenotype, supporting a single lymphoid neoplasm rather than isolated plasmacytoma progressing to myeloma.
